user_comment: These are perfect! They fit true to size. I’m 5’7 160 lbs and got the large. The material is light and they flow so nicely! Love them!

Great boot! I was looking for these for a while and the only decent quality tall ugg style boots I could find were the $200 uggs but I wanted these to wear mostly to the dog park so I didn't want to spend $200 on them. These are perfect though, great alternative. They're about 12 inches tall including the base, I'm 5'6" and they're a little higher than mid calf. The inside is snug with socks but like most fur lined footwear this will probably loosen up after a few wears.

I’m 5’8” 140 lbs. these pants are high waisted, and rest a little above my belly button. When I wear it like that, the hem rests above my ankles, which is a little short. I can pull the waistband down so that the top of the hits the top of my foot, but then I have a weird baggy crotch area. These pants would probably work great for someone under 5’7”. The feel of the material is like swishy athletic thin material while still being very opaque. I’m pretty sure it’s synthetic, but it still feels breathable and would probably be comfortable in a hot climate. I got these to be comfortable lounge pants, potential dress up pants for a work function, but also work for if I’m stretching or doing light exercise. I’m probably still gonna wear these pants even though they’re a little short.

These are great stretchy, business casual office pants. High waisted. They are a thicker and nicer material, but almost feel like yoga pants. Amazon recommended me a medium and the thick waistband cut into my stomach more than I wanted. Reordered a large and am very pleased. 5'9" ~167lbs. Hugs your curves in the right places. I can squat, high kick, move around however I need in these. No pockets. Emphasis on business casual, not formal dress pants, you could definitely dress them up or down.

I like the pants. They fit like a glove (XL, 5’5, 195 lbs), and are comfortable. There are no pockets which make the look I am going for flow seamlessly. There is no zipper and has an elastic waistband. The thickness is appropriate for me, not too thick but not super thin. I will wash the pants this evening for wear tomorrow so will come back and let you know if they are wrinkle resistant. The other problem is they are not as long as they appear in the picture (which is what I wanted). The pants had a smudge on them that I did not do. I’m so disappointed that the vendors take back items only to resell them without doing a quality check. Or maybe they don’t care and just send out dirty items. Overall the functionality of the pant fits the need I have for them. The cost point and style is why I bought this item.

As other reviews previously stated, yes the calf size is tight upon arrival. Just like any other riding boot you have to wear them and break them in. I’ve been using my boots 4 weeks now prior to writing this review. The boots slip on without any hesitation, zipper goes up fine and no excessive compression near the calves. I’ve rinsed my boots every time with the hose. No water has leaked in. Overall great boot. I am standing at 5’5, 155 pounds.

I love these pants! I ordered them because I needed pants for hiking in the fall and cooler weather. A friend of mine owns a pair similar to this that she has let me borrow, and I really liked them. When I ordered these, I wasn’t sure what to expect, but I’m so glad I took a chance. The size I ordered was medium in the 27.5 length. I currently weigh 132 pounds and I am 5’2”. These pants are a perfect fit for me. A small would’ve been too tight through the thighs and rear end, but I am bottom heavy and have to consider my shape. These pants wick water nicely, but don’t feel plastic or weird. They feel really good and have a fleece liner. They are cozy, warm, but not hot. They are not overly insulated, they are not too thick, they bend and flex well, and don’t look bulky. I think they are flattering. These pants do not have back pockets, but they have nice deep front pockets. I can easily put my phone, keys, etc, in these pockets without a problem. The waistband is wide and feels good. It’s also an elastic waistband with and adjustable string. I feel like the only reason anyone would not like these pants is if they are expecting them to be super warm and insulated. These would not be good for really cold, snowy weather. They would be a good layer for really cold, snowy weather, but on their own, they are not intended for that. If you need these to stay warm and cozy in crisp, cool weather, then these are exactly what you need. I can see myself wearing these to outdoor sporting events, hiking, or just in general when I’m going to be outside a lot during cooler weather. They are so comfortable and would look good with a T-shirt, sweatshirt, or whatever. I would not hesitate to recommend these, and the price is quite reasonable.

These G Gradual fleece lined pants are high-quality, super warm, and the fabric is a dream for dog owners, pet hair just slides right off. At 5'3" and 160 lbs, I tried the medium in the 27.5" length. The petite length was just right, but I do wish there was a touch more room overall; the fit is a tad snug. If you’re between sizes like me, you might find yourself balancing between a better fit in the waist or the perfect length. The pockets are thoughtful, including a small inner one for keys, which is handy for walking. The fleece lining offers cozy warmth without bulk, making these an excellent choice for chilly days. Just keep in mind: the side pockets can stick out a bit if the fit is snug, so consider sizing up or pairing with longer tops for the best look and comfort.

I was scared of ordering boots online but I was so happy with this purchase! I’m 5’4 for reference but I have long looking legs, so I was worried about the height of the boots, but they fit about absolutely perfectly! I can’t tell you yet how they will last but for now they lasted a whole day walking around in a busy city! Also tmi- but I have an ingrown toenail and I was scared it would hurt it and thankfully it didn’t! I usually can’t last in tighter looking shoes especially towards the toes but I had no problem in these! They definitely don’t feel expensive but for the price I got them for? 100% worth it!! I’ll definitely be ordering more this fall

Perfect fit for me! I am low waisted by 2” but want waist high pants, 198#, 5’5-1/5. Mid rise 18 petite does a perfect job ! Oh I forgot the bigggg thing! If I buy high rise, I have to fold over 2” and I sooo hate that. Next time I’ll try regular mid rise and see if I can get them a bit longer❤️

Finally a pair of jeans that don't create a muffin top or cut me in half at the waist! I've always loved Lee jeans and these fit my 72 year old body great. Thus grandma isn't ready for pull on jeans yet, lol. There is just enough stretch in the fabric to be comfortable but not saggy after wearing for a few hours. They sit right at my navel, which I like in my jeans. Plenty long enough too. And I really like the lighter color denim.

Beautiful boots that will serve you for many years to come; true to size and particularly if you have larger calves (5 7" 161 lbs but healthy calves), they are supremely roomy. I'm medium foot wide so initially the size 10 was slightly snug on first try however I can tell they will stretch out for my foot. Color is spot on; style is timeless and they are an incredible value for the money. A side note is whether the zipper (medium grade) will hold up however given the give and just right roomy calf room, it probably won't be an issue! At this price point, this is a super comfortable stylish win!

These pants rock, and here’s why: 1) Color is a true yellow that’s not too bright. 2) The fit is perfect! I’m 5’5” and 128lbs and bought the XS. (If you’re smaller than that, you might be able to get by if you move the button over) 3) Length is great and works well with heels and even flats. 3) Finallyyy some material that isn’t cheap and doesn’t pill! It hides panty lines and drapes beautifully. It also doesn’t look “polyestery” at all. 4) I wasn’t sure the silver on the waistband would work, but it’s subtle. To my surprise, I wore gold jewelry with it and didn’t think twice about the two different metals. I’ll definitely be ordering a few more pairs! ***To the supplier, to make these even more on trend, I’d recommend making the buttons and metal the same color as the pants. :)

Hey y’all! I read reviews about the short length for these pants… but they now have a Long option (for an extra coat). I am 5’8” 130lbs. I purchased the Small Long, and it fits me well. There is a half inch hem if you need to let it out. The pants are polyester with a flexible waist, so you could size down if you wanted them tighter. There are pockets in the front and in the back! After high watering in smallsmost of my life, I am always grateful to find pants that fit both my width and my length!

I ordered these jeans for my mom, who has lost 78 pounds, and they fit perfect. She loves the way they fit, the quality, the style, and how comfortable they are. Before this she never wore any jeans at all. She is short, maybe 5'3, so they are a little longer on her but we are just going to get them hemmed. We would definitely order again.

Good material, not that stretchy, not that dick. But they are comfortable. They’re very flattering to the curves. Especially the posterior curves. So if you’ve got a little bit in the trunk, and you’ve got a nice round shape to it, and you’ve got some round curvy hips, these are the jeans for you. But I would caution you to check their sizes, and maybe be considering buying a size down as these ran a little bit big. If you are short, you’ll need to hem the bottom of these jeans as they’re a little bit long, but if you’re in the tall range like a 5’7 Ish, These jeans would fit perfect on you. The print is nice, but it’s not as pronounced as it looks in the photos. Overall, this is a good jean. If I could, I would get 4 1/2 stars.

I think I am more of a full figure- I have a butt and hips with a smaller waist. I bought size 18- my normal size these fit perfectly. The denim is more like 90s denim but with some stretch and they are high waisted with a boot cut/flare. They give Y2K vibes. If you are full figured and like high waist boot cut these are for you! I own a farm and really struggle to find a denim that I can work in and still be comfortable- these are perfect. Thicker denim offers more protection, these are comfy for riding or working. They also look nice enough to wear out. They are a great multi use jeans. These are made for taller women, I am 5’7” so not to tall but if I am not in my work boots they drag, I have a 31” inseam ( longer legs with a short torso) if you are taller with hips/ butt these are for you. I can see why shorter women do not like them, or women with small butts find them uncomfortable. If you have a pan fold and a butt these are great- order your true size, the stretch does loosen after a few hours of wearing so if you are in between sizes order down, if you are a smaller figure you will also probably want to order down a size. These are the best jeans I have found for my current size in a long time. My new favorite.

I’m a 5’2 1/2”, 145-150 lb woman with long legs for my height. Regardless, I’m still short. So, I wasn’t expecting a miraculous fit. Boy, was I surprised. Size M, 31” inseam is perfect on me. I’m heading out to Yellowstone in a few weeks and these COZY warm sweatpants are going to be a great addition to my southern California wardrobe. I’m even thinking about ordering another pair. Delivery was quick so there still is more time. The only negative is they are not a slimming fit. All that fleece has to go somewhere. Mind you, they don’t add a lot of extra width, but neither are not slimming. Boy, are they soft, tho. It’s not worth subtracting a star.BTW, this was my second pair ordered because the first was too large (size L). Did I return them? Nope, my husband has claimed them for himself.Did I mention they are cozy warm?

I'm a runner, do yoga, lift & gym rat. I have 6 pairs of HeyNuts leggings. These are a 10/10. I will NEVER buy the expensive L brand ever again. I'm 5'8 and 124 pounds, and I wear a small.

I really like this brand bc I’m 5’9 and the pants reach all the way to my ankles. They are super soft and feel like lulu lemon. However, with the green sage pair (I love the color btw) they have this weird inside pocket in the front that you can see.. it’s not a flat waist band and that’s what I wanted, but it does have nice tummy control. You can also see all panty lines, so you’ll have to wear thin underwear. I have to return them bc of the pocket and also bc my weight fluctuates between sizes 4 and 6 and I got size small. If your weight fluctuates, I recommend to size up. I’m gonna get the black in size medium, in hopes that black doesn’t have the weird front pocket in the waist band.

My dad got me one pair for Christmas and I turned around and bought 3 more colors. I have been on the hunt for leggings that make me feel good. These sit right, the front seam doesn’t bother me at all I feel like it is less of a problem than seamless leggings are for me in the front. They have a little slide to them so getting the right size does matter. If you are in-between sizes, go down. However, even with a little slide (truly just a little) they hug my body really well. My boyfriend’s first words without knowing I was trying something new was a compliment. Very flattering on my small butt lol and they are pretty high waisted so keep that in mind. The material is buttery, and reminds me of a pair of Lululemon leggings I have. All the colors I have - green, brown, maroon and black are squat proof but they are not sweat proof. I didn’t know this until today hahah but I am at the gym to sweat, so I don’t mind much. However, I know on a warmer day I probably would keep that in mind for the lighter colors. I am 130lbs, 5’4, wearing a size small 28 length. I would go 26 if you prefer it to hit at and show your ankle.

I ordered these last year (2024) and they were fantastic! Ordered a pair of beige ones Aug 2025, and now the fabric is SYNTHETIC! Gross! It is now a mixture of nylon, rayon, (80%), cotton and spandex. They took a good product and destroyed it. These are going straight back. Review from Aug 2024: These are 80% cotton and 20% flax. Really thin which is good for hot weather! It seems like they will hold up. I ordered the dark blue first then the khaki and white. With the khaki and white you need to wear lighter colored underwear but they are not super translucent. Very acceptable in my opinion (I hate see through stuff). I first bought XL but they were way too big. The L fits better... so would say they run big. I'm tall (5'9") and the dark blue ones fit perfect in length! (I rarely find pants long enough). However, the white and khaki ones are almost 1 inch shorter!! Such a disappointment! I will still keep them and just scoot them down lower on my hips, but that discovery was a bit of a disappointment. Before I ordered the white and khaki colors, I washed the blue ones on hot and threw them in the dryer (warm) to see if they would sitting shrink in length - they did not, so that is good. Seems like they must have pre-shrunk the fabric before the pants were sewn. (All manufacturers should do this in my opinion). Let's hope the white and khaki ones don't shrink. (Then they will definitely be too short.)

I bought an XL but could have sized down. Gave it 4 start because inseam is off, it’s more for tall people n im short( I’m 5’4). They are light and comfortable. I would order again but a smaller size n different color.
134 lb, 5”5 good fit. In M NOT 80% cotton and 20% linen as claimed. Comfortable.

I think they are good looking- I purchased the grey and there is a bit of brown tinge? Not a true grey but it doesn’t matter to me.. no damages upon arriving. I’m 5’8” usually wear sizes 8.5-9 and I got a 41. The sole looks durable but we shall see how long they last!

I have such strong mixed feelings about these pants. I'm 5'8", about 150 pounds, pear shaped, and all legs. I wear size 6 pants and I'm almost always a very solid medium. I work at an outdoor school where the temperatures are in the 20-40s(F) for months. I don't need to win any fashion awards, my main goal is warmth and comfort. I LOVE that these pants come in multiple lengths because that's incredibly hard to find for sweatpants and my legs are long and I need my ankles covered because it's cold. I took the advice of everyone and sized up and bought a large in 34in the first time (black pants in pictures). The waistband, even sitting on my natural waist, is still incredibly tight. You can see in the pictures where it's making my tummy squish out the top. I love the way the rest of the pant fits and the length. I decided to order a second pair, this time an XL in the same 34in length (grey pants in pictures). This waistband fits a lot more comfortably and I can wear it lower on my hips which I find more comfortable, but they are so baggy everywhere else. They are also at least 2 inches longer than the black pair labeled the same length, which I find weird. Another thing I'm not a fan of is the line across the top of the butt. I'm not sure its purpose because it's thick, unflattering, and I can feel it through the pants if I'm not wearing a base layer. Despite the flaws, they are thick and warm so I'm keeping both.
color_display: baby polar fleece, light heather grey

user_comment: Soft soft soft and multiple washes and still soft!! I’m 5’7 135 and long legged. Was so glad I could order the 34 inch inseam. Usually sweats are just ever-so-slightly too short. I bought in multiple colors. I bought the polar fleece ones

As another reviewer said, finally sweats that are long enough and don't come from the men's department. They are plenty long and don't have extra room for a package I don't have. I am 5'11", 170 lbs. with an athletic build. I got an XL, with the 34 in. inseam. I meant to order the 36 in. inseam as much of my height comes from my legs but as you can see these are plenty long. The pictures were taken after the sweats had been washed and dried. Although the tag says not to put in the dryer, I did and they came out fine. I tend to wear my pants down on my hips a little probably from a lifetime of not having pants otherwise long enough and you can see that the drawstring remains in the original position so they fit very comfortably around the waist. I didn't know what to expect with all of the reviews having contradictory information on sizing. They are super soft and kept me warm walking outdoors in freezing temps (that's not hyperbole the temperatures were below freezing and my legs didn't even get chilled after walking nearly a mile). My dog who oversaw the making of this review thinks they look really comfortable and will be great for many long walks.

I will absolutely be purchasing several more pairs of these… they are comfortable and fit perfectly with room in all the appropriate places!! I ordered an XL with the 36” inseam after reading the reviews because of the waistband and I’m so glad I did, they are perfect and I don’t have to keep them so high on my waistline. user_comment: I ordered these pants (Small, 28 inseam) and they fit perfectly. See the pair on the left. I am a small person and it is hard for me to find pants that fit me. I was so happy with the first pair that I ordered two more pairs in different colors. One of those pairs fit perfectly. The other pair had a bag that was labeled Small, 28 inseam, but the pair inside was labeled 31 inseam. I returned it. In hopes of getting the correct size, I ordered two more pairs. I received two pairs both marked as Small, 28 inseam, but (as you can see in the photo, the pair on the right are obviously 31 inseam, and look like they are an extra large or plus size). I kept the pair that fit, returned the wrong size, and ordered two more pairs. This time the two pairs I received are labeled Small, 28 inseam, but are clearly 31 inseam and really large. I would definitely give these pants a five-star rating if I could get 3 more pairs where the size is the same as the initial pair I got. I don't know how to get help from Amazon...so the only thing left for me to do is to write a bad review and hope that the merchant responds (not fair to the merchant in my opinion). It's so hard for a petite person to find pants that fit that I'm stubbornly reordering the pants in hopes of finally getting the right size pants again. MERCHANT, PLEASE RESPOND! Great pants. Not sure what's going on with sizing.

This dress fit perfectly. I am 115# and 5'3". It does fit like a maxi on me. The dress is very comfortable and not see through.

Adorable print...on my undies!! As noted by other reviewers, this is such an adorable dress, but the fabric is way too thin! I tried it on with floral underwear and could see the full print. Even if I wear beige under garments, the pockets show thru, again highlighting just how thin the fabric is. I ordered the white floral to layer with my denim jacket, but I'll have to use it as a sun dress at home. Perhaps a print in the darker colors would be more forgiving. On the plus side, I do love the fit and flare cut which is perfect on my 5' 5" 140 lb frame. The fabric is light without the cheap clingy feel of some poly blends. The seams are well sewn and I really like the pleats across the bodice for added interest. Overall, this is a cute, comfy dress at a nice price. I would just shy away from the white...

I travel out of the country quite often as a a volunteer for different organizations and perform various tasks from cleaning out dog pens for an animal rescue to teaching English language classes for a remote village, so wardrobe choice is essential for these trips! But regardless of the task, location or weather, there is always a need for a nice, simple, lightweight, wash and wear type dress and I am pleased to say this item fits the bill. As far as size, I am 5’-5”, 135 lbs, 34B/C and the small fit perfectly. It has a high rounded neckline and flutter sleeves, as well as 3 lines of pleats across the bust that give the garment a bit of flair. The dress curves in to form a defined waist before it cuts back out to a flowy A-line that ends mid-calf. All the seams appear to be tight without loose threads and is overall a well made garment. The material is a blend of lightweight 95% poly and 5% spandex which gives it a bit of stretch to be able to go over the head and avoid buttons or zippers, so it’s easy on/easy off and is wrinkle free for the most part. The pattern is on the front side of the fabric only with the backside being plain white. I chose a darker color in case the material was see through and, from what I’ve seen in other reviews, it was a good choice. I am extremely happy with the dress and it will be the perfect lightweight “nice” dress for almost any occasion while traveling.

I am so happy with this dress. It is the very popular 95% polyester 5% spandex blend. This fabric feels soft, it flows well, and it has enough stretch be comfortable. It is well-made with no weak seams or loose threads. It has side pockets which I find a real plus. I am 5' 2" and the length is about mid-calf for me. The a-line style is comfortable and forgiving in all the right places. I really appreciate the horizontal tucks across the bodice that elevate the look to something more fashionable than a basic tee-shirt dress. I chose the 01 blue floral pattern which was my favorite of many color options. I think it will look very smart paired with a white linen jacket this spring. You could even add a belt to change up the look. I am sure this will be one of my favorites this spring and summer.

Funny story - I almost don’t even order this because I thought for this price point it was going to be poor material, wouldn’t be flattering, and would be cheaply made. I am so glad I took a chance!!! Not only is this better than I expected, I plan to buy more in other colors/patterns! The fit of this dress is amazing. It is true to size (I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 169# 38C body) and I feel like I am quite comfortable in it. The length is really nice. The detail on the chest really adds a nice touch that prevents this from just being a t-shirt dress. I am actually already planning to put a nice jacket over this and wear it to a work event coming up! I travel nearly weekly for work, so the fact that this also was not at all wrinkled when I opened it is huge. I always am on the lookout for things that lack and travel well. The color and the pattern were actually better than I expected based on the photo in the advertisement. I seriously could not be happier with it!

I’d stay pregnant forever if it meant I could wear these pants every day. They’re very flattering, extremely comfortable and easy to dress up or down. The only downside is they don’t come in more colors! I’m 5’4” and ordered the regular length and it’s perfect

The structure of the shoes/boots is great! It's well made and the heel feels sturdy. I only gave it 4/5 stars because the material feels like after constant wear it will start peeling, but I think that's just normal for faux suede. I tend to have wide calfs (I'm 170lbs) and these fit just about right while wearing leggings. So if you have wide calfs, these might not be for you. I wear a size 9W or 9.5. I got these in 9.5 and they fit true to size. Overall they are super cute and perfect for the fall. The color matches what the picture shows.

I’m usually a 9 in boots but I got a 8.5. It still fit but could be a bit snug if your not use to fitted shoes. The heel was a perfect hight as I’m 5’4 very plump with short legs, I hate walking in heels lol. Only con- the fur is very cheap (like a stuffed animal from the .99 cent store) & came slightly madden but that can be brushed out. Nonetheless, I needed a quick boot and it did the job.

I had high hopes for this dress, but unfortunately it didn’t work for me. I often struggle to find dresses that aren’t too long, as I’m only 5’1” and weigh 185 pounds. On me, the dress felt unflattering and frumpy. I asked my sister to try it on instead—she’s the one shown in the photos. She is 5’4” and weighs roughly 170 pounds. Even on her, the dress still feels quite long, which leads me to believe it isn’t well-suited for shorter individuals. Overall, I didn’t find the dress flattering for my body type. For reference, this dress is an XL, while I usually wear a 2XL.

Nice light fabric, pleasantly suprised how well they fit and look. I’m 5’9 about 150lbs and purchased a medium in 31 inch inseam which fit great length wise!

These pants are really nice looking. The wine color is even prettier in person and the fabric is so lightweight and comfortable. They are perfect for travel or lounging at home since they are almost unbelievably airy. The front seam makes my legs look longer and I love that it has pockets. I am 5’6” and 145 lbs and got the Medium with the 29” inseam. The fit confused me a bit because the waistband was not snug enough, and the pants felt a little baggy and long. I honestly can’t tell if a shorter inseam would fix it or if the slightly loose waistband was the main issue. The chart suggested 27” for my height, and I am wondering now if I probably should have chosen that. They are beautiful and super comfy. I just wish the fit was a bit better on me.

Actually had a hard time getting a picture of these because I tried them on as soon as they arrived and then wore them around the house for like 3 days straight! Being 5'8" I got the 31" inseam and wow... I'm not used to having a bit of pants pooled around the ankles! I've already worn these casually around the house, out on a weekend, and even to the gym once! The material is like REI sport apparel quality... very lightweight and flowy, with all directions stretch, and the length is perfect. Super flattering. With the stylish pleat down the front, I may even be able to style these for office wear! Easy to wash. Dries super fast for hand washing during travel, and packs/unpacks with NO wrinkles. Basically, the perfect all around pants.

Very comfortable, I am about 5 foot seven and 140 pounds wearing a size medium and the fit is great. however, the material is not the best quality. The stitching doesn’t seem very strong and unfortunately does not have pockets. The chest material is quite thin so you need to wear a bra with it. Wish it had built-in padding because the back is quite low, making it difficult to find a bra that fits with it. Overall, the jumpsuit is cute and flattering for the price.
Runs big.. I ordered a size small I'm 5'2 (not meant to fit tigh like it says.. it's very loose and long .. it is very comfortable, quality is good the style is nice.

I like it I think I got too big of a size! I will be ordering another one in a smaller size I'm 5’3 and 145 lbs I ordered a M The length is very long I’m stepping on the bottom of the pants But I really like how comfortable it is

I looooved these modal sweatpants from G4Free. The fabric is dreamy soft, like butter. The legs are straight but slightly wide. Slant pockets. The ribbed waistband is adjustable with a drawstring. These pants had everything I could have dreamed about except length. I am 6 feet tall and my inseam is 33-34 inches and the tall version of these pants have a 31 inch inseam, which in my opinion is not considered a size “tall”. So tall ladies beware. On me, these pants are ankle pants at best. Length disappointment aside, they are so well made and the fabric so luxurious. If you don’t have really long legs, you will be pleased with these sweatpants from G4Free. If they offered a 33 inch inseam, I’d be right on it.

PROS: The pants drape beautifully and dont wrinkle! I bought 5 pairs different colors. They're heavy enough to hold their shape and cover my bulges (unlike other wide pleated pants), and they're light enough to wear in spring, summer, or fall. Second, they're so comfortable! I could live in them. Third, they dress up or down. I think they would enhance any body type. I ordered one size up because I didnt want any "pull" across heavy areas, and these delivered! CONS: First thing: I had to hem all 5 pairs (these pics are after hemming). For reference, I'm 5'6" but wear 2-3" heels or platform sneekers. The Burgundy and Light Pink pants were 3" too long; Green and Beige were 2" too long; Black were 1" too long. But easy to hem. Second, these are all 100% synthetic fabric, no cotton. But they tumble dry or dry clean or line dry equally nicely (the tag says no tumbling dry but it's no problem) and no wrinkles--or if you get a couple, theyre easy to steam. Last but not least, they're very high waisted, but can ride lower when hemmed, as pictured. Overall, fantastic purchase! A little pricey but worth it. :) Great for mix and match!

I was worried that these would fit weird. They are awesome! The material is heavy, but with the wide leg, I can wear these even in the summer. The color is more of a chocolate mousse, that some reddish undertones, but I'm ok with that. I am 5'4, 190, curvy and based on reviews I ordered my standard XXL so that they would be roomy and they are. They are hella long for me but I sew, so I am going to tailor them for a specific pair of shoes. I ironed it on low/medium with lots of steam and they look beautiful. I do have a couple of picky things....For the price I would have expected the strings to be clipped. I love that they are dress slacks material and have both side pockets and cargo pockets...and yes, I will use the cargo pockets. I do like the pleats, but I am going to top stitch them flat for about 1.5 inches. It lays better and helps hide the "pooch". One last thing. I love that it has elastic across the back waistband. The photo isn't really clear whether it does. Not sure if its a size thing or standard.

They are hella long for me but I sew, so I am going to tailor them for a specific pair of shoes. I ironed it on low/medium with lots of steam and they look beautiful. I do have a couple of picky things....For the price I would have expected the strings to be clipped. I love that they are dress slacks material and have both side pockets and cargo pockets...and yes, I will use the cargo pockets. I do like the pleats, but I am going to top stitch them flat for about 1.5 inches. It lays better and helps hide the "pooch". One last thing. I love that it has elastic across the back waistband. The photo isn't really clear whether it does. Not sure if its a size thing or standard.

5'6" 115-118 lbs. (depending on the day) ordered a size 4 (normal size). These pants are made for us long legged ladies. I love when I can find a pair of pants that hits the floor or even the top of any of my shoes, flats or heels, which you can wear both with these pants. They are high waisted, as in right at the belly button (I have very long legs with a short torso), slight pleat in the front (great for hiding the perimenopause pooch), elastic waist band in the back. I own them in five different colors, grey, black, khaki, green and oatmeal. They can be dressed upped for the office or dressed down for casual were (yes they are that comfy to wear). I love the cargo, baggy look of these pants. I did take one star off, because the sizing/length is not consistant. I actually had to have the oatmeal colored ones hemmed by 1" because they were actually too long (surprise)! I am excited to order more.

These are the perfect pants for my build. I’m 5’8” & 160 pounds and the pants have just the right amount of stretch for my belly and enough leg room without looking baggy. My sizing basics, to start: I am apple/heart-shaped (broad shoulders) Height 5'6" (170 cm) Weight 125 lbs (about 9 st.) Breast 36" (B-cup) (91 cm) Waist 30" (76 cm) Hips 36" (94 cm) I bought two variations: Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Classic High Rise Tapered Jean, Madison wash, 6 average and Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Classic High Rise Tapered Jean, Portland Wash, 4 Average They were both too large for me. I could pull both of them up over my hips while they were zipped and buttoned, so that means wayyy too big. I'm not remarkably sized in any area except my shoulders, so I was a bit shocked at how large these were on me. The cut was tapered, supposedly, but still really wide at the calves. Size 4 picture attached. I didn't take a photo of the 6 before returning. The material wasn't terribly heavy, but lightweight denim due to the stretch capability (which I hate but can hardly ever find any that don't have elastane or similar materials). Stitching seemed well made.

Ordered two pair of these, an 8L (recommended by Amazon) and a 6L (because 6 is my usual size in pants). For reference, I’m 5’7” and 125 lbs, abt a 37” hip. The 8L were big, boxy, too long and too loose (wrong size); the 6L were a better fit—definitely not tight, though at least 4” too long. Returned both these Long-sized Amazon orders, and since Amanda regular 6 was not available, I ordered a pair in that size elsewhere. The reg 6 fits better, though still longer than other regular-sized jeans I own (a plus—can launder without worry). The Amanda style is a more loose-fit style than skinny jeans, and definitely look looser on me than on the model. But they are a higher-waisted, stretchy style, esp good for when you’re going to be doing a lot of sitting (eg. travel). *UPDATE: The 6R seemed to stretch out and get even looser after a few days of travel wearing last week. Based on reviews by others my size or larger, I ordered a size 4R (Zermatt), even though Amazon was now suggesting I order 6L. The 4R is actually the best fit in this Amanda style, and is still plenty long. *PHOTOS are of the 4R. (These pants did need to be washed right away due to strong chemical smell.) 2nd UPDATE: I ordered the exact same 4Reg GV Amanda jeans this week in black and in a medium (“Frisco”) blue. But both of these were 1” shorter and a bit snugger than the 4Reg powder blue “Zermatt” and navy “Scottsdale” colors I’d ordered before. Go figure. These skimpier 4Reg pants had a “Made in Bangladesh” label; the two blue options successfully ordered before are 1” longer and a perfect fit, and labeled “Made in Jordan.” Returning the Bangladesh jeans for refund; will try to just buy the ones made in Jordan.

Purchased XL in black & light denim; 5'7"; 219 LBS; Medium thick bottom/hips. These jeans were very hard to pull up on me. I am not a member of the medical community, but I have been replacing some of my leggings with scrubs because of the comfort. And these by Med Couture are my favorites. They are an updated straight legged style. The details on these scrubs really dress them up.On the practical side. They are fairly lightweight, but I am not cold in them. These scrubs have 3% spandex so there is good stretch and range of motion. I was thrilled to be able to order them in a petite length. I am 5 feet tall, 110 pounds and the split hem skims perfectly over the top of my shoes. The small fit true to size. I ordered them in the color pewter and love this dark shade because it compliments many of my tops. There are six pockets, with the front ones being roomy enough to handle a cell phone. I really like the double side seams, the split hem and ribbed fabric on front pocket. These special touches make these scrubs a cut above the rest for me! I want more and highly recommend!

Awesome! Truly amazing — Amazon pants have never fit me right, but I kept trying, and today I finally found the pair. These are the best pants — they look great, even when I’m bloated, and you’d never know it! After a major weight gain, nothing seemed to look right… but these do! For reference, I’m 5’7” and about 170 lbs.

These are very comfortable to wear and the pockets are deep and soft on the inside. They hug to your middle and behind but are loose fitting on the legs, like dress pants would be. The waist is comfortable, and not as tight as some other yoga pants I own. The fabric has stretch to it. It is true to sizing. Only note is the length of the pants. I chose the 31" inseam for up to 5'8" and the pants are a little long. I think with shoes or boots they will be fine. For ref. I am 5'7" 145# and the Med fit me perfectly.

I purchased these for my daughter. She wears a size 10 wide shoe. This brand usually will fit average to wide so I ordered the size 10 in black faux suede. The black is a nice deep velvet black color. It has cute western embroidered detailing on the shaft. The toe is pointed and plain. It has a zipper on the ankle but is basically a pull on style boot with boot straps on the sides. My daughter is 5’4” and has thick calves and wide feet. This boot fit her feet and calves comfortably. The were not too tight. The main issue and the reason why I deducted a star is they are super tall. They hit about the top third of her kneecap and she said was very uncomfortable. Fortunately sine these are a soft suede fabric she was able to ruche the ankles a bit to get them under her knee but to me it just looks odd and sloppy. These would be perfect for someone tall with a size 10 foot but anyone on the shorter side may have the same issue.

Oh. My. Gosh! These are gorgeous boots! First off let me tell you that I’m NOT a cowboy boot girl but I am a boot and shoe lover and I own dozens of each and when I saw these beauties on Amazon I had to try them. Well, these are so wonderful that I’m now a cowgirl boot girl! Let me tell you about why I fell for these upon trying them on and walking in them: - they’re of a thick suede like material that’s sturdy yet soft and flexible. - the inner fabric is soft and easy against my skin. - their cushiony insole is Oh-So comfortable so I can wear them all day with no achy feet. - the chunky block heel is super cool! - the outsole is non slip making them safe for winter wear. - the half zipper makes them super easy to slip on and off. - the beautiful embroidered design is strikingly attractive and eye catching. - they come in multiple gorgeous, feminine colors so I’ll get a few more pair to go with different outfits. I chose the brown and this color is more of a dark tan that’s extremely appealing and that goes with everything. - they fit true to size and the footbed is wide enough that you don’t need to size up to accommodate the softly pointed toe. Note in Sizing: don’t size up. I normally wear 7.5 or 8 in shoes and boots and I sized up to an 8.5 because of the pointed toe but they’re roomy on me so I didn’t need to size up the 8 would fit much better. I’ll just put a heel pad in these ones so they’ll fit better. Note: If there’s one, and only one, thing that is off about these boots it’s their shaft height, it’s very tall. I’m 5’ 4”and these boots come all the way up to cover my knee. Since they have a curve at front and back I can bend my knees comfortably while wearing them but I’d much prefer them to hit at the bottom of my knee. Since these have so many other really great benefits and since I can easily bend my knees in them I’ll deal with the height.

I ordered these pants following the size chart and still ended up returning them due to fit. My waist measures 35 inches and my hips 41 inches, so I ordered XL with a 27-inch inseam (I’m 5’5”). The inseam length was perfect for my height. However, the waist ran large. Laid flat, the waist measures 17.4 inches, which resulted in a loose fit at the waist, as shown in my video. In the video I misspoke about my height 😅 but the measurements shown are accurate. I really liked the blue color, and the modal fabric feels very soft against the skin. The fabric type matches exactly what’s described in the product listing. It also has two side pockets, which make it easy to style either casually or in a more polished, dressier way. I returned them due to the waist fit, not the quality. I will definitely reorder them in one or two sizes down, as the length and fabric worked well for me. CONCLUSION Great fabric, color, and versatility, but the waist runs large. I recommend checking measurements carefully before ordering. I hope this review helps you decide.

I love these pants/these sets, just didn’t love the Khaki color on me. I’m 5’0 115lbs (smaller waist, hold my weight in my legs and hips) and the XS 27inch inseam was perfect. I’m also wearing the shirt in an XS. I returned and am going to order a different color. But the quality is incredible and so soft!

These pants are great. Edit to add, they 'snag' very easily. I didn't do anything memorable to make this happen. Too bad, I really liked them. Nice thickness for almost all seasons (probably too warm for actual summer), and camouflaging age &/or weight related "skin imperfections". Good pockets with a slightly smaller opening. You can get your phone in, but it won't fall out as you move around. They seem well made. Im 5'9, 150-155 lbs. I ordered both medium and large looking for good length (amazon said a L would fit best). I'm sending large back. The length was just a tad long, but the waist & hips were too big. I have not laundered yet. It says dry flat, so I'm hoping there's no shrinkage.

These pants are super flattering and comfortable for everyday wear or work. The Summer Tan color is a beautiful neutral that’s easy to pair with almost any top. The fabric feels sturdy but has a nice stretch, so they move with you without losing their shape throughout the day. I ordered a 14 Short, and while they fit great in the waist and hips, they’re still just a bit long for my height (I’m around 5’0”). I’ll probably get them hemmed ( they are tucked under in the photo) for a perfect length, but I still really like the slim, tailored look. They give that “put-together” vibe without feeling stiff or uncomfortable. If you’re in between lengths or on the shorter side like me, just be aware you might need a slight adjustment. Still a great buy overall—I’d definitely recommend them!

Pros: These are a good quality and feel like yoga pants versus tights. I have the black and navy and neither are see-through. They hold their shape (e.g. don’t get saggy knees by the end of the day). I LOVE that they have the side pockets for my phone and I refuse to buy pants without pockets. Standard yoga pants were getting uncomfortable being tight on my belly and these provide the stretchy relief I needed and I think it will stretch well as my belly continues to grow. Con: I am 5’5” and 114 lbs. I ordered a small. They fit everywhere, but like other purchasers have mentioned, the horizontal seam across the butt sags too low and should be placed about 3” higher. I end up hiking up to the pants frequently because it has the feeling of when you’re butt crack is showing from low rise jeans. I hope the manufacturer heeds the feedback and adjusts the seam height. Overall, I feel like it is a good product for the price, but would be much more satisfied with a seam adjustment.

These are my favorite pants. They are warm, wide-legged, and super comfortable. They are stylish too because wide pants are trending. I got these a few months ago when they were $31 but I noticed the price went up. I would say it was worth the $30. I might buy another if there is another sale. After washing, the quality is still the same! For reference I’m 5’1 and ~110 pounds! They are perfect length. Good for ~50 F weather!

5'3, 120lbs, waist 26', hip 37', ordered khaki, size small with 31' inseam. They have a beautiful, shapely fit without being too tight. I love the khaki color and because they are fleece lined, they are not see-through. Edit: After loving the khaki colored pants, I ordered them in black. Very disappointing. They came with an elastic band sewn into the top of the waist band, which is a non-negotiable for me. I believe pants that are expertly tailored do not need elastic bands around the stomach. Also, the black ones came a full inch shorter than the khaki ones despite ordering the 31' inseam. The khaki pants' label has the 31' inseam length printed on the label, the black pants' label doesnt even list the inseam length. I hope the company goes back to the waistband-free version and gets their inseams figured out. To conclude, the khaki colored pants are all I've been wearing this winter. I have many items by G4Free, they are a 10/10 brand, but the black version of these pants did not work and needed to be returned.

I am 5’2” and 100lbs. Shoe size 5.5/6. I’m pretty skinny. Socks fit perfectly on me- not too tight or loose. Doesn’t roll down. Material is pretty decent imo. It stops right below the knee which is what I was looking for. It’s not easy or comfortable to pull above the knee. The foot part has some more room for someone with bigger feet than mine (I can easily stretch it like 4 more inches comfortably). Overall, has some stretchiness. There is no smell. I’ll probably buy more colors later.

I had already bought a pair of this scrub before and since I am 5’6” wearing size3-5 I order XS tall. The first pair they sent me was really baggy compared to my previous pants. They did not fit like an xs I emailed them and they immediately sent me new pants which fit way different, and better. It was kind of weird because the first pants sent to me were larger and had different tags compared to the second pair they sent me. You can see how the one W/tags is larger looking than the one without tags. Plus the second pair seemed to be a little darker in color. Not sure if the pair w/tags was just an older/different version? Just a little sketchy feeling when ordering but other than that the second pair fit SO well. This brand is amazing. Wash and dry over and over with no problems.

I now have 3 pairs after my first ones lasted so well for 2 years and numerous washes. I have 1 in pewter, caribbean, and light gray now and I love them all. Im 5'3" 34-28-39.5 and usually wear a size 2 womens or size 3-5 in juniors pants and i wear an xs regular in these. They don't drag on the floor with shoes on and they fit my waist and butt comfortably. The drawstring is basically for show, it doesn't tighten much. The material is soft and light. Doesn't shrink in the dryer. Doesn't hold on to wrinkles or lint. Colors don't fade. No pilling in the inner thighs even after 2 whole years of use. Which, if u have this problem you understand it's almost unheard of. I LOVE THEM. If i was allowed to wear scrubs to work every day I'd buy a bunch of these. I pair it with the Juliet 2245 top in xs.

Loving the side pockets as well.

I am 5’’7” @148 lbs. my inseam from my crotch to the bottom of my foot is 32 inches. I bought the medium in a 32" and it’s really perfect. I’ve included a picture with hem without shoes. I like the material it’s a soft, stretching material. There’s not a lot of compression, but I don’t really need compression. They’re extremely comfortable, and just perfect for what my intention was, which was probably to lounge around the house or to practice yoga. As far as thickness they are average, I can’t see anything when I’ve bend over to me their a nice, medium weight, leisure pants. They was well no shrinking. I ordered a second pair.

I am 5'7", and the 29" (blue) are a bit short for me, at least to wear with sneakers. The 32" alternative (wine red in the picture) were initually too long, and I ended up folding the bottoms before the first wash. For my legs, 30-31" would be ideal. If you plan to wear these pants for yoga practice, then the 29" is good length. I think after a warm wash the 32" wine pants became OK, still a tiny bit long, but don't require folding anymore and don't sweep the ground, just touch it in sneakers. The pants are pretty thick and a bit compressing, comparing to a lighter version I have from another brand. Love the shape of the front, and deep side pockets. The wine color works great with blue (bright, light and sky), or pink/light purple.

My husband and I have some upcoming travel plans that will take us to some areas that will have temps in the upper 90’s… too hot! I’ve been looking for clothing that is not only cool but lightweight and wrinkle-resistant. These pants are perfect!!I’m a former ICU/ER nurse and had to wear scrubs daily at work. They were always comfortable, but it never occurred to me, now that I’m retired, to purchase scrubs for daily wear. These were really a revelation for me! Very neat, no ironing required, lightweight, and multiple pockets, what more could a girl ask for?These have an elastic waist that is very forgiving, as well as a drawstring for reinforcement. The fabric is not see through, but is rather thin, so although there are multiple pockets, I wouldn’t try to stuff them with bulky items. A really nice detail is the small slit in the outer seam at the bottom edges, it really makes the pants look nice. These are available in petite, regular and tall. I have a 32” inseam, so ordered the Tall size. Although the “Tall” states it is 32.5” inseam but measures 33.25”. I have no problem hemming them up an inch but just an FYI. I’m just delighted that I can find pants long enough for me.I am thrilled to have found these but must still deduct one star for the length.I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

I’m just delighted that I can find pants long enough for me.I am thrilled to have found these but must still deduct one star for the length.I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

I’m just delighted that I can find pants long enough for me.I am thrilled to have found these but must still deduct one star for the length.I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

They are a dry fit material. I like how they feel. I do have to pull them up sometimes. I am 5’11” and about 205 and I got a large. They seem to fit fine. If I went a size down I think it would be too tight. Overall I like them. I ordered black and grey as well. I like the pockets too!

4’10, 110lbs, size small - should have gotten picture of me showing it in full length of legging but I didn’t have much time. Cons: crisscross part folds over while working out and bending, etc. if it is high on your waist like me. The description says 7/8 length, so if you’re my height or shorter, even at that lenght, it’s still a bit long and bunch a bit at the ankle. It isn’t tagless, and so I was a little annoyed to have to cut it off and carefully take out the take from the threads. The tag is rough and scratchy. Pros: cute, nice color options, soft and comfortable fabric. No front seams as advertised and has side pockets. I’d say it’s worth it when it’s on sale. Almost perfect if you fit it better in length. I hope the company listens to customers ans create even more size options for petites! I don’t want to keep having to resort to cropped leggings!

Do reference: I’m 5ft 123 lbs. These fit great and are very comfortable material. however they DO NOT stay up. It is so annoying to have to keep pulling them up during workouts or just everyday use. I would not recommend and I won’t buy the again. So sad.

I am 5'4" and the boots reach right at the knee which is exactly what I was looking for. The only reason I gave it 4.5 is because they run slightly big. I ordered my regular size (9) and they fit big in the foot area, but perfect around the calves area. I ordered half a size smaller 8.5 and they were perfect in the foot area, but they did not zip up in the calves area. I ended up keeping the size 9 because the width in the calves area is a few millimeters biggers allowing me to zip them up . I included pictures of the differences between my regular size of boots and these.

Overall, these are great pants (and attractive enough that they definitely don’t need to be relegated to lounge pants)! They are very comfortable and the cut is flattering. At 5’2” and 110lbs, the 28” inseam fits perfectly. Having said that, I do have some lumps and bumps in my upper thighs that unfortunately the material follows perfectly, highlighting them and making me feel self conscious (however, a nice pair of slip shorts solves this problem). I think a darker color would prevent this and the pants are so flattering otherwise I plan to order a darker color to try it!

This is a great pair of boots to wear as part of a costume or for fashion. They are made of faux leather so I’m not sure how confident I am that they could be used for riding as stated in the listing without damaging the finish. It will be interesting to see how the finish on the heel holds up as well. I can’t tell if it’s a solid piece or a veneer but it’s some kind of plastic/resin. As a bonus, they come in a really nice box if you want to give them to somebody. That being said, these are truly knee high boots. I am 5’7” tall and am more torso than leg. I usually have to buy petite jeans. These boots go all the way up to my knees. I also have large wide feet. I got a size 11 and was skeptical when I saw how narrow the toe was. But my feet went in easily and the boots were comfortable. The heel was also higher than I normally wear, but it did not put too much pressure on my toes. I could definitely wear these going out, but I don’t think I could survive a whole day in them. The boots are comfortable and lightweight so it is easy to move around. The stitching for the design, inside of the boot, and around the soul are all good quality. My only real issue is that even though I have the boots pulled up all the way, there is still some sagging between my ankle in the middle of my calf. The top of the boots are snug around the top of my calves. I will post a couple of pictures to show what I’m talking about. I did fill them with bubble wrap to try and smooth out those spots while they sit in my closet. Otherwise I like the style and cut of the boots. Price wise I think these are a good value for what you’re getting. I had an old pair of knee-high boots that I had for over a decade and they finally bit the dust so I was happy to come upon these.

I could definitely wear these going out, but I don’t think I could survive a whole day in them. The boots are comfortable and lightweight so it is easy to move around. The stitching for the design, inside of the boot, and around the soul are all good quality. My only real issue is that even though I have the boots pulled up all the way, there is still some sagging between my ankle in the middle of my calf. The top of the boots are snug around the top of my calves. I will post a couple of pictures to show what I’m talking about. I did fill them with bubble wrap to try and smooth out those spots while they sit in my closet. Otherwise I like the style and cut of the boots. Price wise I think these are a good value for what you’re getting. I had an old pair of knee-high boots that I had for over a decade and they finally bit the dust so I was happy to come upon these.

Beautiful boots!! They're faux leather with an elegant and subtle embroidery design. Also appreciate the block heel. However, If you're short and thick these boots may not be for you. They're too long in the shaft for my 5"2' frame and barely fit my calf. They would be great boots for a taller and thinner person IMO. They're also pricey and would recommend purchasing them when they go on sale.

Value: 8/10 Quality: 10/10 Material: man-made Color: dark brown Size: I ordered up a 1/2 size for socks, perfect fit Step into style with these man-made, dark brown cowboy boots. They're like the trusty steed of footwear—stylish, comfortable, and always ready for an adventure! The block heel and slightly cushioned insole ensure all-day comfort. And let’s talk about synthetic material: easy to clean and maintain, these boots laugh in the face of mud puddles and rainstorms. Slipped them on with ease, though they might be snug for those with athletic calves. Standing at 5’2”, I found these boots hit just below my knees—making them my go-to rain boots with a twist. Are they worth the splurge? Time will tell, but they're a great leather alternative for bad weather! I’m eyeing the pink version of these bad weather beauties.

Update: i returned them. kept falling down. not sure what "7/8 petite" means on the label of the pic i provided. But they were not made for my tall body. Update: i have only washed them, air dried. i want to say that if you're taller than 5'9, i would advise against the 27 in inseam. the crotch keeps falling down between my thighs. i will get the shorter inseam ones next time. i thought i needed the longer length, but honestly, the more i move, the more they pull down. i understand that may be due to the size so i will get an xxl next. I am 5'9, 197lbs. I have long legs and a short torso. My waist size is 38 in, my hips are 45 in. I bought the XL 27 in inseam one in black. Not see through. Opaque. I am super happy that they are not thin as skin like some other brands out there. I love that they are actual leggings, instead of colored nylons. They don't compress the fat around my knees, but there is a slight compression around my middle. They come up past my bellybutton and still reach my ankles. They don't unfold at the waist when i sit. I don't work out i just wanted a nice pair of leggings. The pockets are slim fitted to the thighs so no baggy-ness. They fit me very nice. IF i had went by their size chart, i would've got an xxl. But i decided to go down a size for the compression, and they are very comfortable and i am happy i did. i am not a paid influencer and i didn't receive these as a promotion. Just a real 46 yr old mom of 3. :)

My stats: almost 70y/o, 5’4”, 112lbs, waist 27”, hips 38”, inseam 29”. I bought size 6 with a 30” inseam. These fit my body type well. The fabric is not very thick like jeans with a higher % of cotton. I’m assuming like all jeans with a synthetic/cotton fabric that they will stretch out as the day progresses. I will wear an elastic belt to keep them fitting as this occurs. They are standard height waist, not high waisted as described. The waistband comes even with my hips. I haven’t washed them yet but I think they are very nice. If they shrink, it’s nothing that a few squats won’t fix by stretching them out. I am happy with my purchase.

Read All - I am very happy with how these jean fit. I was not sure what size to order, as my weight has been fluctuating. I usually were a size 12 in most pants, I weight 164 and I am 5'5". When I looked at the size chart and checked with another pair of wranglers I already own and still fit at size10W, I went with the 10W 30L. Length is perfect for my built however the fit is pretty snug. What I mean is it feels like I'm between sizes. The jeans are made with a stretchy material and give enough (At least for me) to get into. Maybe I should have ordered a 12W but these look flattering and if I lose 5-10lbs they would be the perfect fit. Side note-I hold my weigth mostly in my hips and some in my gut. I'm not sure how they wash up or if they shrink in wash. Although they are on the snug side, they are comfortable. I have attached some photo and hope this helps with your decision.

This is my first pair of Wrangler jeans, and I decided to try them because of the reviews and as they were a bargain. I had been fairly confident they would fit after examining their sizing, but was still surprised to feel for myself that they indeed fit like a glove. I'm 165cm, weigh about 70kg, and with a 30-31" waist and 41-42" hips. Many of the jeans I own (mostly Levi's, a few pairs of Uniqlo) fit one or the other. With stretchy material the fit is better, but I always feel that the fit is slightly off. These Wranglers wowed me after I tried them on. They are a true high waist, which helps to disguise my midsection, and are snug but comfortable in the hips, even when sitting down. I've ordered three more pairs in the same measurements as they were all on sale, and look forward to receiving them. I'll probably try more Wranglers in future as well due to how well this fits, and how much I like the way I look when I'm in them.

You guys! If you have long legs, you need these! I am 5’7” - mostly legs. Weight is 135-140. These are a nice thick sweat pant with wide legs (not too wide) and deep pockets. They fit like a dream! They are not tight fitting, which I did not want. I ordered a medium and everything is perfect! So perfect I just ordered another pair in grey. Sizing on these was really difficult. It took a few tries to figure it out so hopefully I can help someone. After reading about how raw denim works, it is best to choose a size that is slightly tight along your body.Because as you wear them, it will conform to your body.And stretch about a half an inch. So keep that in mind when you are looking at the size chart.You may want to go smaller I am 3 month postpartum looking for pants, leaving me somewhat squishy in the middle leaving me at a 31 waist and 42 hip. When I tried the size 11 to my measurements they where waaay too big. I tried the 9, still not good. Left crotch saggy. So I decided to skip the seven and went to the 5. I like the fit in the legs and honestly I think I will get them to fit great after constant wears, just a lil tight in the waist but I think if I get them wet amd wear them around I can fix that. I think the moral of the story is I woild size down 1 or even 2 sizes if you like your pants to fit tighter and not have a baggy crotch. This is coming from someone tall without alot of booty so If you are more curvy you may have a different experience but if u look like me size down. In the end u really like them and I think they will last me a long time

I like the way these jeans fit they are made of cotton and they do not have any stretch. They are like the kind I wore back in the day. They are made to last.. But I ordered the lite color and obviously I was sent the wrong color these are much darker. I really wanted the lighter color for an event I was going to but now it’s too late. I’m not sure if I want to send them back or not because these kind of pants are hard to find. Plus who knows if I would get the color I want even though I couldn’t get them in time when I needed them. I took too stars off because I was sent the wrong color. Other than that I really like the jeans. For reference I’m about 5”9 and weigh 130 pounds I ordered the size 3-36 and they fit perfectly. Update… I decided to keep the one that they sent me that was the Wong color. I tried again and I got the right color this time 😁😁 but these don’t fit the same as the darker ones. They are a little bit big in the behind. The length is great it’s hard to find jeans that are long enough. I have washed them 2 times in hot water hoping they would shrink just a little (I have lost 8 pounds since I posted my review on the darker!(I wish it was more) I’m happy they didn’t shrink in the length.The bottom of the pants keep rolling up I’m not sure why. I’m Trying to gain some weight so maybe they will fit better. Overall I like the pants if I gain too much weight I might need some that are bigger and I will definitely come back and buy more of these pants.

EDITED REVIEW*** Umm... I'm not quite sure what happened, but I decided to order these Cowboy Cut in Blue because the Black ones were perfect. These jeans are the exact same size, but the blue ones are HUGE. They don't fit. Additionally I believe these were a return because they came with no tags and they feel like they've been washed. I'm disappointed. But my black jeans are still great. So these are some TRUE 100% cotton Jeans. There is no give and zero stretch which is what I wanted. After looking at the size chart I decided to get a size 1×30. I am 5'4, 125lbs and wear a size 25 in Levis 501s. In my opinion these jeans fit and look similar. I am definitely glad I got the size 1 because the zero definitely would have been too small. I can't wait to break these in and I'll definitely be getting another pair in a different color

I'm nearly 6' tall, and I have a 34" inseam which is nearly impossible to find. I have spent up to $100 on jeans from other stores to get a pair that are long enough, and they all have terrible gaps in the back. These Lee Meridian jeans over in the low $20 range and fit better than anything else I have bought at any price. They have a bit of stretch to them and they do get just a bit looser after I've worn them for a bit. I bought my normal size, a 22L, and they are perfect for when I don't want to feel constrained. They did stretch, so I bought a 20L, which is what I have on in the video. They were a bit tight in the waist at first but quickly stretched enough to make them very comfortable, too. These are not super-heavy denim; they are a bit lighter-weight, which I happen to love. I have 5 pairs of different Lee jeans in 3-4 different styles. I love them all, but these are my favorite!

Buying jeans as a tall plus size woman has never been an easy experience after watching some YouTube videos of Amazon hauls and reading the reviews about these jeans I decided to give it a shot. I purchased a pair last night in 18 Plus, the Meridian. They arrived today and they fit absolutely like a dream. i’m 511 my waist is a 38 hips are 46 and the jeans went on without any problem. They’re easy to move in so far no sagging and I’m looking forward to wearing them. These are not like other plus size jeans that have a lot of stretch in the material and so after a while, they become baggy the material is denim, even though it’s lightweight. Buy the jeans!

I've never owned a pair of uggs that go past my upper ankle until this pair. All past pairs fit excellent. How these boot fits around my calf is another story. I measured my calf with my foot flat on the floor and relaxed and my calf 44.5 inches. Keep in mind I'm also 6ft so I know I'm not an average size woman....but these boots are a bit TIGHT at the calf. With leggings they fit with ease. Over jeans they are a bit tighter, but they DO make it over the jean material. I can walk around and they aren't uncomfortable at all, just not a lot of wiggle room if thats what your looking for.

Never order jeans based on just whatever you normally wear. Read the size chart but then read the reviews on sizing. I am 5'6 and weigh 145lbs. In levi signature jeans, I'm a size 4/27 because they run large and are stretchy. In typical levis, lee, american eagle and wrangler, I am a size 28-29 depending on style and stretch. I ordered a size 8 in these pants after reviewing the chart. The Amazon Essential pants I own for work run too big but a few comments on these jeans said they ran small so I just picked the higher end. The fit is confusing. The waist runs a tad small while the rest fits me fine (thick thighs). If you have a smaller waist but are curvy in the hips and rear, these might be perfect for you. These are in no one "medium stretch" as stated. They stretch out throughout the day after wearing them, just like traditional non-stretch jeans. I prefer jeans that have a tad more stretch so they hold their shape and are comfortable to sit in. I will say, they came out of the package very stiff and are a tad softer and more comfortable after washing. They do not look terrible on me but they are not my favorite or most comfortable. Again, buy these based on the size chart and if you have a slimmer waist with a curvy figure and you will probably look fantastic.

Overall, I like the jeans but doubt I would order again. I ordered a size 6, regular.What I like is the fabric. It is a nice denim weight...rugged, yet not overly heavy. There is very little stretch in the denim which I prefer. The pants seem to be sewn well and will hold up over time so you won't split the knees out as fast as some jeans.What I don't like is the fit. To be clear, if I were my 20-something self again, I would probably like them a lot, but being older and having given birth to three boys, the pants are too tight around my waist. The length is also a bit short for me after washing. The pants fit very well in the legs and buttocks so a size up would probably not work .For reference, I am 5'6" tall, weight 128lbs, a bit long legged but never enough to purchase longs (wish I had this time) and I consistently wear a size 6 in all my other jeans.

I don’t often take the time to write a review on anything. I’m a 53-year-old, 6'2" tall female (assigned at birth) who is all legs (37" inseam). I weigh 210lbs and am curvy with a big booty. The 31" long leggings actually go ALL THE WAY DOWN to the bottom of my ankles, and even have a little extra. I wear an XL. They give a nice, firm hug, but are so soft and comfortable. I’m medically supposed to wear compression from my high-waist to my ankles due to chronic illness (POTS, EDS, and 5 other incurable autoimmune diseases). I personally layer a size L under my usual XL to add additional compression. I’m also autistic and have ADHD. I don’t do compression wear well AT ALL! These leggings aren’t causing any sensory issues for me whatsoever.

These are nice pants! I got a size 12 regular and I'm 5'3. I didn't want to get the short size because I like my pants longer but I will likely hem them an inch or two so they are more comfortable with and without heels. They are super stretchy so I could've even gone a size down and with the short size option, I just wanted wiggle room. I didn't have the sticker on the leg so there was no damage to the pants. They also do have back pockets!!! You just need to remove the small strip of stitching. The front pockets can fit my fist. They are mid rise and pass the squat test lol. Once I give these a try and see how long they hold up I'll probably get more colors!

These pants are lovely, great fabric and VERY well made. For my body type, they don’t work. There is nothing wrong with them at all and I encourage people to purchase these if they sound like a good fit. I am only trying to help people in their purchasing/buying debacle. I am 5’6, 130 lbs. I bought a size 4. They fit just fine but the waist was a bit snug (not tight, but snug). The legs were large/unprofessional/baggy on me. But the length was excessive. If I was wearing a 3” heel, fine. But anything shorter than that, they looked like warehouse pants. I attached pic to give an idea. I kept these because I actually like the baggy style when not at work. However, I was searching for a casual business pant and these didn’t quite fit the requirement. Oh, another thing worth mentioning…. They are kinda tight in the butt crack area. So if you sit all day, you probably won’t be able to sit all day.

Ok, I still don’t know what cut these are but I would say somewhere between bootcut and like a straight wide leg? They are fitted through the top, straight and then straight to the bottom with the a wide leg opening from the knee down. But ad pictured in the product photos, these are not like the flowy wide leg pants a palazzo style. I got the Heather gray color and they look very nice and classy. Definitely going to be my dressier work days go-to. I wore these and dressed them up with the heels, and the black top/blazer you can kind of see pictured to work one day and my husband was like oh you look extra professional today!The main highlight: They are comfy as heck! The stretch is just right, and pretty much true to size, with some caveats. I’m 5’4” and about 170ish, thick thighs, some butt, not a lot of hips, then some pudgy stomach stuff takes over the waistline. I normally wear a 10 regular and purchased that size. At first, I was a little worried they would be tight and that it would put strain on the fly/button but once they were past my thighs and all the way up I could tell they would be fine by the stretch in them. I was very pleased with how they hugged my butt and actually made it look more like the cute booty I used to have in my 20s! 😂 I tried to take pics of back but, girl, they couldn’t do it justice. lol Anyway, the only con would be if you are short, these absolutely run long. I don’t mind that, I will be wearing these with heels and I like my pants on the longer side, so not a problem for me. I did add a pic of the length in a pair of heeled boots, probably about 3” heels so you can see how they hit.The material weight is a mid weight. Might be ok in summer with AC in office setting, maybe a little warm. Not heavy though I’ll be wearing these through Michigan winters just running from my car to the office. It’s not like I’m going doing anything active in them. Perfect for fall/spring.I have only worn them with belts. I just feel like it pulls the outfit together better. So not sure if there would be the back gap issue or slipping but they feel like they’re secure all around without a belt.All pockets are real, just bit smaller like you’d expect on dressy pants. They have been very wrinkle resistant so far, even a bit pet hair resistant. I’m hoping the black material is the same because I just ordered these again in black. (Fingers crossed they’re just as good)I do still prefer everyday casual wide, flowy work pants, but these are absolutely up there with my new top fav pants.

Ok, I still don’t know what cut these are but I would say somewhere between bootcut and like a straight wide leg? They are fitted through the top, straight and then straight to the bottom with the a wide leg opening from the knee down. But ad pictured in the product photos, these are not like the flowy wide leg pants a palazzo style. I got the Heather gray color and they look very nice and classy. Definitely going to be my dressier work days go-to. I wore these and dressed them up with the heels, and the black top/blazer you can kind of see pictured to work one day and my husband was like oh you look extra professional today!The main highlight: They are comfy as heck! The stretch is just right, and pretty much true to size, with some caveats. I’m 5’4” and about 170ish, thick thighs, some butt, not a lot of hips, then some pudgy stomach stuff takes over the waistline. I normally wear a 10 regular and purchased that size. At first, I was a little worried they would be tight and that it would put strain on the fly/button but once they were past my thighs and all the way up I could tell they would be fine by the stretch in them. I was very pleased with how they hugged my butt and actually made it look more like the cute booty I used to have in my 20s! 😂 I tried to take pics of back but, girl, they couldn’t do it justice. lol Anyway, the only con would be if you are short, these absolutely run long. I don’t mind that, I will be wearing these with heels and I like my pants on the longer side, so not a problem for me. I did add a pic of the length in a pair of heeled boots, probably about 3” heels so you can see how they hit.The material weight is a mid weight. I usually don’t have much luck with pants. However, these fit really well and I will be ordering more in other colors. Great quality, not too heavy, and perfect fit for my shape. I am 5’6” and 175 pounds. My struggle is I carry my weight in my belly but have straight hips, and thinner rear and thighs. So usually if I order a 12 to fit my waist it is too baggy in the hips. These are not! I recommend ordering short if you have a normal inseam. These are size 12 Short and they go exactly to the back of my heel without shoes.

These pants are really very comfortable. I’m probably about 200 pounds right now, 5’ 6” and a bit of an apple. I tend to carry most of my weight in my belly area. I bought these in a size 14 regular and they fit great except for they are maybe a little long which is surprising. With heels or loafers though they’ll be just fine but if you’re shorter than me, you might want to get the shorter inseam. These pants are also a little bit more of a wide leg or even boot cut, not a straight leg. I’m OK with that though, so I will be keeping these. I do wish they were a little bit less expensive but I’m overall happy with my purchase. If they go on sale, maybe I’ll see about getting different colors.

I also bought in black and the length is more normal but width of pant leg is smaller and overall fits a bit smaller.

I am 5,1' and weigh 118, I am normally a 6-8 (inbewteen) which is too big in these for the waist and long. I might just have to hem them as these are the short ones as well. I will be returning it for a 4-6 if they have that. There were some wrinkles but that is to be expected. They also seem durable and able to be washed.

These leggings really are soo nice!! Breathable but not thin whatsoever! Not see through! It fits perfect to size! Only thing is, I didn’t know there was a regular size and a petite size for smaller length. I am 5’3” and the regular ones are kinda long that I’m stepping on them. The petite ones would’ve worked better for me personally. The belly band is also very comfortable. It’s folded over for now since I’m not that big yet. But overall I seriously do love these leggings and most definitely will be buying more!

These are such great pants and soooo soft and comfortable!! I love that this brand is a fraction of the price of the Canada lemon brand, and also way better quality!! I also love the extended sizing and lengths, in my case an XXS and 35 inseam. Perfect fit!! Certainly long enough, and fitted enough to look good for literally any occasion (lounge, gym, yoga, errands, flights, or casual outings where you want comfy but not leggings). They really accent my little booty as well! Really impressed with these pants given the price!! For reference: I am 5'9", and 118-20lbs -- and purchased XXS, 35 inseam in dark charcoal grey.
