I was trying to find fashionable wide leg pants that were 1. comfortable, 2. flattering and 3. looked dressy These pants are IT. I have them in black and navy. They are super stretchy just like my favorite joggers but look really put together. I can wear them to church, work, or just around the house. They have some light compression around the waist (like yoga pants) which contributes to the flattering silhouette. I’m 5’7” and the pants are a perfect length. I will always be in flat shoes and they hit well. I’ve washed them many times and dried them in the dryer on low heat. They are holding up well. I don’t know how long wide leg pants will be fashionable but I think these will hold up at least until skinny pants are back in.

My Stats: almost 70 y/o, 5’4”, 27” waist, 38” hips, 29” inseam. AI and the size chart recommended a size M These are a little big on me but thankfully I have discovered boy shorts underwear that is very in making clothes that are a bit big fit better. The legs are wide legs but straight not flared. Length goes over the top of my shoe which is what I like. Nice navy blue. Not too dark. They seem well made. The seams are sewn well and there are no loose threads or defects. I’m happy with this purchase

Amazing fit. Finally a yoga pants for the TALL girls. Very comfortable. Love that it can be dressed up or down. Definitely considering getting another pair in a different color. 5’10” Size 10 ordered Medium 35” inseam.
I am 5'3.5" 135# I ordered Medium 29"inseam. Fit is perfect. The color is a brick red in real life, not maroon or burgundy. Love that they have rear pocket lines not just plain backside.

I am 81 years old and I have bad scoliosis that my body, goes to the right. It affects how my pants fit. You may notice in photos. The second picture, I didn’t fix the waist band, It goes flat if I had realized when my husband took the picture. Nothing is binding me at the top portion of waist band. I bend over and sit very comfortable. The front pockets fit my iPhone 15 pro plus, the back pockets are not open. Can’t use them. It’s lawfully hard to find pants with having your body sway to the right. If you have this problem I believe you will like these pants. I am 5’ 1” tall and the length of the pants is 27”.. my weight on scale 115 ibs but maybe the way I stand which I need to hold on to something. My right leg/knee hyperextend and goes back. So if you have this problem these pants are great. No problem. The material is so soft and the feels great on, it’s wonderful. The pockets lay flat and don’t pull out. With having scoliosis the pockets open up but not with these pants. There lay flat. Yay. I just got these pants on Oct 17 th 2025. I ordered size Small taking a chance. I was so surprised these were wonderful to wear and the small was good. I didn’t want to buy the medium because as I know now the band would have been loose. Causing another problem pulling the waist band up. No thank you. The length is 27”Across the hem is 11” just double for back of hem. Across the middle of pant leg is 10” would be 20” around. The waist at top, the best I can do is 10” double that. It has a curve. Hope this helps. I also wear a long brace on right leg from thigh to ankle I would need something under the straps from keeping it from going down. You might have this problem too. These are the best pants and in style with wide leg. I have not washed them yet since I just got them.

PROS: Mid rise, very moderate flare legs, not wide. The great thing is elastic waist on the back only, this allowed the drawstring more adjustable to fit my waist. The cargo pockets with a unique stretch Red knit interior pocket, I place my cell phone inside it. The side vents legs easy to size up to the right inseam. It's a true size for me, especially the adjustable waist.CONS: The front two pockets could be deeper, because they shrink little after washed. Still I can put my whole palm inside. Material 55% Cotton, 45% Polyester, good and not thin. It would be even better if there is a little % Spandex, to allow some stretchable on pants and the drawstring. plus less wrinkle after dryer. I normally took out cloths after dryer and hang it up while it's Warm to avoid iron.Wash it before size : I was thinking to size the length, but it shrink after washed and no need to.True size: I ordered Medium petite, it was a little loose and fit just right after washed. You may think it's little huge but it's not after you wash it. I am 5'3"Overall I like it. I returned two other pairs and keep this one. The mid rise and adjustable waist is my most favorite. Plus very mild flare legs, not wide.

I normally wear dickies signature but really wanted to try out a pair of joggers when I got new scrubs for a new new job. I was skeptical of all the reviews from nurses that said how well they repel their pets hair because one pet is a lot different than the dozens of patients I see in a day. But they really do keep all the hair off! I didn't reach for a lint roller or latex glove all day. They fit as expected- I'm a tiny person with a 24in waist and the xs was just a touch big, but I have this problem with all brands of scrubs. Washed with hot water and dried on high and they shrunk up to a more comfortable fit without losing shape or color.

Not the most comfortable scrub pants I have worn. I purchased the royal blue color in an X-small. I am 5'0 and 137lbs. They are a little long and slightly big for me, I needed more scrubs asap and the petite sizing was all out. I purchased a matching EDS top in the same color, it has not arrived yet but I am slightly worried that the colors will not match. I prefer for my scrubs to not drag on the floor but most of mine do because of my height. Dickies scrubs are known for holding up well.

I've been buying ribbed tanks from Old Navy for years. They're perfect for summer and layering. The rib knit is very stretchy and comfortable. I'm 5'9" with a long torso, and appreciate that the Tall length has plenty of length to tuck in without worrying about it coming untucked. I decided to get this tank from Gap to see if it is any better quality than the Old Navy ones. It turns out that the fabric content, texture, and finishings are all the same between the two tanks. The only notable difference is that the back of the Gap tank is cut quite a bit higher, and the arm holes are cut just a tiny bit lower. (The Old Navy tank in the photos is lighter because I've had it since 2017.) The Gap tank curves in slightly along the waist, whereas the Old Navy tank is cut straight down. If you're wearing these fitted, it doesn't really make a difference because of the stretch. Unless you have a strong preference about the back neckline, go with whichever one has a better price, because they're largely the same.

Finding leggings that don't look like high-waters is a constant struggle when you're 5'10", so these Zylioo flare leggings were a breath of fresh air. I ordered the XL Tall, and for once, I actually have length to spare. They reach all the way down past my ankles, which is a rare find for "tall" sizes that usually fall short. The fabric is really the standout feature here. Most yoga pants these days have that slick, shiny polyester feel, but these are 95% cotton. It makes a huge difference in how they feel against your skin—they're breathable, soft, and have a more natural look. They also have a bit of light insulation, which makes them perfect for those crisp 50 to 70-degree days. I’ve worn them for yoga, jogging on the treadmill, and even heavy weight lifting and squats, and they’ve held up beautifully. The high waistband stays put without rolling down, and those side pockets are deep enough to actually hold a phone securely while you're moving. I did want to mention that when I first unpacked them, there was a random mismatched blue thread sewn into the bottom of one leg. Luckily, I was able to just pull it out without any issues, and it didn't affect the seam at all. Even with that tiny hiccup, the quality and fit are so good that it hasn't deterred me from wanting more. They transition so easily from a workout to a casual outfit with a hoodie. I honestly love these so much I’m already planning on ordering a second pair.

These flare leggings are really soft, stretchy, and comfortable right out of the package. The cotton fabric feels smooth and breathable, and they have a nice amount of stretch without feeling flimsy. They are easy to wear for lounging, errands, or light workouts, and the side pockets are a big plus for holding a phone or small essentials. Fit wise, these definitely run very tall. I usually order tall in all of my pants. I am 5'8, but my legs are proportionate to someone closer to 6 feet, with a much shorter torso. Even with that, these leggings were so long that they bunched up at the bottom. Because of that, if you are tall but not extremely tall, you might actually be better off ordering the regular length instead of tall. That said, if you are truly tall or struggle to find leggings that are long enough, these could be a great option. The extra length means no awkward cropped look, and the flare still falls nicely when the length works for your height. Overall, these are comfortable, well made leggings with a flattering flare and useful pockets. Just pay close attention to the length before ordering. For very tall women, these are a strong choice. For moderately tall women, the regular length may actually be the better fit.

I have a 36" inseam and always have problems with getting pants that are long enough. The second problem is getting the size right when ordering on-line. I have ordered tall leggings in the past from Amazon and am pretty confident that I am a size Large--10/12. I ordered these in Extra Long and they are a perfect fit. To be honest I would probably been okay with just the "Long" but I like that there is a little extra in the length for when I pull them all the way up in the waist.The other thing I love about these pants are the side pockets. Perfect to fit your phone.These leggings are super comfortable and I would consider them a medium weight and not cheap looking or feeling at all. I washed and dried them in the dryer before wearing and there was no shrinking. I am very happy with these leggings.

Measurements (inches): 40-30-40 Regular Size: US Women's 8-10 (8's usually fit me perfectly - 10's are usually looser). I purchased a size medium (10-12 according to chart). Height: 5'7" Weight: 158 lbs I really like these scrubs. I took a chance and ordered them, even with no other reviews, and was pleasantly surprised. I ordered a medium (I usually order small in scrubs), and they do fit like my other medium scrubs. They are a bit baggy but I’m not sure if I will try ordering them in a small yet as I’d hate for them to be too tight on my thighs; plus, let’s be honest, when I clean up bodily fluids the good majority of my day the last thing I care about is looking cute. They are comfortable and not overpriced ($22 when I bought them). The color is actually pretty accurate as well and it’s very pretty, my photo doesn’t do it justice. TAKE YOUR BODY MEASUREMENTS when comparing to the size chart. I've labeled where I take my measurements. It’s a huge pet peeve of mine when reviewers just share their weight (which isn't very relevant) and include no measurements! Two people the same height & weight can look vastly different. I went with a size medium, which according to the size chart is equivalent to size's 10-12. My waist and hip measurement's fit within the size chart's 10-12 waist and hip measurements. I played it safe and went with this size but I do think a small would fit just fine on me. I definitely have extra room when worn at my waist but luckily they have a tie. Overall though, I am happy with my purchase and will be ordering more of these.

I just had a baby and I wasn’t sure what my size would be so I ordered different brands. I’m 5’5 and weight 240. I got regular and they will drag but they are soft and comfortable they are not too tight which I didn’t want because I had a C-section so I need them to be looser. If you like them tight then size down. I did a squat, bent over, kicked around and they are good to go and I trust they won’t rip or give me problems. There’s one pocket on the side and two regular pockets. I’m satisfied.

TLDR: Breathable. Thick enough that even white pants won’t show your underwear. Easy to move around in. Most comfortable scrub pants I’ve ever worn. I’ve tried 5 different kinds of white scrubs pants to wear for my nursing school graduation ceremony, and these were the winner! The material is breathable, but still thick enough to not show the color of my underwear. I can’t ever work in these since they are white, but if I had them in a different color I would wear them to work every day happily. The fabric stretches with movement very nicely. It doesn’t make my legs feel like trapped sausages every time I bend my knees or cross my legs. Out of all the different pants I’ve tried, these were technically the most expensive, but they are absolutely worth every penny. Clearly these are more expensive for a REASON, because they are such high-quality. Sizing is just as expected! I normally wear a size medium, and these fit me exactly like a size medium should. (I’m 5’8” and probably 140-150lbs)

Size 10 is a perfect 10. These wide-calfed boots have room to spare, as you can see in my video, with 14.75 inch calves. The height is shown on 5’7” build. You get a relaxed slouchy drape around the ankles. What I like most about these is the ombré type coloring at the toes. I also like the extra seam details going down center front and back, as well as across the ankle and heel sections. The bottom soles have textured gripping layers on the heel and ball of foot areas. Overall, very comfortable with a good fit.(video review included)

The suede type material is really lovely and soft to wear. I have slightly large calves and had no issue zipping these up. I love the toe shape, nice and rounded while not looking cut off or making my feet or legs appear short (I'm 5'5" for reference). The top comes up near my knee but not so tall that it digs into the backs of my knees while sitting. Heels feel solid and seem well attached. My only issue is that they are rather tight on the feet, especially around the back of the heel/ankle area which is ever so slightly uncomfortable. I'm hoping this will wear in over time as I do really like the style of the boots but it may have fit better if I had sized up. I'm between an 8-8.5 and went with size 8. The length of the toe is fine but it's just tight over the top of the foot and back in the ankle slightly. So if you have wider feet, these may not work great for you and if in doubt, go up for sure.

So if you have wider feet, these may not work great for you and if in doubt, go up for sure.

I bought Med. I’m 5’5” and 130 lbs I needed sweats that were comfortable and baggy enough to fit over a knee brace. I bought a grey pair and black pair. If I didn’t need extra room I would have been fine and in a small. They are a little bit long for my short frame but the legs have an elastic cuff so that helped. However the cuff is not so tight or big that I can’t pull it all the way up my leg. That was important factor because it didn’t want to have to take them off to pull the knee brace on. The waist elastic is comfortable and sits at the waist line. Not super high waisted but not super low. As for quality, all seams are fine and the pockets are even lined. Pockets aren’t super deep but deep enough for my cell phone. Over all the quality exceeded my expectations for the price.

These knee-length boots are surprisingly comfortable and easy to walk in. The toe is pointed, but the boots are not uncomfortable on my feet at all, not even on the toes. The heel is fairly big, but because the width of the heel is wide, they are stable and easy to walk in. The sole has some padding, and I found them comfortable to both walk and stand. They fit true to size for a medium-width foot. I am short, 5ft 2" tall, and they hit me right at the knees, so they are truly tall boots! I currently weigh 177 lbs and wear a size 14 in pants, and the boots fit over my calves comfortably. This is a big deal because in the past, I have had issues with long boots fitting my legs, so these are truly a nice, wide width to fit women with larger legs. The quality of these boots is really nice. I chose the suede style in black, and they look and feel expensive. These are nice-looking boots that will work well with skinny jeans, skirts, and leggings. Recommended!

I love the length, I have shorter legs, but I am 5'5 so the length is great, I don't need to hem them. This green is bright and saturated- I love the color so much! The waistband is just snug enough on the 3x to fit my waist flat and be flattering. These are more like a spring/summer weight, and I love all the colors they come in. Love the color and the fabric but the fit is terrible! Looks like old ladies pants! I am 5’3 small size, 130 pounds and look like a sausage in it.

First and foremost, these are extremely comfortable. I would call them mid-rise with a comfortable elastic band and smooth-to-the-touch fabric on the outside. The pockets are also nice and deep and can easily fit a phone. I would not necessarily call these fleece lined, it's more like butter soft leggings on the inside. Definitely not thick but warmer than standard spandex pants. They also run short. I am only 5'4 and they just touch the top of my foot. If you like your pants a little longer or if you are taller, these might not be the best for you. The leg is also a little wider all the way down than other straight leg pants I own. There's also a faux zipper seam on the front which is a little weird, but it gives it a more pants-like look. These could absolutely pass for work slacks if you wanted.

At 61 years old, 5'4", and 115 pounds, I have never once in my entire life been described as "tall." I'm a size 2 and have been buying "regular" length pants for 5 decades without incident. So when I saw these fleece-lined pants came in short, regular, and tall options, I confidently ordered Small-Regular (should have been Medium-Tall) At 5'4" I needed the tall length. I'm still processing this revelation. When I put them on, the front pockets flared out instead of lying flat - which tells me I needed to size up. Looking back at the actual size chart (which I should have done FIRST), I realize they provided the correct information about their product, I just needed to LOOK. But here's the thing: despite my sizing failure, I can tell these are quality pants. At the time of this listing the price is $39.99, and for that you're getting something genuinely nice. The fabric is 90% polyester and 10% elastane with a fleece lining that's different from typical fuzzy fleece - it's flatter, shorter, and feels substantial. I uploaded a photo so you can see the texture. This is the kind of fleece that looks like it'll survive countless wash cycles without pilling into those annoying little fabric balls that make everything look cheap. It feels durable and well-made. My advice: These pants are legitimately good quality. But LOOK AT THE SIZE CHART. Don't assume your normal size will work. Don't assume your height category is what you think it is. Actually measure and compare. If they fit properly, I genuinely think I would have loved these. The fabric quality alone makes them worth trying again with the right measurements. I hope this review has been for you!

I got these fleece-lined trousers for two primary reasons: their petite sizing, which is often difficult to find and prevents them from dragging on the floor, and their fleece lining. I find them to be exceptionally soft and comfortable. As someone who is 5'2", I appreciate that they do not drag. They are true to size and possess a pleasant thickness, which is precisely what I sought to avoid feeling cold. I particularly appreciate their trouser style, as it allows me to pair them with a nice shirt and appropriate footwear for a more refined appearance when going out. Other pants made from similar materials I have encountered often lack the formality required for various events, making this a truly excellent feature. These trousers are of high quality and will undoubtedly provide warmth and comfort while also offering a more polished look.

These are quite comfortable, lightweight, yet not flimsy. The waistband is comfortable, and they are easy to move in. They might be a bit too silky for yoga for some people, though I tried Tree pose with my foot resting on my inner thigh and it didn’t slip, so I think they’re fine. I can’t think of any movements that would be restricted by these pants. I really like the feel. They have slash pockets (could be deeper) and a tie/elastic waistband. I’m 5’4 and the medium is a perfect length for me, though I’m about 135 so a thinner, taller woman would also fit these and they are plenty roomy for curvier or more athletic women. I usually wear an 8/10. They are super comfortable to wear around the house and to run errands.

These Yogipace yoga pants feel and look great, though I’m more inclined to think of them as athleisure wear. I’m not a serious athlete, but I enjoy leisure as much as the next person. And no disrespect meant to the yoga workout set, but why draw the line there? These don’t look like exercise pants to me. They drape nicely and seem versatile enough to be perfect for relaxing around the house on a lazy rainy day, or to toss in my carry-on bag for a weekend getaway. They make me think of that movie called The Sisterhood of the Traveling Pants, about the pair of pants that fit anybody (or was that any body?). I think these may become my traveling pants. They’re designed to be wrinkle-free and easy-care, and they’re a sleek, classy black that will look good for everything from hitting the trail to going out for coffee or casual cocktails. I like the high-rise, slim-fit design of these pants, with an elastic drawstring waist so I can custom-fit them to my day or my activity level. The material is super-soft and lightweight, as well as comfy and uber-stretchy. That’s what 12% spandex in the fabric content will do for you. I can’t imagine these pants would ever feel or look baggy. And pockets are always a must-have for me. These have pockets that are roomy enough to hold my iPhone and then some. I’m a bit over 150 lbs with a 31” waist, and the medium fits me fine so the size chart was spot-on. But my 5’ 8”-plus height prompted me to order the Tall option with the 35” inseam, and that was a mistake. I should’ve read the guidelines more carefully and ordered the Long option instead. My bad. These are about 4 inches too long for me, but a little hemming will fix that right up. Better too long than too short, obviously. I’ve made that mistake once or twice, too. On the other hand… maybe I shouldn’t hem them just yet. These pants are stylish enough that wearing them to an upscale restaurant isn’t out of the question, in which case I may want to pair heels with them.

These high-rise, straight-leg yoga pants are amazing. I am 5'5 and ordered an XL because I was worried about them being tight. The XL fits perfectly, high-waisted with a relaxed straight leg silhouette. I probably could have gone down to a Large for a more fitted look, but I love the roomy feel of the XL for everyday wear. The fabric is soft, stretchy and lightweight yet warm enough for cool walks. The length is spot on for my height and they look cute with sneakers or boots. They have become my go-to for dog walks, lounging at home and running errands. Great quality and very versatile.

I really appreciate the fact that the brand gives multiple options to customize your fit vs just putting “small, medium, large, xl” etc. but these are pretty thin yoga leggings, not see through, and the material is sweat wicking. I personally love these a lot and the cut at the bottom is PERFECT!!! I don’t like when the ankle area of leggings are so enormous that my ankles get swallowed by the ocean of fabric, so this was perfect length for me.

I don’t like when the ankle area of leggings are so enormous that my ankles get swallowed by the ocean of fabric, so this was perfect length for me.

Happy mama, happy daughter. 🥰

I ordered these because of how comfortable they looked and the price point was pretty decent. After receiving them, they are definitely very high waisted and definitely very long. I’m only 5’3, and I would definitely have to pay to get these altered. So one negative, but I often expect length will be that way. As for comfort, they are like wearing jogger pants that are a bit more structured. Still very comfortable. For me, I do carry weight in my hips, so they do clean a little bit more there. But I feel overall they are pretty flattering. They also do feel decently warm as well. Overall, a nice pair of comfortable pants to wear casually out with sneakers or even maybe dress it up with some ballet flats and a nicer top. I probably wouldn’t wear it out to a nice dinner, but definitely out running errands during the day.

When I first opened these, I thought "Oh no, how did we get the size THAT wrong?" I double and triple checked everything, and yes, they are the size they're supposed to be. Well, they stretch, so try them on. I was skeptical as I pulled them up all the way and . . they packed in a lot of my overweight, slimming me, and ever so comfortably. Sold. But so long! It's the trend now, to have your pants puddle around your feet. I'll probably hem them up a bit but in the meantime, they look great with my two-inch heel mules. They come out of the wash beautifully. They're much lighter than regular denim, but they're still substantial. And they're soft. Back to the size; I ordered Large based on Amazon's recommendation and didn't look at the size chart, being in a hurry. As it turned out, the Large really is a bit small, though I wear it comfortably. I'm 5/5" and 160 pounds. Skip the size chart link at the top and scroll down to the seller's size chart on the listing. I'll probably get at least one more pair of these in another color.

First of all, I want to say I LOVE love love gym people as a brand. I get a bunch of their stuff for exercising and lounging and its fantastic quality. This is also fantastic quality and well-made. I had been hoping the pants would be better looking, but alas, they look like leggings painted with a jean pattern. That being said, they also FEEL exatly like leggings, which is a win in my book (even though they're fairly ugly lol). They also look EXACTLY like they do in the photo online, so if you're looking for jeggings that look somewhat like jeans, these aren't it. That's fine in my book, becuase it's correct advertising. I'd probably wear these more if they were more jean-looking, but that doesn't mean they're not perfect for someone else. For my needs, these will be used for things like long flights/airports when I still want to look a little more polished than sweatpants, and/or for when I have a long top that will cover the waist. I ordered a size medium based off the size chart (which says size 8-10 or a waist for 32-35 inches), and it fits me perfectly, even at the end of the day. The pants are very stretchy. I would suggest ordering based off your normal numeric size as opposed to the waist inches, since I'd say this is more comfortable for a 30-32 inch waist than 32-35 inch. TLDR: very comfy, very stretchy, and very accurate to the photos (for good or for bad)

These high waisted pull on jeans are really nice, very good quality, and look pretty good on, I’m just not sure they are for my body type. I am an apple shape and carry all my weight in my postpartum belly. Based on the size chart I ordered a medium, and they fit well (5’7, 150). These are so incredibly high waisted, almost to the bottom of my ribcage. Unfortunately I puff out a little below the waist band. That is not the fault of the jeans, it is my body type. But they definitely snatch you if you like that pinch waist feeling. The fabric is really nice, heavy, but not as stiff as some denim. I love the length, sometimes jeans just hit my ankle, but these go to the tops of my shoes. All the pockets are functional. I will be wearing these with something like a longer sweater or cardigan to cover the belly part. Overall these are really great pull on jeans, but women with an hourglass shape can probably pull these off better.

They are very soft and comfy and i would use them as lounge wear or around the house. They are what i would actually classify as sweatpants material unlike a lot of these other brands who advertise that. I’m 5’6” and weigh 145lbs. I got a medium but the waistband was more like a large. Good thing there is a drawstring. They are on the shorter side especially compared to other joggers. They hit me below my belly button and barely go to my ankles without a lot of room into between. Other joggers tend to be roomy and slouchier to allow for movement. The black color looked a little faded. I honestly debated on returning them because of the fit and everything I just listed previously but they are so frickin comfortable that I couldn’t say no. I washed them and literally started wearing them the next day. Zero regrets.

These pants are very soft. Not too thick or thin. If you’re hot natured you can likely wear these outside in 30-50 degree weather. If you’re cold natured maybe 50-60 degrees. I am 5’2”, 115 lbs. The first pictures you see are the pants on me before washing. As you can see, they were just slightly bunched at the ankle. After washing in warm water and drying on cottons, they shrunk just a little bit as you can see from the pictures on the left. Both lengths are fine with me. They are a medium blue color with a draw string and cuffs at the ankles. I got these on sale for $10 which I think was a steal! I am very impressed with the quality and have worn them several times already for walking outdoors and once to the gym for lifting. I highly recommend and plan to get another pair soon.

Measurements for XS in inches: -Waist/width for XS: 14”-14.5” Measurements for S/Small in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Ankle for S: 4.5" -Front rise for S: 10.5” -Waist/width for S: 15"-15.5" -Inseam for S: 28" -Overall length for S: 38" Measurements for S/Small in inches (AFTER laundry): -Ankle for S: 4.5" -Front rise for S: 10” -Waist/width for S: 15"-15.5” -Inseam for S: 26" -Overall length for S: 35" Measurements for M/Medium in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Ankle for M: 5" -Front rise for M: 10.5” -Waist/width for M: 17"-17.5" -Inseam for M: 28.5" -Overall length for M: 39" Measurements for L/Large in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Front rise for L: 11" -Waist/width for L: 18"-18.5" -Inseam for L: 27" -Overall length for L: 38" ----------- **Original Review**: If I was going for mid to high-rise then I would pick XS. The overall size fits my body better (4’11”, 95 lbs). However, I prefer a loose fit since I have a sensitive (pudgy) belly so I kept size Small. It looks kind of baggy on me but that’s ok. I rather breathe easily. I hide that area with a big t-shirt. I prefer all my pants/jeans/panties to be low-waist, which is hard to find as we are no longer in the low-rise trend. Size S sits about 2 inches below my belly button while XS sits right below my belly button. I think these joggers are better suited for apple shaped bodies since it’s wide at the top (waist) and slimmer/tighter in the knees and calves. Works for me! Too many pants these days are narrow in the waist so I was relieved and surprised that this wasn’t. The inner fleece sheds as I wear it throughout the day. I find fuzz and lint on the floor and stuck around the ankles. It’s not so bad. I have experienced much worse. It also pills a little on the outside. Pockets are decent. Not too shallow, but not so deep either. I would say this jogger is a medium weight/thickness. I washed them in cold water and dried in low heat. They did not shrink (read update/edit below). I bought them on sale so I’m not mad at the imperfections. Overall, I am satisfied with the jogger. Lots of prints/patterns and colors to choose from. ----------- **UPDATE/EDIT (Oct 2022)**: I recently bought another jogger and when I tried it on new (before washing/drying) I noticed it's bigger than my old joggers. Initially I thought they redesigned or updated the model, but nope they shrink. It’s actually ok with me since I am petite. It fits me better after laundry. Before drying: it had a longer inseam (+2"), longer front rise (+0.5"), longer overall length (+3"), and wider/looser throughout the thighs and legs. I recommend using cold water and low heat or line dry. I also want to point out that the Charcoal Heather came with a dark gray drawstring, which looks better in my opinion. The model is wearing a white colored drawstring. ---------------- *UPDATE/EDIT* (Nov 2024): I noticed that the cheetah and zebra joggers have a wider/looser waist (about 1 inch) compared to the solid colors. I don't see those animal printed joggers listed anymore though. They must have been discontinued. Now it makes me wonder which joggers did I use to measure. This is frustrating when they do not design or manufacture it consistently and caused me to give out confusing measurements. I apologize. ——————- *UPDATE/EDIT* (Nov 2024): I just bought two more colors and they’re wider than usual in the waist. Their lack of quality control is driving me crazy. Are they attaching wrong size tags on the joggers or changed manufacturers? I had the same experience with their other thinner joggers without drawstring. I love the joggers though. Just need to figure out the right size!

Before drying: it had a longer inseam (+2"), longer front rise (+0.5"), longer overall length (+3"), and wider/looser throughout the thighs and legs. I recommend using cold water and low heat or line dry. I also want to point out that the Charcoal Heather came with a dark gray drawstring, which looks better in my opinion. The model is wearing a white colored drawstring. ---------------- *UPDATE/EDIT* (Nov 2024): I noticed that the cheetah and zebra joggers have a wider/looser waist (about 1 inch) compared to the solid colors. I don't see those animal printed joggers listed anymore though. They must have been discontinued. Now it makes me wonder which joggers did I use to measure. This is frustrating when they do not design or manufacture it consistently and caused me to give out confusing measurements. I apologize. ——————- *UPDATE/EDIT* (Nov 2024): I just bought two more colors and they’re wider than usual in the waist. Their lack of quality control is driving me crazy. Are they attaching wrong size tags on the joggers or changed manufacturers? I had the same experience with their other thinner joggers without drawstring. I love the joggers though. Just need to figure out the right size!

Measurements for XS in inches: -Waist/width for XS: 14”-14.5” Measurements for S/Small in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Ankle for S: 4.5" -Front rise for S: 10.5” -Waist/width for S: 15"-15.5" -Inseam for S: 28" -Overall length for S: 38" Measurements for S/Small in inches (AFTER laundry): -Ankle for S: 4.5" -Front rise for S: 10” -Waist/width for S: 15"-15.5” -Inseam for S: 26" -Overall length for S: 35" Measurements for M/Medium in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Ankle for M: 5" -Front rise for M: 10.5” -Waist/width for M: 17"-17.5" -Inseam for M: 28.5" -Overall length for M: 39" Measurements for L/Large in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Front rise for L: 11" -Waist/width for L: 18"-18.5" -Inseam for L: 27" -Overall length for L: 38" ----------- **Original Review**: If I was going for mid to high-rise then I would pick XS. The overall size fits my body better (4’11”, 95 lbs). However, I prefer a loose fit since I have a sensitive (pudgy) belly so I kept size Small. It looks kind of baggy on me but that’s ok. I rather breathe easily. I hide that area with a big t-shirt. I prefer all my pants/jeans/panties to be low-waist, which is hard to find as we are no longer in the low-rise trend. Size S sits about 2 inches below my belly button while XS sits right below my belly button. I think these joggers are better suited for apple shaped bodies since it’s wide at the top (waist) and slimmer/tighter in the knees and calves. Works for me! Too many pants these days are narrow in the waist so I was relieved and surprised that this wasn’t. The inner fleece sheds as I wear it throughout the day. I find fuzz and lint on the floor and stuck around the ankles. It’s not so bad. I have experienced much worse. It also pills a little on the outside. Pockets are decent. Not too shallow, but not so deep either. I would say this jogger is a medium weight/thickness. I washed them in cold water and dried in low heat. They did not shrink (read update/edit below). For $10, I’ll give it a 4-star review. The 1-star deduction is due to the color difference between these black sweatpants and my other black sweatshirts (see 1st pic). The pants are like a ‘cheap’ version of black…maybe? Not sure. But they are comfortable, light and soft. Many reviewers said they were very baggy but I am 5’1 and about 122 lbs and the small worked for me and that’s my usual size. If they shrink in the wash, however, they might become leggings. :) And I am fairly short in stature so if these pants fit me well in length, then they might be too short for taller people. It’s rare to find sweatpants that look like they were made for short people. And for that, I’ll give it 4-stars. Overall, I’m satisfied and will keep them. If they had been more expensive, I would be returning them because of the weaker color tone.

First off, I will say do NOT follow Amazon’s size recommendations on this one. It told me to originally order a 2x when I needed a Large. I’m about 5’5”, 190 and carry most of my weight in my tummy area. I was worried about them shrinking as many reviews suggested but I just washed them on warm and dried them completely on medium and if they shrunk at all I can’t tell. They are supposed to be a tighter fitting jogger which I love because they are warmer that way and living in Minnesota I will likely live in these during the winter months. I immediately bought 2 more pair after washing these to make sure I knew what size I needed. So yeah, don’t get crazy and order 2 sizes up, you may not need to. The length is perfect for my height too! They go to my ankles yet are not too long. Just perfect to tuck into snow boots! I also got all 3 pair on sale for about $10 each which is a steal for this quality. And yes, they have pockets! Yay! Perfect sweats. Even my 13 yr old daughter said they were cute which is saying something since I’m old and usually out of style. lol I highly recommend these. I also love the Amazon Essentials hoodies! They’re wonderful!

I originally ordered this Lee Lux Comforte in the Navy in size 6. Amazon insisted 6 was the size for me. According to the size chart, it was close. I’m 5‘6“ tall. I weigh 135 to 140, waist is 29. hips are 38. I am also a little bit long-waisted. The waist of the 6 was OK. It was firm with not much give. However, the 6 was quite snug through the hips and thighs, and it made the pockets gap. I opted to return the 6 because these will primarily be for work and they were just slightly too small everywhere. I ordered the same slacks in size 8. The waist is still OK in the 8 a little bigger and a little looser, but I’ll still be able to wear them after Thanksgiving dinner. There is more room in the hips, but they still hug the top of the hip nicely before they fall to the wide leg. The leg in the 8 is loose at on my thighs, but the 6 was tight on my thighs. They are a bit longer. Unfortunately, they still make a funny wrinkle at the groin. This happens to me sometimes because I am slightly long-waisted. Because these are on sale for $16 and I really need navy slacks, I’m going to keep the 8. For anyone with a waist over 29, or hips more than 36, or generous thighs, or a long-waist, I think the 8 is a better choice. The fabric is not thin. It is a nice weight and it will be good for work. It might be warm on hot days.

Okay, listen up fellow Amazonians: I am 6'2" and 210 lbs, and these pants are so close to being long enough it’s almost disrespectful. Like… they ALMOST understood the assignment. Stupid close. Close enough that I’m willing to forgive them because everything else about them is fantastic. They’re thick but breathable (in a good, quality-fabric way). They’re wrinkle resistant, which is great because ironing is a chore I absolutely refuse to participate in. They have the perfect wide leg that makes it look like I’m purposefully elegant instead of floundering to both have pants long enough that are also stylish. Thy are pretty sturdy and not very stretchy so buy your actual size. And the navy color? Chef’s kiss. Honestly, if Lee ever decides to add two more inches of length, I will personally write them a thank-you letter and bake them cookies. Until then, these are the best “not flood pants” I’ve ever found. Would buy again. Already did. In black. Same review but you know, I'd say black instead of navy. Duh.

I got these to wear to work, I work at the post office and so I got the navy blue color. Based off of the reviews I read about shrinking after washing, I went on ahead and bought the long and I am so glad I did. I am 5 foot 4 and I normally wear a regular length or a short. Other than that they are fantastic they have held up really well and I love how thick the fabric is but they are so soft and comfortable it feels like I'm wearing pajamas almost. I don’t even know where to start. After searching HIGH AND LOW, ordering nearly 10 different pants, multiple trips to UPS to return, I found the ONE. I am 5’7, ~158lbs. These fit like a glove. They are so soft, flattering, and so so so comfortable. The stretch is *chefs kiss*. I had originally ordered the long size & two pockets option but it actually turned out to be too long and the front was sewn weird, so I reordered the regular with 4 pockets and it’s perfect for me (flats girly) and the front pockets are perfect. These feel perfect for work. They’re simple but still look classy and they’re not heavy or weigh you down. Feels like a second skin. Although pricier than I’m used to, I’d have to say it’s worth it, absolutely no regrets and I will be ordering more colors once I wash these and see how they hold up. Will update if something goes awry lol!

The only reason why I returned is because the 31inch inseam were not included in the Amazon prime deals or I would have def exchanged. I will say I am usually a small in everything, they were slightly loose in the waist band they were not falling down so still 5 stars so comfy. You could go from the office to Pilates in these. Also I got the ones with the front pockets. For those wondering, when you sit down it does NOT do the weird thing wear the pockets pop out.

These are perfect for an office that requires business casual attire if you really don’t enjoy wearing stuffy and uncomfortable dress pants! My favorite features are the small details that make them truly appear like business pants: the belt loops, the pockets, the non-functioning button on the front, and the faux zipper. It really does feel like I get to wear yoga pants to work without being under-dressed. The fabric is stretchy, comfortable, reasonably thick (you can’t see through them), and the waistband is not too tight at all (no pinching)! I love that Yogipace offers different inseam lengths. Being a shorter woman, I get tired of shopping for hard-to-find petite lengths or hemming regular length pants. Ordering the inseam length I needed really saved me from the hassle. Overall, these pants are pretty great quality for the price. I will definitely be purchasing more of these! My measurements are as follows: 34.5” waist, 42” hip, 23.5” thigh, and my height is 5’2”. I would say these pants fit true to size. For reference, I bought the Medium size with the 29” inseam. Without my ankle boots on, the hem of the pants sits right on top of my foot. They don’t bunch at the bottom, so I could easily wear the pants with ballet flats or loafers as well.

For reference, I ordered an XL/31" inseam based on the sizing chart - I can't confirm if they're actually XL since the tag looks like it was chewed away or something. The pants are very comfortable and fit well, they are just not made that well. As others have noted, they're covered in lint/hair, so keep a lint roller handy. I loved that this has front and back pockets, but the front pockets weren't sewn properly so there are holes in them. I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

These jeans fit me perfectly, and I have always loved a button fly jean. They are soft and comfortable, and the long length drapes my foot but doesn't drag the floor. (I'm 5'10", 34 inch inseam). I've washed them once, and the inseam is still long enough. However, these DEFINITELY seem like bell bottoms to me. The leg opening covers my entire size 11 foot, and the pant legs flap against each other when I walk. Not a deal breaker because they're so comfortable, but I'm debating whether to take these in to have them tapered slightly so they look more like a flare or even a bootcut. Alterations would still worth the cost to me, given that these were not expensive to begin with, and I love the fit otherwise.

Finally, a nice quality cargo pant for tall/curvy women! If you have been looking for a good cargo pant, look no further! The high waist sits just above my belly button, and the inseam is a great length for me- I am 5’9”, regular inseam 31-32, ordered a size 14 in black. The material feels sturdy while not being too heavy. I wanted something mostly made out of cotton for durability, so this pant was a great choice for the price. Other cargo pants just did not have all the criteria I was looking for, but this one did! The pockets are all functional, and they lay flat without sticking out awkwardly. The stitching is neatly done and seems to be good quality. I like the straight leg silhouette, it’s flattering on a fuller figure. You can easily tuck a shirt in if you wear these pants for work. I am wearing a belt in these photos, although it’s not necessary to wear a belt with these pants. They fit true to size and are comfortable. They do pick up animal hair pretty easily, but I can live with that. If they hold up good after washing them, I will definitely order in some more colors.

At first I didn’t like the look of these out of the bag, with the half-elastic waistband and slightly stiff cotton. However, on? They look cute and they feel great. I’m 5’5” and the length is perfect. Without shoes they skim the floor and with shoes they fall just over the laces. I tried them on after I washed them, to see if they shrink. I used cold water but imagine they could/will shrink over time so I air dry (since right now the fit is perfect.) I normally wear a medium top and a 6 or 8 bottom. I ordered the 6 and they fit just right. Pockets are placed well and are actually useful (not just for show.) My iPhone fits easily in the two larger front/side pockets. Back pockets are a bit smaller than typical jeans’. The waist is very comfortable and from the waistband down to the tummy, it’s slightly fitted and supportive. From there down it’s loose and wide legged—overall very flattering, even for my height. My only complaint is that it has a slight sheen to it and the cotton isn’t soft, it’s stiff and can make a bit of noise while I’m walking. Just wish it was softer and a little thicker. But since it’s thinner, it makes for a great pant for Spring or mild days.

I’m 5’6 and about 120lbs, 26” waist and 36” hips, I have a 28” inseam. I’m normally a size 2 but ordered the recommended size 4. These pants are a little looser on my waist then I like, I probably could’ve gone with a size 2. This fabric is kind of a thinner slightly stretchy twill. It feels fairly durable but also lighter weight. The cargo pockets have snaps, The rear pockets have buttons. The backside of the waistband has elastic, which kind of makes me feel like a four year old. The legs are plenty long. The stitching is mediocre at best, but good enough. They’ll work fine for being work pants as I intended it to be.

Height: 5’2, weight 110 lbs, waist 27.5”, Hips 35”, inseam 29, ordered XS, 29 inseam, color Navy Blue. Let me start by saying that the main reason for the 1-star rating is the condition in which I received these pants—they were FULL of dog or cat hair. While I do love animals, when I pay for a "new" item, the least I expect is for the item to be clean. I understand that some people with pets may try on items and return them, but I believe it's the company's responsibility to inspect returned items before sending them to another buyer. The condition I received these pants in is simply unacceptable. Putting that aside, I'd have given these pants 3 stars. Fabric: I have purchased 5 different colors of pants from this same brand, but the ones with bands around the waist area. The fabric is very comfortable and flattering, and I wear them almost every day for my small business. The fabric of these ones is the same. Size: I'm 5'1" and weigh 110 lbs. I ordered an XS with a 29 inseam, the same size I ordered in the other pants. The waist area is a bit tight, but not too bad. Considering only the waist, I might have ordered a size S instead. However, the legs are noticeably wider than the other pairs I own, about 1 to 1.5 inches wider, which I don't really like. The 29-inch length is perfect, as it's the same length as the other pants, and it looks equally good. Color: The navy blue color is very pretty. In general, I like this brand because the pants are comfortable and can be dressed up or down. However, I'm extremely disappointed with receiving pants covered in hair and not being entirely satisfied with the style. I'm not sure if I would specifically recommend these pants to friends or family.

These are so amazing and great quality- I want to keep them so bad, but the tiny elastic waistband (thickness of a hair tie) at the very top is the most annoying thing in the world. Because of the high waist, the top band hits just below the ribs, causing an unnecessary fitted feeling in a sensitive area, and I’m reminded of it every time I breathe. For some bodies it may serve a purpose, but these fit absolutely perfectly without it, and the drawstring is there to control how tight of a fit you want. If they were to remove that band or redesign, I would buy them in every color and wear every day during this New England winter! I tried a few size/length combos but the 28” Small worked best for me (5’6, 130 lbs).

I found these high-waist bootcut dress pants to be reasonably true-to-size (ordered medium), but I'm relatively short (5' 4") and the legs are a tad long, so I'll either have to wear at least a couple inches of heels with them or hem the cuffs. The fabric is reasonably thick and the waist elastic quite strong. Overall, they're quite comfortable and have a smooth look, with the bootcut aspect providing some nice flare. Do be aware that these slacks do not have pockets of any kind. I think they'll go reasonably well with a blouse and blazer combination, and the black color goes with almost anything. If I had my way though, I'd ditch the decorative metal thingy on the front and add some pockets.

I am 5ft and 2 and a half inches tall and 165 pounds in the end of my second trimester the Large petite size fit me perfectly. I was so pleased with this pair of pants I will definitely be purchasing the other colors and highly recommend them. The inseam fit perfectly and the belly panel was so comfortable and stretchy. The seam where the waist and belly panel meet is also not too tight or uncomfortable which I enjoy because I am a repeat c-section mom and it can hurt when pants are tight in the area where my incision scar is. These pants will also be very comfortable during my recovery after birth. I have not had an issue with washing them. The pockets also were able to fit my iPhone with the case on in them and they are very stretchy as well.

The color is very nice, the fit was great however I do feel like they were on the short side. I was planning to wear a wedge sandal and they looked bad, would have needed to wear a flat sandal, so if you rover 5'5" or taller these may be short on you, for the under 5'5" girlies these will probably work great. I am sadly returning these though because they look like pajama/lounge pants, they are a nice thick ribbed cotton material and they just don't have that dressy resort wear feel to them, even with a nice top and shoe. These pants are very comfy and flowy. I have the black and it's not see-through. They are high-waisted and have pockets. I get more compliments on these pants. I can dress up or down. The pockets are a delight. Pretty true to size. I am 5'4" 158 and a large are flowy. I would love more colors.

You’ll want to live in these palazzo pants in hot weather. They are a blend of viscose and linen with the best features of each fabric. They are so lightweight and skin-friendly that you don’t feel the material next to your skin. The best part is that they don’t wrinkle nearly as much as pure linen, even though they look identical to linen. They feature a drawstring waist, side pockets, and wide legs. I’m on the shorter side, and these are a little long for me (inseam 28 1/2 inches), but I’m wearing them with platform sandals. For the best fit, I recommend sizing down. Washing instructions are simple: machine wash and hang to dry.

These boots have become my favorite! I just got these this past month and I was worried about the burgundy color being to bright or looking cheap. But these definitely got the approval of many when I wear them out. I was able dance in them for some time and didn't end up with any blisters. They feel well made and not too snug nor too loose. The heel isn't uncomfortable and just the right height. It sits right under my knee with me being 5'5. The price point was perfect for these, I have seen higher price points at major fashion stores and these honestly look like they could pass for the same brand. You honestly won't regret these! Makes for the perfect fall boot!

Comfortable and true to size! Snagged these for two back to back get togethers over the weekend and loved them! I really like the block heel for longer wear and they aren’t too tall for the taller gals. For reference, I am 5’9”, shoe size 10.

I needed a pair of black pants for a business-casual look and chose these because the fabric seemed low-maintenance. I’m 5'8", 153 lbs, and usually wear a medium tall. These run shorter than most talls, the description says the inseam is 31.5" and that was accurate. I bought them knowing I’d wear them with flats so that worked out fine. The pants look great on and although they arrived pretty wrinkled, the wrinkles relaxed once I put them on..being black helped hide them too. My only concern is the inside seams, they’re not finished very well, with loose threads and some fraying that makes them feel cheaply made. I’m not sure how durable they’ll be long-term but I don’t plan to wear them constantly. Overall they fit nicely, just feel slightly overpriced for the quality.

So sadly not a great choice for me, but for those who’re still interested in them they’re very comfortable and stretchy. I love these leggings. They aren’t see thru! They fit comfortable. I’m 5’1 and weight 145 lbs, I was hesitant to buy the small/medium but they fit amazing. They are also stretchy too. Color and fabric material are the best. I had to reorder a few more since it’s hard to find good leggings with good fabric.

Took these to New York with me and they were perfect under my jeans, tights and anything I needed some warmth for! They aren’t super thick which I really loved, you can almost see my tattoos through them. I added them under my thermal tights that weren’t super warm and it was amazing. These definitely are layering thermals so don’t expect to get all your warmth from them but they do the job in 30 degree weather. I definitely could’ve sized down especially since they loosen up quite a bit as the day goes on but you never know with thick thighs. Im 5’9 and they were pretty high waisted sometimes they even stretched up to my boobs lol, which I loved because I hate when the waistband presses on the middle of my stomach.

I'm 5'1" 106 pounds and went with my normal small/med which was true to size and almost a perfect fit. There was a few areas that were loose but areas i can work with. For the price these are a really good deal. They are made of a thinner quality very soft and stretchy material. They are slightly see through but not bad. Of course the thicker you are and the more the material stretches the more see through they will be. The color in the ad is pretty close to the actual color. Definitely recommend Update: I purchased these in the wine red as well which didn't fit as well as the beige color. For some reason the fit was alot looser and bunched in the crotch area. Not sure if I'm going to return these or not.

I was thrilled to find pants long enough and sensory friendly but the 34” in the light blue was too long. They didn’t have light blue in a 32” large so I ended up buying 32” in a darker wash and while it was the perfect length, the fit was so much baggier and not as soft. Both were Larges. I wanted a lighter wash but they didn’t have a light blue in a 32” so I bought the plain Blue and it wasn’t even 32” it was shorter but labeled 32” and looked like high waders on me. Wthhhh??! I have ordered 3 more in various washes and lengths to see if I hit some sort of jackpot since in theory these would be great. But so far it looks like I’ll be returning them all. Ughhh. On the quest again. Update: I ordered a Medium in the darker blue wash 32 inch inseam (even labeled as such), and while that one fits better, it’s SHORT! It’s 30” long. Omggggg. It’s literally a gamble what you’re going to get and after ordering like 6 pairs in various sizes and lengths, none were as advertised and my “Goldilocks” pair. Such a bummer as what fits well isn’t long enough or is too long and what doesn’t fit well is the right length. Crappy brand.

For reference I am 6’0 tall with 28” waist and 39” hips and I ordered these pants in a 28x34 light wash. I love the 34” inseam length on these pants, but they were a little too big in the waist and hips for me. I ordered the light wash color and the what I got is a very saturated vibrant blue it looks absolutely nothing like the color in the picture. I am going to exchange for a smaller size and hope that maybe they just sent me the wrong color, but if not I’m just going to return them.

Hourglass/Pear approved! I’ve been searching for work pants for-ev-er and finally found a pair that fits my shape. I’m 5’7, 170lbs with wider hips and a smaller waist. These fit like a glove! They’ll be so useful in Wisconsin winters too. The length is perfect for me. I did order a size Large Tall. My natural waist sits pretty high so the extra length is necessary. There is a tight band around the very top of the pants that doesn’t stretch, but once the pants are on, they aren’t sliding down because of it! I can sit down and stand up without having to readjust my pants. The material is a tad shiny but not AS shiny as typical workout pants. Pockets are functional and hold my iPhone 14 without issue. Back pockets are NOT functional. Very stretchy but will hold their shape. NO tummy compression but the wide band helps a little. Will definitely order another pair for work.

These pants are a great-quality stretchy material that feels good. As you can see in the close-up picture, it’s got a diagonal pattern more similar to jeans rather than the completely smooth knit I’ve seen in other professional yoga pants I own. But it’s still fine enough that the texture isn’t all that noticeable. I like the shape of these pants overall, BUT they tend to sag after just the first few minutes of wearing them, so I have to hike them up a little every so often. (Maybe if I had gotten a smaller size that would be less of an issue, but they aren't loose anywhere, so I was hesitant to size down.) -I’m 5’5”, and the 31” inseam falls below my ankle but doesn’t drag the floor. -It’s fairly high-waisted (comes to half an inch under my belly button). -I’m 142lbs, and the Large is a pretty good fit for me. Definitely would stretch for a few more pounds, and a medium might have actually worked better, but I’m fairly happy with the fit. Overall, a nice pair of work pants!

These pants exceeded my expectations. I love that the burgundy color is so rich, and it pairs beautifully with a lot of pieces I already own. They are really lightweight which is nice, so they’re more appropriate to wear in the Spring/Summer. So far, I’ve lounged in them at home and they are extremely comfortable. The side stripes give a sporty aesthetic, that also makes them great for my workout days. I’m 5’10” and these pants fit well, so tall-girl approved! I’m 150lbs 28 waist/40 hips and ordered the medium for a slightly oversized look and they fit my waist and hips comfortably, so I would say they are true to size. The slits at the bottom were also a nice detail and makes the pants more breathable. Overall, I’m absolutely in love with these pants and would love to grab other colors.

These pants have been my go-to for years. I have to say I think they run slightly large, as my measurements are an inch or two over the size listing and the pants are comfortably loose. I just bought a 16 and love the way they fit, but I definitely could have opted for the 14 if I wanted a more form-fitting look. I’m 5’3” and prefer to shorten the length about 1 inch, but I have worn them with the 29” short inseam as is, and they look okay that way too (just pool a little at the ankle). They machine wash and dry just fine, but I don’t use powdered detergent or oxiclean in the wash as they often collect some of it on their surface, requiring them to be wiped down with a damp cloth to remove it. I’ve seen other reviews complaining of white residue after washing. I’ve found that this can be avoided by using only liquid detergent. I love these pants and highly recommend them.

I love pull-on pants (no buttons/zippers) and have been on the hunt for a great straight leg pair. I was initially turned off by how they looked on the models but took a chance anyway & I'm so glad I did! The quality is great: very well-made, comfy pants with a nice, stretchy material that can be worn in all seasons, ideal length for me (I'm 5'5") & the waist band has a thickness to it that gives you some tummy control. I immediately ordered more and now have these in black, gray, plaid (see video) & just got the dark brown ones. Some pull-on pants can look cheap & these definitely don't- VERY happy with this purchase!

This brand specializes in longer lengths for tall people. I am 5'8" 132lbs. I ordered size M-Tall which was the 32in length. The length is absolutely perfect. The leg width and slight taper is very flattering, and the pants are well made and comfortable. I usually get a size small but based on the size chart, I ordered medium and I'm glad I did because the waist band is snug and fits like a small typically would feel on me, so check the size chart and you may also want to size up. The only thing I don't love about the pants is the cargo pockets with the big flap at the top. They add bulk to the legs and I don't find them flattering. I'd prefer no pocket or a flush, smooth zipper on top instead of the flap. In my experience, after washing, the flaps always look crinkled, crooked, and stick out more. Overall, I like the length and comfort of these pants and will look at this brand to find other styles with 32" length.

Tall ladies…you’re gonna love these pants! Why is it so hard for us tall gals, or just gals with a long inseam, to find pants that reach the foot instead of above the ankle? If you want a pair of cotton, yes cotton (another hard find nowadays), sweatpants these are awesome. Yes, a bit too long for me before washing but I expected that being about a 35 inch inseam myself. But I wanted to dry them and knowing cotton with usually shrink in the dryer, I went for it. And the fit is perfect a tad longer than floor length which is how I wear my pants. Keep them out of the dryer and you will have 36 inch inseam with no shrinkage from washing in cold water. The quality is exceptional with no loose threads and all seams sewn well. I appreciate the elastic waistband as it offers the wearer to not use the drawstring. The waistband feels soft against the skin and all the cargo pockets lie flat even after putting in the dryer. The fabric has good weight without being heavyweight - meaning the fabric is warm without the weight. The side pockets also lie along your silhouette without flaring out (quite unattractive!) and have good depth. I love these pants, and I rarely say such a thing but these are really high quality, well made athletic pants that can be worn out for errands or outdoors doing yard work or adventuring into the woods. Great product and well priced!

Tall ladies…you’re gonna love these pants! Why is it so hard for us tall gals, or just gals with a long inseam, to find pants that reach the foot instead of above the ankle? If you want a pair of cotton, yes cotton (another hard find nowadays), sweatpants these are awesome. Yes, a bit too long for me before washing but I expected that being about a 35 inch inseam myself. But I wanted to dry them and knowing cotton with usually shrink in the dryer, I went for it. And the fit is perfect a tad longer than floor length which is how I wear my pants. Keep them out of the dryer and you will have 36 inch inseam with no shrinkage from washing in cold water. The quality is exceptional with no loose threads and all seams sewn well. I appreciate the elastic waistband as it offers the wearer to not use the drawstring. The waistband feels soft against the skin and all the cargo pockets lie flat even after putting in the dryer. The fabric has good weight without being heavyweight - meaning the fabric is warm without the weight. The side pockets also lie along your silhouette without flaring out (quite unattractive!) and have good depth. I love these pants, and I rarely say such a thing but these are really high quality, well made athletic pants that can be worn out for errands or outdoors doing yard work or adventuring into the woods. Great product and well priced!

As a 5'9" woman with a 27" waist and 37" hips, finding joggers that don’t awkwardly hover above my ankles has been a struggle. I ordered the Small Tall (32" inseam) and—cue the happy dance—the fit is spot-on. Not only do they hit the perfect length, but they also hug in all the right places without feeling restrictive. 🧵 Fabric & Feel: When I first pulled them out of the bag, I worried they might be too heavy. But once I slipped them on, I totally changed my tune. The cotton-poly blend is soft, breathable, and surprisingly cozy—ideal for cooler months. 🎒 Pockets Galore: The cargo pockets are generously sized, which might feel a bit bold for some, but they’re super practical. I’ve stashed my phone, keys, and even a small snack bar in them while out walking. Whether you're hiking, running errands, or just lounging, these pants are ready for action. 👟 Styling Ideas: I plan to wear them with a fitted long-sleeve tee for a sporty vibe and also will wear a denim jacket and white sneakers for casual errands. Another option I see me wearing them with a slim turtleneck and ankle boots for a cozy fall look. 🎁 Final Thoughts: These joggers are perfect for tall women. They’re functional, flattering, and versatile enough to wear from morning yoga to evening Netflix. If you’ve been let down by “tall” pants that still fall short—these get it right.

At last, we tall women can have pants that fit! These sweatpants with pockets in a "L tall" are perfect for my 5' 10" 130 pound body. They are a heavy weight, so I won't be wearing them in summer, but for winter with a warm sweatshirt they will be perfect. The 2 pockets are pretty large, large enough for a cell phone or wallet, but not really deep. There are no back pockets. The black color is slimming and practical. They are not lined with fleece but are plenty thick and warm. They are 89% cotton and 11% polyester. And the length is a real treat, especially while wearing clunky athletic shoes. These would be perfect to wear on a long flight or road trip. The drawstring waist is for thin waists. Overall, these are great sweatpants. I include 2 photos.

Finding joggers with a long enough inseam has always been a struggle, but these Zylioo pants are a game-changer! The 34" inseam is absolutely perfect—they're finally long enough without being baggy or bulky. The material is so soft and comfortable, making them great for everything from a workout to just lounging around the house. The elastic waist and pockets are also super functional. These are the perfect casual pants for tall women, and I couldn't be happier with the fit and quality. I'm definitely ordering another pair!

I dont wear sweat pants often so im not really picky about them. Recently I had surgery and needed some easy lounging pants so ordered these. I am 5'8" so having the extra length option is great! Overall they are a good quality material and fit true to size. The one thing that bothered me was the cargo pockets on each leg because the pocket on the left leg was double stitched and lays nicely while the one on the right side didnt get double stitched so it does lay nice against the leg. I think it was an error in manufacturing and hope that I received a flawed pair. The elastic waist fits well and has a nice stretch to it as well as a strings that tighten the waistband if needed. Overall they are a decent pair of sweats but the flawed cargo pockets bug me asthetically.

The size medium in these Zylioo joggers will fit a UK10/12 and are a comfortable relaxed fit. The elasticated drawstring waistband is high rise. The legs straight cut leg with non elasticated ankles which a prefer. I’m 5’6” and the tall 32’ seam option is ok for me. A little bit longer than I usually wear but fine. If you are 5’7” I think they would be the perfect length. The are a medium weight fabric which is soft and smooth on the outside. On the inside there is no lining just the mesh feel backing of the top layer. Not the softest of linings. The side pockets are a good depth and the cargo style leg pockets are useful and add a bit of style to the joggers. The fabric thickness makes them a good all year round pair of joggers which I find very comfortable. The grey colour option is a pale grey that is well resin the product photos. Overall the quality of the fabric and the manufacturing is good. At the time of ordering they were priced at £32.99 which although I like them I feel is a bit expensive.

These are great casual pants for hanging out around the house. They almost remind me of scrubs. I like the straight leg styling but with the ability to tighten the legs at the ankle. The drawstring is a working string so can easily tighten the waistline. I also like that although they look like they are ribbed, it is more of a pattern on the fabric. The fabric itself is flat and thin. They are VERY thin, though. It almost feels like a tensel fabric. Which could be nice on hotter days? Just know they don't provide any support and may show imperfections. I really love this brown color! It is rich and dark and provides a great break from black. FIT: True to size. I'm 5'6", 138 lbs and size 4 wearing a small.

The 35 inch inseam is so perfect for tall girlies like me. I wish I had sized down though. In the picture I’m wearing size medium. They slip down since they are too big. Being 4'11" and 23 weeks pregnant, finding comfortable pants can be a real challenge. These leggings in a size small fit for the most part! The fabric is soft, and I really appreciate the pockets (so handy for my phone). The belly support is great – it feels gentle but holds things in place nicely. My only complaint is the length. As expected, they're quite long on me, so I'll need to have them hemmed. But honestly, at my height, that's pretty much a given with most pants Overall, I'm happy with these leggings and would recommend them to other petite mamas-to-be.

These lightweight pants are comfortable and well made. The polyester/viscose/elastane creates a nice fabric with just enough stretch. The front pleats add a detailed touch, and the pockets are deep. As promised, the legs are wide. While flat, they measure 12” across. One note: The back has elastic across it. That is not shown in the photos. While I appreciate the flexibility that this offers, it can be unflattering. These pants are advertised for tall ladies. I’m 5’11” and they are almost long enough for me. Fortunately, the hem appears to be almost two inches long. I plan to let these out. My usual size is 12-14 and the XL fits perfectly.

Overall, I feel glad that I got these pants. They are lightweight, a beautiful blue color, the right cut on the waist, and match the upscale casual spring and summer style I'm looking for. I was glad that these pants were given sizeable pockets (thank you!) and even more surprised and delighted that there are actually about 6 inch slits at the outside bottoms of each pant leg, exposing the bottom of your calf and ankle. This was a nice touch that makes them more flowy and almost flare like when walking, vs. rigid straight leg. The two downsides I have: 1) They are slightly transparent in certain light, beware, as you can see in the photo- I hope that helps. 2) The other "issue" which is more of a personal issue is the length of the pant leg. I would prefer these to be longer. I would rather them almost drag on the floor when wearing flats and then be a bit highwater with heels. But instead they are at my ankle when standing, above the ankle when sitting, and seem a bit too short legged for heels at my 5' 7" stature with longer than usual legs. My wedge sandals were a bit off, but I persevered and owned the look. I'm ready for hell or high water. The stretchy waistband was very forgiving and easy to sit at any part of the waist without slipping and sliding about. I wore these all day and was at ease. Almost like wearing pajama pants out to the nail salon and dinner, but elevated. Score. Get these for upscale beachy, elongating and effortless, plus comfort and coolness in the heat of summer. These are a great value compared to linen style pants I've seen elsewhere and even cuter in many ways.

I am so in love with these pants! I have put on a lot of weight over the past year and needed to get some new clothes for an upcoming vacation. These pants are perfect for summer. They are lightweight but not see-through at all. I wore them on a very warm day and did not sweat much. They are also surprisingly durable. I actually fell on my knee the first day I was wearing them and did not scratch or affect the material on the pants at all which was surprising. I’ve worn them numerous times now and have gotten several compliments. I put them in the washing machine in cold water and dry them on low heat and they hold up just fine. They are also somewhat wrinkle resistant. I folded them in half and rolled them in my suitcase and the next day I hung them up at the hotel and there were no wrinkles. For reference I am 5‘5“ approximately 212 pounds and the XXL fit me perfectly. They are a little long on me, but that is OK because I like the way they look. I am definitely ordering more because that this price they are definitely worth adding a few pairs to my wardrobe for summer and fall!

Finally a pair of pants for taller or longer legged people that don't cost an arm and one of those long legs! I'm 5'9" and am mostly legs. Have been having an EXTREMELY difficult time the past 6 months when shopping for pants online (or anywhere really), that: 1). Are Attractive 2). Are Actually Long Enough (not just made to look like it in ads photos) 3). Are NOT overly expensive solely because they were made with longer inseams... Ugh! **Finally found this awesome pair and am so happy with them!** Actually sized for Tall people, true 33" inseam, super comfortable, really flattering, great color, great quality, and all for an affordable price! So happy I've now ordered one pair in each color. No more pretending that I like capri length pants solely bc that's how short "normal" pants look on me. Lol - Super happy with the price also. I can buy 4 pairs for what 1 pair would cost at stores that offer Tall Sizing.

These can be great pants for the office (they look professional but feel stretchy) with a few adjustments. The navy pants run smaller so go up a size. The black pants are true to size. Make sure to try them on with the shoes you’ll wear (not barefoot) to judge length.

My colors never look right. This vest is a dark navy, not light blue.This is a great vest. Thick and well-made. Two external zip pockets and 1 interior hidden pocket. Good zippers. Water resistant. Nice and warm.The size chart is accurate. I'm a woman with a 50 inch chest and 54 inch hip/belly area. I decided to go up to the 3X and it fits loosely in the chest and comfortably in the belly. So choose the fit based on the largest portion of your body and whether you want to wear heavy shirts underneath.The one thing I don't like is that when the zipper is zipped all the way up, the neck is tight. A big and tall person is also going to have a larger neck size and the vest doesn't accommodate that well. If you had a hoodie on, or a thick gaiter, you might not be able to comfortably zip all the way up to your chin. But if you're wearing something else around you neck, you might not need to.

I love vests like this. I'm very hot blooded and usually can't stand to wear a full coat when I'm working outside, even in the winter. This is very lightweight and does an excellent job of keeping my core toasty while allowing full freedom of movement in my arms. At 5'9" and 255lbs, I usually wear a 2X. Running true to size, I ordered a 2X and it fits me perfectly. I can also easily wear a flannel or hoodie underneath it on colder days. The material is similar to a good quality ski jacket. Very easy to wear.

This is a very nice dress shirt. It does have some stretch to it as stated in the title but does not stretch to much. The sleeves go all the way to my wrists so that is wonderful! It fits true to size. For reference, I am 5'6", 118lbs, and I ordered a small tall. The buttons are secure on both the shirt and cuffs. It does tend to wrinkle so keep that in mind. It has pleats down the front and looks good with both pants and skirts. All the seams are secure with no loose strings. I have not noticed much if any shrinkage after washing so that is always good.
