This is a very nice quality, and well-made white button-down shirt. I am 5'2" and 135#. I ordered this shirt in Petite Medium because I was concerned that it might run small, but it doesn't. I actually wish I had gotten the Petite Small instead. The fabric is nice and smooth, almost a satin feel to it. It's light and drapey, but not thin, so the bra doesn't show through. It seems well made with nice details on the sleeves. I do wish the cuffs would be a little bit tighter. The only thing that bothers me about this shirt is that there is no info anywhere that says what the fabric content is, which is something I thought is a requirement for clothing in the US, including imports. Google tells me this clothing company is based in Korea. Beyond that, I like this shirt and I do recommend it.

I actually wish I had gotten the Petite Small instead. The fabric is nice and smooth, almost a satin feel to it. It's light and drapey, but not thin, so the bra doesn't show through. It seems well made with nice details on the sleeves. I do wish the cuffs would be a little bit tighter. The only thing that bothers me about this shirt is that there is no info anywhere that says what the fabric content is, which is something I thought is a requirement for clothing in the US, including imports. Google tells me this clothing company is based in Korea. Beyond that, I like this shirt and I do recommend it.

I was pleasantly surprised by the quality and fit of this BBIBBI Women's Stretch Long Sleeve Button Down Dress Shirt! It came in pristine condition, with impeccable stitching, buttons securely attached, and a quality medium weight fabric that holds it's shape, is not see through, and is a true bright white. All the things I look for in a quality dress shirt!Prior to wearing it, I washed the shirt with other whites on a medium warm cycle and dried it on the regular dryer cycle. It did not shrink, most of the wrinkles came out and it was still a beautiful bright white. I ordered my usual size small and it was a perfect fit. For reference, I am petite (5'1") 125lbs, 32B/C. There was enough length in the body and sleeves for taller women. I liked that it had figure flattering seams and was not boxy. While there was plenty of room for comfortable movement, there was also some stretch for those a bit more busty then me.This shirt is very versatile and looks great on it's own, but would also look great under a sweater, blazer or vest. It would look great with a scarf or tie or open at the neck with a necklace. This type of shirt is a classic and never goes out of style. Considering the quality it should be a staple in your closet for several seasons! I think it is worth the pricepoint.

I’m a tall girl, 5’10”, and it’s so difficult to find dress shirts that fit like they should. Most of the time, sleeves are too short, and if they are fitted, the curve at the waist is too high and the shirt tails too short. Larger sizes just grow outward… however, THIS BBiBBi button down dress shirt is an absolutely PERFECT fit. I can’t even believe it. The shirt is exactly like shown in the product images, and the sleeves are long enough to wear under a blazer, hitting my wrists just as they should. The top is fitted, with no extra bulk under the armpits or through the waist. The fabric is smooth and has some stretch, while not easily wrinkling. It makes for a very comfortable fit. The shirt tails stay tucked in when I raise my arms, and it truly is everything I need in a dress shirt. I wish there were other color choices available, I’d buy more! For the price of this shirt, I feel like I got an excellent product for my money. The shirt has washed well, and it resists wrinkling if I take it out of the dryer while still a bit damp and hang it to finish drying. For reference, I’m about 160 lbs, about a 36 C/D and I ordered a size medium-tall.

These are a thick heavyweight wide leg sweatpants. The cotton is soft and smooth but not as soft as other pants that may be a cotton blend instead of 100% cotton. They are a little long on me, but at only 5’2” it is about like most wide leg sweats fit. Haven’t tried washing yet but the care instructions state they can’t be put in the dryer and instead to line dry in the shade. Not the most practical for lounge wear, but hopefully if wash in cold water and dry on low heat they won’t shrink. Slightly snug in waist but it does have a decent bit of stretch still.

The wide leg is pretty wide for me, I’m not used to that. But since I only wore them while I was home it was OK. Although I don’t think I will have any issues wearing them out. I had a pair of white sneakers on with them and they looked good. I like the light brown color, it’s very neutral and perfect for the fall. The waist band is a bit wider than I was expecting, but isn’t a bad thing. I like that they have both front and back pockets. They are long, which doesn’t bother me since I’m only 5’2” and all my pants are long. Overall I like the fit and feel of these pants and would recommend them.

I love the pants. They are of a good quality and true to size. I'm 5'3", and the short inseam is the perfect length. I ordered the curvy bootcut, and they fit everywhere without being too tight in the waist. The 4 stars is purely because of how it was shipped. I clearly marked the option to have the pants sent in Amazon packaging because I know manufacturer shipping materials are often lackluster at best. As you can see in the photo, that's not standard Amazon packaging. It's raining here today, so if I hadn't been here when UPS dropped the pants at my door, rain definitely would have gotten into the packaging and possibly ruined the pants. Great pants, but you're taking a risk with the shipping.

Needed some comfy - but professional- pants for an interview. Shopping for pants online is hit or miss so I was extra thankful and surprised these were a hit. I read the reviews and bought the higher end of my normal size and they fit perfectly. I am between a 14 curvy fit to16 regular fit with waist gap so I ordered the 16. They are very comfortable and flattering so I had more confidence going into my interview than I would have had with some ill-fitting, cheaply made pants. They are very good quality for the price and I’m sure I will get quite a few wears out of them. I did not have any kind of waist gap and they stretched enough to go over my curvy hips without looking like I was going to bust out of them like a can of biscuits. The length was perfect - socks weren’t showing when I sat down and I wasn’t walking on them. I could have even worn a little heel with them if I felt like breaking an ankle to make an lasting impression on my interviewers. For reference, I am 5’5 and about 190lbs.

I love these pants. I'm 5'7, size 12, 33in waist, 45in hips, 170lbs and a size 12 fits perfectly, maybe a tiny bit big but I don't like things too tight. I I have a hard time finding pants that fit both my waist and hips and are not too tight. These are comfortable and fit well. Stretchy but don't look like yoga pants. The grey, however, is very dark - much darker than the photo. I wish these came in a lighter grey. I plan on buying a couple more colors.

These are an awesome purchase!! I am curvy/plus size and have a hard time finding a pant that fits proportionally let alone is flattering! These fit me so well and are pretty comfy on top of it. I will be buying more colors. Love wearing them with a boot since I am 5’3 to give the full boot cut look. Highly recommend!!

These pants are perfect for the office. They don’t show any lint or other material which is very nice. They fit perfectly and as expected. I got a size 4, I am 5’ 7” and 160lbs. It is a little short on me but I have very long legs so I’m used to it. These pants actually went longer than some of my other dress pants which was very nice! Will definitely buy more

They are longer than a 32 inch inseam. I have a 34-in inseam and these are long on me which is okay, I can fold them under. Negatives: material is very thin, pockets are too low & thin, decorative thread is fraying after 2 washes with Woolite on delicate. Wanted to love, but not worth the money.

I hate scrubs. For 30 years, I would run home to change before I was seen outside of work in them. They never fit right, they’re uncomfortable, the material sucks and is so thin that I’m always cold. I found these and gave one pair a try, without having much hope. Well, let me tell you, they are amazing. They are so comfortable and feel and look so great that I’d wear them outside of work, too! I will never wear anything else. Every time they go on sale, I buy another pair or two because I’m afraid that someday, they’ll disappear and I’ll never be able to find anything like them ever again - they are that great! Some stats - I’m 5’8 and 185 or so, somewhat curvy with a belly and the kind of waist that lends itself to muffin tops. I wear a size L for scrubs and a 12 for jeans. I bought these in both XL - which is a wee bit too long and slightly loose, and a L - which is the perfect size for me and fits like a glove, makes my legs and butt look good, and doesn’t pinch or pull anywhere or give me the dreaded muffin top. LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE PANTS! (This is the only pic I have of me wearing them but I’ll tell you that bending down in them is effortless!)

I ordered multiple brands off of amazon just to see which scrub pants will be comfortable for me to work in. I just had a c section so I needed something comfortable in my stomach, these are a little snug. I’m on the fence about exchanging them for a bigger size. I am able to squat/bend over no problem. The pockets are deep. They were the most “expensive” ones that I bought and I got two based off reviews but I’m not too happy about them because of how snug they do feel. I am 5’5 240lbs I wear size 16jeans from American eagle/target/Aeropostale

The waistband was comfortably loose, just snug enough to stay up but I’m a little worried the large will cut into my soft upper hip area and be both uncomfortable and unsightly. Unfortunately the XL is too large through the hip and looks sloppy. The length is correct.

Tall ladies… I am 5”10” 105lbs- straight figure- and pants are SO hard to find!! 35-36” inseam usually is best for me but I ordered the XS 34” in these and they are close to perfect and yet above “doable”. The black pants are slightly longer than the navy in this size but I’m keeping both pairs because finding nice pants is rare. These pants fit perfectly & will keep the length as long as I don’t put them in the dryer.I also ordered (but returned) “ichosy” pants but they were not as flattering for my figure. They would probably work for someone that has a slightly curvy figure (with hips).Photos don't really show the nice quality of these pants but I wanted to give an idea of the length & my stick figure. Also attached photo of the ichosy listing in case you want to go that route.

I did not realize these are like glorified sweatpants. I was trying to gift my daughter a pair of nice work slacks. I also didn’t realize they had an elastic waistband. I saw the photo that looked like they had a zipper & button. I think they look really tacky. Note the button is off center and the stitching is coming out of one seam. Also, she can be an XS or a S. I read reviews that said they run small so I got her the S. But they are too big. Nothing is nice about these except she said the fabric is really soft. She is 5’10”, about 125/130lbs, and I ordered a S in both 32” & 34”. She ideally needs 33” so neither length is ideal.

These boots are super cute and wide calf friendly! They are not the most comfortable boot ever but as long as you’re not standing for long periods of time they are a yes! I’m 5’3 and they come just below my knee.

I’m giving this a 4 star because the only thing wrong with this item is that, it is extremely wide for my skinny leg. I’m a size 0-2, 5’7 and size 10 in shoes. I’ve always had problems finding tall affordable boots that fit me almost perfectly at least. As you can see in my pictures, my legs are practically swimming in them. Other than that, I think this is a great quality item. The heels are comfortable. I didn’t wear them outside, so I wouldn’t really know how slip resistant they are. The color is a bit tricky. I know forsure they’re dark red. Just not the same shade as the brand displayed in their pictures. It’s more dark red than burgundy or wine shade. If you don’t mind wide boots even with skinny legs, then this is a great buy for you ✨

Not exactly flattering but are any scrubs? They were baggy in some areas and fitting in others but that could just be me with how I am loosing weight. I was expecting a MUCH softer material these are VERY rough I understand they are workwear but I was also expecting them to be better quality being Cherokee. The tag in the back of them is very long and irritating and the elastic is also rough and scratchy I have to wear a under shirt just to make it comfortable on my waistline. They were a good length I’m 5’9 and they were perfect for me. They also had a higher waistline which I do prefer. They did seem a little tight on my knees though when I sit down but I just adjust the pants and I’m fine but it is annoying to do all day. Is it annoying enough to return them no I need pants for work I couldn’t wait to return them and there isn’t anything local so I’m making the best of it by cutting the tag out and wearing a tank top tucked in to them. I was happy with how stretchy the elastic is though it’s very stretchy and forgiving! I have a very sensitive stomach so that was a plus for me! These do have lots of nice sized pockets for all the little things we need to carry along as well. I’ll definitely be looking for a softer material next time though!!!

i usually wear the iflex collection from cherokee, but i needed new scrubs quickly so i purchased these! there werent many reviews on these so i was a little nervous to buy them. they actually fit pretty well (im a size small and im 5’2 120 lbs for reference). they sit on the waist very nicely and have a nice feel to them. they do not have that paper feeling either! the only con is that when i sit, they kind of get low around the back and i have to pick them up right when i stand. but other than that, these are cute if you’re looking for a basic pair of scrub pants that are durable and work well :-)

I am not used to wearing scrubs, but these seemed nice. I am 5'4" 145lbs and bought the medium petite. The elastic waistband is nice, but if I was a little bigger, they would probably start to be snug on the hips. They aren't tight around my thighs which is nice since they are a little on the large side. They are a little rough and stiff out of the package, but hopefully a wash or two helps that. I paired these with the Cherokee women's v-neck top and the color matched perfectly.

These lined leggings are awesome! Oh my gosh, I’ve always been hesitant about trying them because I am very hot-natured, but even though they are thicker and warm, they are not suffocating. The fabric is very high quality and could easily pass as dress pants if needed. I am 5’5” and go between medium and large (apple shaped), and medium fits perfectly although very little room to spare. I could have probably fit into a large as well but the medium is not uncomfortable.

i don’t recall seeing any plus sized girls review these boots yet, if i missed it, oh well. i am 5’3, 215 lbs. my calves measure at 17.5in around. these AREN’T the most wide calve friendly boots! i was able to get one boot to fully zip up all the way after using rubbing alcohol and some heat (haha). it didn’t take me long though and no damage was caused! they’re stretchy for sure. regardless, i can make these work. they’re SUPER cute, SO so comfy, the heel is the perfect height! i definitely recommend and have already shared the link to so many friends. love these!

I ordered these maternity jeans for my daughter. Her baby is due in December. I got her a size large because she wants to make sure her clothes are roomy and accommodate her growing shape. Although I love these jeans and the way they are cut, I’m deducting a star because they seem to run a little small. This large fits more like a medium in my opinion. I’m posting a photo of myself wearing these pants and I’m not the one that’s pregnant. I weigh 133 pounds currently and I’m 5‘2“ tall. The jeans are plenty long, and I’m sure they will work on my daughter who is 5‘6“. They should fit her right now because she is only five months along. However, I’m thinking if you truly are needing size large, then you need to order this in an extra large. These are snug against my hips and thighs. At my size, you would think that they would be a little more roomy.

I have to give these pants 5 stars. They are incredibly comfortable and stretch well without losing their shape. I forgot until I tried them on that they were flared, and they sure do have a fun silhouette! The stomach panel is really soft, and I love that it is a silky material that will slide nicely underneath shirts as opposed to bunching. At 5’9 I kind of wish they were slightly longer, but honestly they look exactly like they do on the model in terms of length. Will definitely be getting plenty of wear out of these!

The pair of maternity jeans are so comfortable to wear. The medium fits so well and offers more room to grow as it is stretchy. The fabric is soft and not itchy on my sensitive skin and all stitchings are done well. The inseam is also the right length for my height 5’3”. It washed well in the washer and tumble dried low so no shrinking. Overall, these are great for its price and quality.

First of all,I expected to have to return these because I’m supposedly 5’4 but I have never had a pair of flares that didn’t drag the ground. So just on a whim I was like let me just try 27 inch inseam bc I’m just now realizing I should have been wearing petite. But other women my height got the 29inch inseam and said it fit perfectly and I’ve never noticed a difference between 29 and 30 both drag the floor. I was assuming because of 4’9-5’0 women ordering the small 27 inch inseam that I would be returning this tomorrow…but somehow these fit me better than any flares ever have. Including waist. A little stretchy but will be perfect with a belt. Go figure 😅Absolutely love these.Worth a try.

It arrived with animaI hair all over it. I get people return things but it could’ve been cleaned before reselling. Anyway, it fits great. I’m 5’1” and about 150 lbs and usually wear a large but I ordered a Med with 27” inseam. Had to keep because I need new pants for work. Will just wash it well.

They look like expensive work pants! They are made of quality material, very stretchy. I'm 5'0 and have a short torso - I can't remember what I ordered whether it was small petite or xs. I measured the inseam of the Express pants I have with the same flare and they fit perfectly. I am wearing a 3" heel in this picture and they do not drag when I walk. I took my heels off to change into flip flops and they were way too long. Highly recommend! I sat comfortably in an 8 hour conference, then wore them to walk around for another few hours. The pants kept shape.

These knee high boots by CELLENZ are as comfortable as they are stylish. The heel is great for me. It's what the industry calls a "kitten" heel. The modern design of these boots slims and elevates your look, without breaking your back doing so because the heel is so doable and easy to wear. Though the heel is narrow, it's sturdy and balanced. I did not feel tippy at all. The toe box has a gentle point, but it has ample room for an average width foot, such as mine. I do not feel any pinching at the sides or in the tip when I'm still or in motion. My heel does not slip up and down. I can even run in these boots. As for size, I normally wear a US 8 or 8.5 in boots. I ordered these in an 8, and they fit me perfectly. I have thick, athletic legs and I was a bit worried that these might be too tight. But I trusted the advertisement that they accommodated wide calves, and to my relief, they fit like they were made for me. The material does sit against my calves, but it's not tight or restricting because it has some give, even as it's not real leather. The lip of the boots does not irritate the back of my legs at all. I'm about 5'4" and they fall just below my knees. I wear them with very thin socks, or no socks at all. The sole has a little bit of traction in the toe box, but it's generally smooth on the underside of the boots. If you're worried about slippage, I'd suggest you simply add some adhesive grips for more peace of mind. I think the burgundy color is beautiful. It's a deep red that leans more cherry crimson instead of a plummy burgundy. There are no orange or golden undertones. This color looks fabulous with a neutral or brown palette, especially. If you have thick calves like me, putting on the boots requires you to pull them on to the ankles, and then sort of shimmy the calf section up like you're putting on knee high socks. To take them off, I find it efficient for me to scrunch them back down to the ankles, and then I pull them off the rest of the way. It's not hard at all. Included are individual fabric bags for each boot for storage, which is nice. All told, I like them a lot! I plan on wearing them often.

But I trusted the advertisement that they accommodated wide calves, and to my relief, they fit like they were made for me. The material does sit against my calves, but it's not tight or restricting because it has some give, even as it's not real leather. The lip of the boots does not irritate the back of my legs at all. I'm about 5'4" and they fall just below my knees. I wear them with very thin socks, or no socks at all. The sole has a little bit of traction in the toe box, but it's generally smooth on the underside of the boots. If you're worried about slippage, I'd suggest you simply add some adhesive grips for more peace of mind. I think the burgundy color is beautiful. It's a deep red that leans more cherry crimson instead of a plummy burgundy. There are no orange or golden undertones. This color looks fabulous with a neutral or brown palette, especially. If you have thick calves like me, putting on the boots requires you to pull them on to the ankles, and then sort of shimmy the calf section up like you're putting on knee high socks. To take them off, I find it efficient for me to scrunch them back down to the ankles, and then I pull them off the rest of the way. It's not hard at all. Included are individual fabric bags for each boot for storage, which is nice. All told, I like them a lot! I plan on wearing them often.

I generally love Sorels. I grew up in Canada, and we know great snow boots. 20+ years ago, Sorels were a high quality brand, and they made stuff that lasts. I've read that recently, their quality has suffered. I was intrigued. I live in California now, but I have been wanting snow boots because we've been going back to Canada during the Winter. And, I need good snow boots. I was stoked to get these. So far, although my initial impressions of these were being impressed with the quality, fit and finish, and looks. All of that was moot because of the sizing.I normally wear a size 7.5 womens. And, that's generally half a size up from my actual foot size. We decided to go with an 8 in these, because I told my husband that you go up another half size in snow boots because of thick winter socks. Wow, I'm glad I did, because otherwise the 7.5's would have been WAY too small and borderline abusive to my feet. Even the 8's are too small. The main reason for this is because of the super narrow forefoot and TIGHT toe box. The boots do not open up at the toes, but almost taper inwards like a ballerina shoe. It's barely comfortable at size 8, and I wish I could return these for a larger pair. But, I'm afraid as a Viner, I'm stuck with these. We can't return products.So, yeah, great looking boots, really nice and warm, but way too small and tight. Size up a FULL SIZE for these. I have normal wide feet, even on the narrow side, and these would be way too tight at my regular size.

Yoga pants are so variable! These are not tummy control. The front waist height is exactly as pictured - just below the belly button. Feels a bit like the front is longer than the back - I prefer the reverse. The biggest negative is the material is alint/dust magnet and it doesn't brush off like some others. See picture - I was working in the shop as my usual and I have like 15 different yoga pants for working in the shop and these are the worst for dust magnet. We also have 4 dogs and 2 cats = the cat hair especially loves these pants! The regular length is perfect for me (5'7 30" waist 40" hips) and I usually wear 32" inseam like on Levis jeans. 31" is always just a bit too short for me. These are great length even when sitting down. The medium is slightly looser than other mediums. The iron on label is not irritating like some other brands. Overall I will not get another pair of these because of the lint magnet issue but the construction and quality of fabric is good and non-see thru for working out.

I love these jeans! They've quickly become my favorite, and now I neglect basically every other pair I own. They are incredibly comfortable and flattering. They're super stretchy without losing their shape. In the photo attached to this review, I'd worn them for at least 10 hours prior to taking the pic. Comfy jeans are a MUST for me, and I can't wear any that are super restricting. These fit the bill and have soooo much give! I get tons of compliments every time I wear them! I'm a true millennial and resisted making the switch from skinny jeans, but I really think these offer the best of both worlds; they're form-fitting around the waist and hips and looser on the legs, without looking super baggy. I'm 5'2 and was worried about the length, but they're perfect (probably because they're advertised as cropped). They're true to size, as I'm generally a size 4 (sometimes a 2), and the size 4 fit like a glove. The material itself seems high quality, and I've washed them multiple times without any fading, shrinking, etc. The only cons: 1) I do wish some stitching was added to prevent the raw hem from unraveling so much. If you buy the raw hem version, it's a TRUE raw hem - they essentially look like they're cut with scissors at the bottom. I wasn't sure how they would wash, but they look like a more natural frayed hem after washing. Still, though, I worry that they could eventually become too frayed because they don't have any stitching to reinforce the length. Already some strings have gotten a bit long, and I've had to trim them. 2) The price keeps rising! I'd love to buy more (probably without the raw hem), but I'll wait for a Prime Day sale to see if I can get a better deal. You can buy AE jeans for less on sale. Overall, I'm super happy with these jeans and will plan on buying more in the future! My sister already bought some and is in love with her pair, too. I'd recommend these for anyone searching for jeans that look amazing without sacrificing an ounce of comfort!

I am 5'7", weigh 210 lbs, and usually wear a size 16/18 pant. I ordered XX-Large. These pants are my favorite linen pants. They are so comfy, with good stretch in the waistband. I’m obsessed. These are super comfy and lightweight. Out of the bag they were pretty wrinkled, but one run through the washer and hang dry and they were perfect. They’ve been washed multiple times (hang dry) and are holding up well to all day wear in the heat. I’m 5’3 in Medium and don’t feel these are too long (just wear higher on waist). Khaki color is NOT see through! No liner needed! Wish it had belt loops is all, and I’ve pulled out the brand label from the front (easy to do). And great side pockets :) Update: I’ve lived in these pants all summer, even through 90+ degree days. They have stood up to standard stain removal cleaners, rough play with the kids and easily survived a couple of accidental runs through the dryer without significant shrinkage. These are STILL a MUST BUY! Especially at the <$30 price point. Such a great value.

· The fabric is a soft, slightly stretchy ponte knit that is breathable and has a nice, substantial weight to it—it doesn't feel thin or cheap at all. The wide-leg design allows for complete freedom of movement, whether I'm walking, running errands, or just lounging at home. · The taupe color is beautiful—a perfect neutral, warm greige that pairs effortlessly with everything in my closet (blacks, whites, creams, navy, and even pastels). The front pleats and high-rise waistband are incredibly slimming and create a polished, dressy silhouette. They expertly hide any problem areas while making your legs look miles long. · UNEXPECTED AND AMAZING POCKETS: This is absolutely my favorite feature. The pockets are deep, functional, and seamlessly integrated. My large smartphone fits securely without creating a bulky look, which is a rare and wonderful find in women's pants. · Petite Size Available: As someone who is 5'2", I was thrilled that a Petite length was offered. It made all the difference in getting a better fit right out of the package. Things to Consider: · Length for Petites: I am 5'2" and ordered the Petite size. When worn high on my waist, the length is full and still grazes the floor, but it's perfectly manageable with sneakers or any kind of heel. For a clean, ankle-length look with flat shoes, someone my height or shorter would likely need to get them hemmed. · Waistband: The waistband is non-roll and very comfortable. It's a true high-rise, so if you prefer a mid-rise fit, that's something to be aware of. For me, it's perfect for tucking in tops. · Sizing: I typically wear a size Medium and found the Medium Petite to fit me perfectly—true to size. Overall, I am thoroughly impressed with the quality for the price. The substantial weight of the fabric makes these pants look and feel far more expensive than they are. Despite the minor length consideration for my height, they are an incredible value.

These are great pants and truly plus friendly. The waistband is elastic and stretchy which keeps it comfortable. There's no zipper or button, although there's a zipper fold it's just for aesthetics. So you aren't going to pop out of it when moving around. I generally wear 2XL and I found these true to size. I also got it in petite. I'm 5'1 and that made it fit a lot better. I'm always having to pull my pants up a ton or hem them. They're just good out of the bag. Although they're for working out I actually think they'll look good as work slacks too. The material is soft and comfortable. It's not thin which is good for moving around. The legs are baggy. Yoga pants that aren't gonna make you leave nothing to the imagination. It feels flowy and comfortable but not in the way. It's made with Lycra so it moves with you. There are nice deep pockets and they're sewn in on a way that looks good, it flows with the pants well so your phone isn't sticking out in a funny way. Machine washable it says with like colors and tumble dry low. Iron on low.

I have always struggled to find pants that are long enough for me, and I can't even imagine how difficult it would be if I were taller. I am 5' 9", which isn't exactly short either, and I have very long legs for my height. I can't shop in stores, especially for pajama pants because they're always too short. When I wash my clothes, I always let my pants air dry so I don't take the chance that they shrink. I have even gone as far as making my own pajama pants, just so I could have something long enough to wear. When I saw these pajama pants in a size tall, I was so excited! They come in a 32" and a 34" length. I ordered the 34 just in case, and I was absolutely ecstatic when I tried them on and found that they were too long. I haven't washed them yet, so I can't comment on how much they'll shrink, but even if they do, they'll still fit me. The stitching on the pants appears to be good quality, and the fabric is nice and cozy. The pants have a pocket on each side, as well as one on the back. According to the measurements I was in between sizes, so I opted for the larger of the two choices. There is a drawstring if you need to tighten the waist. If you are someone like me who struggles to find pants that are long enough, buy these. Unless you need an inseam longer than 34", you will not be disappointed!

I've been wearing these PJ pants for weeks now. They are so comfy and warm but not too hot. The fabric isn't stretchy so you'll definitely need to take that into consideration. I got the medium 32" inseam (not sure of my exact inseam measurements but I am 5'4") but I could have even gone up a size for a little extra room. I'm typically around a smaller and medium usually, but for this a large would have been just fine. While I'm stoked it has pockets at all and they are fairly deep, I wish there were two pockets in the back. Sometimes if I'm just standing and talking I like to rest both hands in the back pockets. And considering I'm wearing the literally everywhere it's kind of annoying. But overall they aren't bad sleep/lounge pants!

I ordered the 32” inseam pants and was impressed that they were exactly that! It can be difficult to find pants to fit my inseam, unless I want capris. I would consider these a good quality lightweight flannel, they’re soft to the touch and didn’t pill after washing. All that being said, these have zero stretch and if you’re on the larger end of a size, consider going up one size if you like a more relaxed, comfortable fit. These are made for tall, slim figures - if you’re like me and are curvy through the hips or thighs or plus size, I’d encourage you to look elsewhere. Slim figured and wonderfully long legged? These could be a great fit for you! THEY HAVE POCKETS!

It is so difficult to find pajama pants that are long enough for me. I'm only 5'5 but I have long legs and wear 33" inseam in jeans for them to be long enough with flat shoes. I have searched for tall/long pajama pants and didn't have any luck until these appeared. First of all, I love the black/white plaid. Next, they were nice and soft straight out of the box. I ordered a 34" inseam and while a little long for me they are great with my slippers because they keep them from dragging. Did I mention they have pockets? They have pockets! Side pockets and back pockets! I washed and tumble dried them before wearing and they came out just as soft as before washing and also came out of the dryer wrinkle free. They have a draw string if you need to cinch the waist but the size chart is accurate. They fit perfectly and are well made. No loose threads or anything. Even if you don't need the long length, you'll have a great pair of flannel pajama pants in the standard length. Highly recommended!

Love these jeans! Perfect color, not too dark, not too light. They are not pull-on but they do have stretch, I am so glad I ordered a size down because this size gives me room but aren’t baggy anywhere, they fit nice, the next size up would have been big, everywhere. I am 5’7” but have always worn long or tall inseams and these fit perfect in length but they bunch a little at the bottom. As a Levi’s wearer since I was a young teen, these are the same quality but are softer and not stiff like the ones I wore as a teen. Will be buying more! The material isn’t thin, it’s very decent. The legs are straight. I have muscular calves and these were not tight on my calves. The pockets are small so you can’t get a cell phone in them, a few keys or maybe a credit card would fit. I didn’t really notice the shaping much but they felt comfy when I put them on.

Review of TTZ Women's Tall Pajama Pants These pajama pants have a warm, cozy look with their blue and green plaid pattern. They're made from 100% cotton, that feels like a soft, breathable, lightweight flannel. There is no stretch in the fabric, but the relaxed, loose-fit design makes them comfortable to lounge or sleep in without feeling restrictive. They have a 32" inseam, which works well for taller wearers. The elastic waistband is comfortable and easy to pull on, and the drawstring adds a bit of adjustability for a better fit. The legs are straight with very little taper. The thigh is about 13-1/2” across. The hem is just about 11” across. From waist to bottom of hem is approximately 44-1/2” long. From top of waist to point of crotch is approximately 16-1/4” long. Overall, the quality looks and feels good. The seams are neatly sewn, and I didn't notice any flaws.

I’m 5’8 and the bottoms go over my heels but the materiel isn’t too thick so usually it bunches nicely around my ankle with shoes. These are great if you wear boots or heels. The size and fit is awesome and stretchy enough not to feel rigid while also not looking baggy or too loose anywhere. I appreciate that it’s comfortable while also maintaining that high waisted look I love. Very soft and durable, I haven’t had any issue with wear or pelting where my thighs rub and no snags or hanging strings. The only issue I’ve had with wear is the little fabric logo tag in the back has worn almost completely off but I don’t mind that. I need to go ahead and remove it if I can without damaging but the color is very consistent and hasn’t faded and the tag blends in so it’s hardly noticeable.

Very comfortable and the length is perfect for me. I'm 5'10 with longer legs. I probably could have ordered a size smaller, but have recently lost 30lbs and wasn't quite sure of the size so I can't tell if they run big.

👖 In the picture : color "Blue Ice" size 12 Long ( 5'7-168cm / 200lbs-90kg) Looks better in person. Pro: - Very Stretchy - SUPER confortable - color is nice, does not fade (1.5 year of use at the time of review - washed with the outside in to prevent fading) - True to size - medium waist height (not low rise - not high rise) Cons: - Long version of this pair of jeans is NOT LONG. (minus 1 star 🌟 for me. I only purchase Long version of pants and its always good for me. I am not extremely tall but this one is NOT. I just roll the pants and wear it as a long capris/ankle length. 👖 This pair or jeans is so confortable it makes up for the cons. I hope this helps. Good luck!

I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

As a consistent TTZ customer, I expected a premium lounge pant, especially at a $30 price point. Unfortunately, these are a major disappointment in both durability and value. While the classic navy and green plaid look great initially, the quality is far below the $30 asking price. ​My Stats: 5'4" | 30" Inseam | 31" Waist Size Ordered: Large (Advertised: 32" Inseam, 31-33" Waist) ​PERFORMANCE & MEASUREMENTS ​I followed a strict "gentle" laundering protocol: cold water wash with smooth fabrics and just 5 minutes on low heat to de-wrinkle before hanging to dry. Despite this, the shrinkage was catastrophic. ​Post-Wash Flat Measurements: ​Inseam: Now 29" (Shrank from the advertised 32"). They are now "high-waters" even for my 5'4" height. ​Thigh: 11.5" flat (approx. 23" total), significantly tighter than the 28.2" promised. ​Waist: 16" flat (32" total). ​LOW VALUE FOR THE PRICE ​At $30, these are positioned as mid-to-high-tier pajama pants, but the fabric performance is "budget" at best. ​Instant Pilling: The fabric is already pilling before its first actual wear. To have a 100% cotton garment look worn out before it even reaches the couch is unacceptable. ​Comparison: I own other TTZ items that have held their shape for years. This specific pair feels like a significant step down in material standards. ​Final Verdict: If you are paying $30 for "Tall" pants, you expect them to stay tall. These fail to deliver on that promise. Between the pilling and the massive shrinkage, these represent very poor value. I cannot recommend them.

While the classic navy and green plaid look great initially, the quality is far below the $30 asking price. ​My Stats: 5'4" | 30" Inseam | 31" Waist Size Ordered: Large (Advertised: 32" Inseam, 31-33" Waist) ​PERFORMANCE & MEASUREMENTS ​I followed a strict "gentle" laundering protocol: cold water wash with smooth fabrics and just 5 minutes on low heat to de-wrinkle before hanging to dry. Despite this, the shrinkage was catastrophic. ​Post-Wash Flat Measurements: ​Inseam: Now 29" (Shrank from the advertised 32"). They are now "high-waters" even for my 5'4" height. ​Thigh: 11.5" flat (approx. 23" total), significantly tighter than the 28.2" promised. ​Waist: 16" flat (32" total). ​LOW VALUE FOR THE PRICE ​At $30, these are positioned as mid-to-high-tier pajama pants, but the fabric performance is "budget" at best. ​Instant Pilling: The fabric is already pilling before its first actual wear. To have a 100% cotton garment look worn out before it even reaches the couch is unacceptable. ​Comparison: I own other TTZ items that have held their shape for years. This specific pair feels like a significant step down in material standards. ​Final Verdict: If you are paying $30 for "Tall" pants, you expect them to stay tall. These fail to deliver on that promise. Between the pilling and the massive shrinkage, these represent very poor value. I cannot recommend them.

I ordered the recommendation XXL and was so surprised that these jeans fit. For context, I’m 6’1 and 255lbs so it is very rare that Amazon jeans fit. But this fit in the waist and the length was great. However… not even 15 minutes into my night and the button broke! It just popped off, the jeans were not too tight. The button is pointing attached. I was able to snap it back on. But it kept popping off all night. I was forced to wear my jeans unbuttoned to prevent me from losing the button This would have been a 5 star review for tall plus size women. But the button issue makes it a 2 star.
10/10 with the pants! For sure please size up. I am 5’7 205 and they fit perfectly! I struggle with length for pants with boots for this pants were perfect! Will buy different colors

They’re not bad, but the button is definitely really cheap quality. I’m 5’2 110lbs, they fit my waist wonderfully but look kinda baggy everywhere else. I want to love them but they’re meh. They work. The color is great, they’re comfortable, I just wish they came in an xs size. I probably wouldn’t buy them again unless they were available in smaller sizes.

I've only worn the boots approx 3 hrs at a time the past 5 days but I wanted to get a review out since it's the time of yr to purchase rain boots. I'll update this after I've used them more. I'll start off saying I'm a Muck boot fan, they are so dang expensive. Hoping Hisea will stand up to my needs as well as Mucks. Shoe size 8-8.5 Calf 13" Height 5'6 I have tiny bones, lean athletic built. Boots Length & Width: i purchased a 9 (Hisea does not do half sizes) making sure i had enough room for my wool socks. I've worn the boots with my wool hiking socks which r thin, light weight wool socks & my medium wool socks. - I have enough length & width for the above mentioned socks. My heavy wool might b too snug, but I generally only wear them at night sleeping when backpacking during winter - I have a narrow foot & have problems finding regular shoes that fits comfortably & that doesn't slip off when walking. Buying sandals i always have to add holes to make straps smaller, like with my Berkinstocks, & my Sketchers velcro straps don't go small enough. The width of the Hisea rain boots fit me nicely & I don't swim in them like I do my clunky Mucks. - if u have a wide foot, specially with a heavier sock, the width could b an issue for u. Sole of Boot: it's very thin compared to a Much. Walking around my yard, garden, on the street it does fine. My concern is how will it do using a shovel & digging into the hard ground. Inner Arch Support: If u are like me & require support u will need to get inserts for them. I've notice it during the short time I've worn them with out doing any hard labor. Material: the boots r much thinner than what I'm use to in a Much & they are not lined. Concerned that I might tear or puncture. Calf Width & Cinching: This was the reason I purchased the boots, being able to cinch up. My Much were so wide on my calves I'd get dirt & water down them. I read several reviews where people complained about them being to tight on their calves. My calf is 13", without cinching there's about 1.25' gap. My use: I'm outside all the time & just purchased property where I'll be digging out a creek & building up the bank, cleaning all the scrub brush out of the forest, building geothermal greenhouse, putting in gardens & orchard, felling trees, milling lumber, building house. I'm much harder on my outside gear than 90% Opinion: if your smaller frame like myself the Hisea boot will fit u nicely. You'll b able to pull over leggings & skinny jeans easily. They'll look nice wearing to school, walking dog, going shopping... Hisea give a zip up tote that the boots role up nicely in & could go into backpack if student wants to change into shoes once at school.

These ones are hard to review. You could call them a rain boot, but I think they’re more of a rain sock.. They do have a 1/2 inch base. However no support and don’t walk on rocky/gravel roads as you feel everything. Size wise I ordered an 8, I range a 7.5-8 and they are tight. The calf is very tight but the plus is no water in. I weigh 128 so not big calves. They are waterproof so I guess you can say rain boots. These would be awesome for travel, because they roll up really small so can travel without taking up room. And they are sup light weight. So yes they work but not for day to day use, I will update when I have really tested them.

I haven’t worn jeans since before I was pregnant (like 3 years ago) because leggings are just so comfy. I also didn’t want to know what size I was now after having 2 babies. I finally decided to get a pair & I’m so happy I got these. For reference, I’m 5’2 & 240lbs & carrying most of the weight in my belly. I got a size 20 & they fit perfectly! They definitely sculpt your body, but at the same time so comfy. I wish these came in more colors!

Now, this review is being written based on initial try-on. I haven’t worn them for any amount of time, so I can’t speak to longevity. For reference I’m a heavy woman. 5’5”, short legs, 240 lbs. The size 18 was perfect, altho a tad long. But that’s ok. I like to cuff anyway. These are extremely comfortable and I plan on buying more pairs.

The waist is super comfy with good stretch, the fabric is soft but not cheaply thin. I'm 5'8" and 180 lbs. They look fine with no shoes on, or probably in the summer with sandals but I hate the flapping around the ankles when the pants don't touch the ground entirely. 1 extra inch longer and I would have been happy but sadly will be returning. Many of you will love this fit and feel, it's just not for me is all.

Pros: - No front seam! - Soft mostly-matte fabric molds to your curves and is squat-proof - Super stretchy so you feel no restriction or tightness in the legs when you're moving around - Double lined waistband offers moderate compression and helps you feel held in - High rise waistband hits right at my belly button - Spacious pockets on both sides can easily fit a large phone - Sizing is true-to-size; I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs and usually wear a size small or size 6 in Lululemon - Flattering booty seam with hidden scrunch offers just a little extra definition and doesn't look scrunchy when you're wearing it - No pilling of the fabric observed after wearing all day and washing once so far, but I'd say any fabric like this is delicate, so be careful around rough surfaces. Also, the scrunch hasn't lost it's scrunchiness after wearing all day. - Neat seams and no loose threads, overall quality feel to the fabric - I'm honestly surprised at how much longer my legs look in photos when I'm wearing these pants (Possible) Cons: - I have short legs despite being 5'5" and they're JUST the right length with sneakers that have a chunky sole. These pants have a 32" inseam and 31" might have been perfect on me. - These are mini-flared pants, so they might take some getting used to if you're more familiar with regular flared pants. I have 14" calves so I had mixed feelings about how snug they were on my calves at first, but they grew on me and I like them more now! I loved wearing these on a mini road trip and think they're great for any time you want to look cute and be comfortable. They'd also be fine for working out, but I usually stick with regular non-flared leggings for that.

Absolutely love these and the v cut ones as well. Perfect for working out or hanging around. Im 5'7 and 170lbs. I have a small waist and very curvy on the bottom. I have tried both the medium and the large both fit me... however, even with the medium and my smaller waist the mediums are uncomfortable. I have had this specific brand for over a year and these rock. Im tall so the ability to pick the inseam is perfect. My first pair i bought in 2024 has now made it to its last legs after being washed every 2 days and worn 4/7 days a week the thigh seems finally started busting however I would still choose these for everything. I walk 5miles a 4 days out of the week and work out heavily with them. I would still recommend these over a lot of other leggings. Buttery soft and if you are an itchy legging person who absolutely hates them these will be your prefered. I would give 5 stars if it wasn't for the inseam bursting after a year but they really have lived through a lot given all my pants burst at the thighs.

I LOVE the way these fit. I’m 5’9”, about 170 lbs and got the size medium and the 34” inseam. They are too long if you’re wearing them with flat shoes but I wear them with shoes that have 2”-3” platforms so they’re the perfect length. The only thing I don’t like is they snag and get fuzzy (from rubbing against stuff) VERY easily. I have 4 pairs of these and 3 of them have some sort of snag or scuff on them. I haven’t even had them a month yet so that is disappointing but otherwise I like them.

I wear these to work out and on those days when I just don’t feel like wearing jeans—ladies, you know exactly what I mean 😉 The fit is just right with amazing tummy control that’s both flattering and comfortable. I’m 5'7", and the length hits perfectly!

I very rarely make Amazon reviews but these jeans are just too good not to say something! They truly feel like real jean material, not see through, and are nice and thick to hide any cellulite- which I appreciate. With the colder months here I’m confident they are thick enough to keep me warm, and once the temps REALLY drop (since I live in the northern U.S.) I’m sure a pair of fleece lined leggings will fit underneath well. At the same time, they are also very stretchy and comfortable. I am 25 weeks pregnant, 5’6 and currently 197lbs (185 pre pregnancy) I typically carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I got the large and they fit perfectly and are stretchy enough to allow me room to grow. It feels amazing to try on a pair of jeans and just smile bc everything fits so well after so many weeks/months of feeling like a frumpy blob lol pre-pregnancy I was used to buying brands like Bridge by Glee and Flying Monkey, which are designed to really lift and flatter the booty. These jeans are definitely not *that* flattering on the butt, but they don’t do me a disservice in that area either. I’m just glad to have something that fits and is on trend at this point. Giving bonus points bc they have real front pockets too! These jeans are definitely worth the cost and are something I plan to keep to wear through future pregnancies as well. Overall, they function well and will serve their purpose well through the rest of my pregnancy. I imagine they will even be nice to wear in my post-partum period also. The light wash that I have here is great for casual, everyday wear and I’m going to go and buy a second pair in a darker wash as soon as I’m done writing this post. If you’re curvy with hips and thighs like me, don’t hesitate to buy these jeans. They’re perfect! P.S. ignore what appears to be discoloration in one of the pictures. I took these photos in dim lighting and I think my phone was just trying to auto-adjust to make the photo appear brighter. These jeans are not discolored :)

To start, the material feels pretty thick and durable. It sort of has that kind of swishy polyester sweatpants feeling/sound when you walk. They are not super breathable and feel a bit “heavy”. Aesthetically they look pretty good and I love the amount of pockets! Now the downsides. (For reference I am 5’10” and 190). I want to love these, especially because they are so affordable, but these pants were not made with butts in mind. The material has some stretch, however, the way the pants are cut does not allow enough room for anyone with a butt. To accommodate, the waist line is pulled way up in the front and way down in the back. It REALLY accentuates the little belly pouch that us women have. When sitting, you have to be really lucky (or skinny maybe) for your underwear/crack not to show and for the front of the waistband not to sever your internal organs. Pictures included. I bend and squat and sit on the floor in my job so these factors really impact my ability to move comfortably. I just really wanted a pink scrub set for Valentine’s Day and I guess I should have known better than choose a cheap set. At least the top is able to be worn comfortably! I included photos to show the waistline and how it fits both standing and (I’m sorry you have to see me like that lol) squatting. Hope this helps a fellow big booty judy!

Cute boots!!! For reference, I’m 5’9” and about 200 lbs and these fit perfectly. Usually a size 10, so that’s what I ordered and it was perfect. My calves are slightly bigger but these have a stretchy seam that isn’t too tight and even leaves room for thin leg warmers if I want. Right now, it’s too hot in Texas to wear but I am looking forward to wearing it in December-February. They have a 2 inch heel so it does make me taller but I’m totally fine with that. I’ve wanted brown boots for a while to complete my fall boot collection and I’m happy with these!

The pants themselves look much nicer than stretchy yoga pants. They do drape nicely from my waist. I’m a 31” inseam and as you can see, they are about 2” too long (great for tall women) The pants have a nice wide waist band with typical amount of give. If you prefer looser in the waist, you should consider up a size if unsure. The seat/thigh area has plenty of room but the overall pant lacks much stretch. The inside of the pants feel like the outside of the typical sweatshirt (smooth /soft) but not fuzzy. Back pockets are pretty much useless and there for decoration. The front slit pockets are a thinner material on the inside and will look pulled if you carry any extra weight in the front area.

These G4Free sweatpants are the first ones I will be wearing that first chilly fall day! The modal fabric is everything- substantial, silky smooth, sleek looking and wrinkle proof! There is a nice choice of inseam lengths. I probably could have used a 28” length but ordered 29” and I don’t mind them a little slouchy. For reference I am 5 feet tall 115 pounds.I love the more tailored, refined style of these sweatpants. The leg width is not overly wide, flattering for petites like me as well as talls. The pockets, both front and back, also lend a polished look if you wished to wear these as business casual attire. The waist band is high but lays flat. These pants may seem pricey, but I take into account the versatility. I would definitely wear them out for a casual dinner. Plus, all of the special features add up to a unique pair of slacks. Highly recommend these wide leg sweatpants and would definitely buy another pair from the wide selection of colors.

These pants may seem pricey, but I take into account the versatility. I would definitely wear them out for a casual dinner. Plus, all of the special features add up to a unique pair of slacks. Highly recommend these wide leg sweatpants and would definitely buy another pair from the wide selection of colors.

I am so impressed with these pants. The material feels beautifully soft and silky and the manufacturing quality is excellent. They have pockets in the front although I think pockets are unnecessary. I don’t like how you can see the outline of the interior pocket so I will probably sew up the pocket entrance to create faux pockets and then cut out the pocket lining. The back has faux pockets and they look cute. In terms of sizing, I ordered an XS with a 27” inseam. I’m 5”4 and 122lb and these fit exactly how I would expect an XS to fit. The pant leg length was perfect for me but I do have short legs so I would say for someone with average length legs, these would be too short on anyone taller than 5’2”. Overall, I am extremely happy with these pants and I plan to order another pair in a different color.

First the stats: I am almost 70, 5’4”, waist 27”, hips38”,inseam 29”, 112lbs. AI suggested size 8. I like my waist band a little loose because of a back issue. I probably should have gotten a 6. These are a little bit big; just a bit. I will definitely need to tuck in my shirt to help hold up the pants. I’ll probably use a belt as well. The rise comes even with my navel.They fit like a nice pair of slacks. I will be able to wear these when playing tour guide to visiting friends and I can wear a merino wool base layer underneath without feeling uncomfortable. I live in the Colorado mountains and it can get pretty cold. The length is what I like. The legs reach the top of my shoes. The fabric is very nice. Not thin. Pockets are a decent size and don’t show through the fabric. Rear pockets are fake but I don’t ever use those when pants have real ones. The sewn in crease makes them look dressy. I am satisfied with these pants and will keep them. If I find they are too big after wearing them for a while I will run a little bit of elastic through the waistband. I would recommend these pants if you want to look nice in cold weather.

These boots are very nice and I’m happy with the purchase. I ordered an 8.5 and they fit well. Normally I wear a size 8, but opted to size up due to having slightly wide feet, and boots tending to be more narrow. They’re sleek, flattering, and the kitten heel makes them easy to style with both dresses and skirts without being too tall for everyday wear. The only thing I’ll note is the height. I’m 5’5.5”, and on me, they sit about 1.5–2 inches below my knee, rather than the 4 inches shown on the model. Still, it’s a good look and adds a dressier touch. The material feels nice, great color, they don’t look cheap, and they shouldn’t require much break-in time. Overall, a stylish and versatile pair of boots that work well for both casual and more polished outfits.

Wow! These boots literally tell the truth and they go a good ways above my knee. They are very warm and fit well. They are super shaggy - the area at the bottom could use a little trimming as the hairs are a bit longer than necessary. The fur looks cheap at first glance, but the boots do look good on. It’s like wearing stuffed animals on your legs. When first putting on the Sweetheart Beauty thigh high shaggy fur boots, it was clear that the inside main part of the boots are made from either very thick cardboard or not-so-stiff interface/sizing; they bent a little. The second and third times I put them on, I was at houses where shoes must be removed. Actually, that means I’ve put them on 2x on those days. Putting the boots back on the last time I WORE them, but the 6th time I put them on, I heard a rip. Then the interface bent so much in both boots that my feet were not able to get all the way in to the bottom. Determined to wear these boots at least a couple more times, I figured I could use my boot horn to get back in and fix the interface. The boots are so tall, the boot horn wasn’t long enough! If this happens to you, push down the boot leg as far down as you can get it and flatten out the interface. NEVER pull on the boot from the top. Do it as carefully as you would pull on tights or hose. Back to the review. $99 is way too much for boots that have cheap faux fur (it’s called “fake” at this level) and zero arch support. Make sure the weather and the ground are dry before stepping out because you are wearing thick, furry socks with thick soles (but we knew that!) As of 12/31/25, they are on sale for $65 with a coupon. That should be that starting price point, and then some discounts for sales from there. I will continue to wear my boots until they fall apart. They get 2 stars because they are cute, temporary shoes that are way overpriced.

I am 5'3 and about 145lbs and the 6 short are perfect. I have decent curve to my backside and I love how these fit like a glove and don't leave that awkward gap at the back waist. Okay so I had originally planned to buy boots like this at a shoe store, but as a big foot and wide calf girly I had no luck.. ANYWHERE. I would say the comfortability of these boots is also really awesome the boot isn’t extremely tight or loose in the calf area which I really like. The material is also very nice and durable, these babies made it through lots of rocky roads in Mexico. All in all, I would highly recommend them.

I absolutely love these pants. I’m 5’8 on a good day and 220. I purchased 18 x 32. I’ve got thick thighs and a tummy that likes to flaunt itself when I really would like it not to. These pants I feel are very flattering to us plus sized beauties. I’m not a fan of boot cut pants but these are truly barely bootcut. They are extremely comfortable and wrinkle resistance. They have not shrunk in the several washes they’ve gone through. I have them in black, navy blue and red. I was a little hesitant to get the red, but I wanted a little color in my life and these just wowed me. One of my favorite pairs.

I bought these a year ago in a 2 short, which is supposed to be a 29” inseam. On that first pair I bought, the inseam is only 27”. Although they were a little too short I liked them because the pockets are large and the pants are form fitting yet items don’t bulge when inside them, they’re like, magic pockets - perfect for work. After a year the button popped off. Considered sewing it back on, but figured I needed a new pair anyway as they’ve been through a lot in the last year. Got another 2 short (a regular says 32” inseam which is way too long for me) ideally I’d like 29”, which is what is advertised. Got my new pair, and the inseam is 28”. Still an inch short of what they claim it is, but it is an inch longer than the pair I got last year so that’s an improvement. The material changed. My pair from last year, the fabric is thicker. I can feel more cotton in the pants, they are heavier, thicker when you pinch them. New pants feel like more nylon than cotton, thinner to the touch, lighter. The old ones feel like they were probably better quality however I actually prefer how they are now. I work in the hospitality industry and spend a lot of time running around a restaurant/kitchen which gets hot, and the thinner material will be more cool to work in I think. They still feel nice, just lost some weight. Great for work!

I bought these to wear for boarding day on an upcoming cruise to match some custom shirts I'm making. The first thing I was attracted to was the rich color. It is exactly as I'd hoped and true to the description. The color is vibrant and these are well made. The fabric is thick so I wouldn't advise wearing in warm weather. I sized down per the reviews and it worked for me. They are are a tad bit long on me even though I ordered short but I am only 4'11. I love how these hug me and the stretch is good enough. These look high end and I appreciate the tailoring. There are small things that stand out for me from other brands I sometimes come across like the fact that threads and buttons match the pants. These seem like they will last a while in my wardrobe. I totally recommend.

These pants are so comfortable! Amazon told me to order a 16, I ordered a 14 short and should exchange for a 12, but they are so comfortable I'm going to keep them. I have a 12 hour chaperone trip to NYC with high school students and need to look professional and stay comfortable and I believe these will be perfect. I will have to hem the length a little, but I'm used to that and just use some iron hem tape so I don't have to sew. I never tuck things in but did to show the waist. I like that the pockets don't gap but the might with a smaller size. The waist band is thick elastic and I wish they'd have skipped the zipper. The last picture shows how big my pair ran. I'm 100% apple shaped, 5'2" and 165 lbs and I wear my clothes loose.

Baggy jeans are in and as a 28 year old trying to keep up with the trends, these pants are my perfect balance of baggy and tight! I am 5 ft 6 in, around 120lbs, and bought the size 26 regular and they fit well. It sits a bit below the belly button, is form fitting on the butt, but loose down the leg. She wore them with white sneakers before washing, and by the end of the day they were stained blue!!

These are hands down my favorite wide-leg jeans right now. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 127 lbs, wearing a size 24, and the fit is excellent. They sit comfortably at the waist, are fitted through the hips and thighs, and then fall into the perfect wide leg. No pulling, no stiffness, and no awkward bunching. The fabric is SO soft with just the right amount of stretch. They feel broken-in right out of the package, which is rare for denim. I can wear these all day without feeling restricted, and they keep their shape really well. One of the biggest surprises is how flattering they are in the back. I have a flatter bottom (so I don’t say this lightly), and these actually give shape and lift without feeling tight or compressive. The soft black wash is rich and versatile, easy to dress up or down. I’ve worn them with boots, heels, and sneakers, and they work with everything. They instantly make an outfit look polished and intentional. If you’re looking for wide-leg jeans that are comfortable, flattering, and actually wearable for real life, these are a must-buy. I’d absolutely repurchase.

Updated Review (2025.12.09) - I ordered 3 pairs—dark wash, regular wash and khaki. Out of the 3, I kept the dark wash (pics 4&5) and returned the other 2. While the leg opening is larger than the waist, it’s not as extreme with the dark wash. I will say these are very comfortable and provide just the right amount of stretch, and did not stretch out at the end of the day. If you have a lower belly pooch, these will cover/slim you but if you eat anything, you’ll definitely feel these being tighter (due to hitting right below the belly button). I am 5’4”, 140lbs and the 26x32 fit like a glove, but I do need to either wear platform shoes or shoes with a small (1”) heal (I’m wearing wedges in pics 4&5), otherwise, they drag the ground. As much as I love wide leg jeans, these are giving me mid-90’s JNCO vibes. I love the fit as they have stretch, good fit in the waist and thighs and sits just below belly button but no plumbers crack); sadly, since the leg opening is wider than the waist, I am returning these.

I really like these jeans. I got them when they were on sale so I feel they were worth the price. The quality is not like a true Levi or a vintage pair but they feel like they will hold up overtime. I am 5 months postpartum so I enjoy the stretchiness. I am 5’5 and like 130-135 right now and got a 27. The length is perfect.

I’m 30 weeks with 4th baby, about 175lbs 5’5”. These pants are comfy. I ordered a L. I honestly probably would prefer a M, but I’m at the very end and I think a M would truly be too tight over these last 2 months. I think the L is a little bit loose on my belly but feels really good on my hips since they are pretty ready to go and have moved a ton. LOL. The belly fabric feels like butter but maybe not quite compressing enough to keep them from slipping down a little. I think a little elastic adjustment band at the top would help with this issue or a more compressing fabric. Everything else is super comfortable. I really enjoy the pockets. I do have concerns that there will be pilling on the insides of the legs based on the fabric, but it hasn’t happened yet. I couldn’t get my belly to stop casting shadows on the crotch area, but I will say that the way these are cut, and how the belly part is, there is possibility for camel toe, all in all, I like them, and I’m honestly just happy to have clothes that fit. Hallelujah!

I do have concerns that there will be pilling on the insides of the legs based on the fabric, but it hasn’t happened yet. I couldn’t get my belly to stop casting shadows on the crotch area, but I will say that the way these are cut, and how the belly part is, there is possibility for camel toe, all in all, I like them, and I’m honestly just happy to have clothes that fit. Hallelujah!

The material is heavenly - very soft and comfortable. Oh and pockets! Two side pockets and back pockets. In love! Funny how some pockets make everything better.I purchased these pants to replace some older straight leg trousers for workwear in my office. They are incredibly comfortable and flattering.Note the care tag indicates "no fabric softener."

I'm 6 foot tall with long legs and on the hefty side. finding big and tall pants is always a problem. When these pants arrived I tried them on and fell in love with them. the fit perfectly, the material is soft and stretchy, and they look professional enough to wear to work. I like them so much I immediately ordered a second pair. But my joy turned to disappointment after I put them on after the first wash and dry. they were now frustratingly just short enough to look and feel awkward. I laid them out ontop of the new unwashed pair and sure enough, they were about 2.5 inches shorter! So disappointing. I really hope the manufacturer reads this and does something to correct it in the future for all of us big and tall women out there struggling to find good clothes.If it were not for the shrinkage I would defintiely gives these pants 5 stars.

Since I work at a bank, my job requires business casual clothing (slacks or dress pants) but it’s been nearly impossible for me to find cute bootcut pants that aren’t insanely high waisted or weird yoga-pants or spandex material. Not to mention the inseam is usually really short for most women’s dress pants which looks weird on me. These pants are a nice mid-waist option that has a slight flare (and perfect inseam) that’s really flattering and versatile. Love that they also have 2 functioning front pockets. For reference, I am 5’6 and about 120-125 lbs and I ordered my typical size 4 which fit perfectly!! They’re really comfy and wash nicely too, just be aware that they wrinkle kinda easily but that’s expected. For the price, I really couldn’t be happier. :) Just wish there was more colors or patterns to choose from.

Love these, have them in 4 different colors now. I’m 5’10, 165lbs and ordered XL. They’re super soft, wash up well and because of the barrel leg style, they hit me at a good ankle length. I will say I ended up dying my brown pair darker (pictured) - I didn’t love the original rust shade. Also have in navy, black and grey - those are all great.

These are okay pants. They’re not exactly bad I just know I’m not going to want to live in them like I was hoping. They’re decently comfortable, I like the pockets, and there’s no issues with the material or construction. The fit just has some quirks that are hard to get past. First of all, they’re much shorter than the picture. I’m a little above average height and my inseam is typically 31-32” so it will fit shorter girls better I just can’t get behind it for me. There’s also just a strangeness to the hip area. It feels too tight in some areas and too loose in others. Again, may just be a body type issue but for me it didn’t work (33” waist, 42” hip). I think these are really just meant to be comfy, loose cargo corduroys for smaller skinner girls so if that’s you then go for it.

These sweat pants are super cute. They're soft and fit well. I'm usually a US Women's Medium or size 8 (5'6", 140ish pounds) and that's what I ordered here. The fit is as expected, but I would have liked a slightly looser elastic on the waist so I could roll them down comfortably. I'm not a high waist girly, never have been. All that said, I'm a little disappointed that they give more of a straight leg look than the barrel leg style I was hoping for. They have the seams like a trad barrel leg jean, but there really isn't much more volume to the legs where you'd expect. Still cute, but a more standard look than I'd wanted.

These exceeded my expectations because of how thick they were, a lot warmer than I anticipated which is great for winter. Not see through at all for white pants, they’re also a true white color. I’m 5’5 120lbs and the small fit perfectly in terms of length and waist size.

I recently picked up these brown corduroy pants in size medium, and they’re an absolute delight! The corduroy is of exceptional quality—medium thick with deep, velvety grooves that give it a soft, flexible texture and a gorgeous sheen that’s even richer in person than in the photos. At 5’1” with a 28-29” waist, the high-waisted, wide-leg design fits beautifully, reaching my ankles for a relaxed yet stylish look. The elastic drawstring waist adds comfort, and the six pockets are a practical bonus, perfect for carrying essentials. The fit is oversized and relaxed. The stitching is sturdy and well-crafted, suggesting these pants will hold up through plenty of wear. Planning to pair them with oversized peplum blouses and sweaters, they transition effortlessly from casual outings to slightly dressier moments. The brown color has a warm, luxurious vibe that’s ideal for fall. My only note is that the wide legs can feel a bit voluminous on me, but they’re so comfortable and chic that I don’t mind. Great value.

These jeans are as described with a couple caveats. Amazon recommends size 20 for me based on my past purchases but these jeans are too big around the waist and I’m a thick girl. The inseam length is as stated in the description. My other area of concern is the ‘front rise’ the description said 9.5” but these measured 10.5”. These are not a true mid rise jean. They come up above my belly button. I’ll keep them and alter them after washed but I would recommend if you’re short-waisted that you look for a different jean if it’s important to you. Also, consider ordering down one size. I’m 5’5” and 205# my true waist measurement is 44”. Hope this review helps.

Well so far from opening the bag and unfolding these jeans and putting them on I was falling in love with each step. Like most women here I'm sure buying clothes online is so stressful and what if , but we have such desires and hopes. Most online models don't always look like every woman and we have to read all these dang reviews . Well I'm 5'7 and probably between 190-210 maybe... haven't weighed myself in a long time. I never post pictures of what I buy.. but these I just had to because they are great!

These dress pants are the most comfortable I’ve had in decades! They fit beautifully and feel amazing on. At 5’9”, I opted for the 32-inch length, which is perfect for my height. I’m always hesitant to order pants online because of my height and weight distribution, but these are a definite hit, and I am so glad that I got them. I just find them to be really attractive. I tend to carry my weight in my hips and thighs, and the material has a slimming effect that I really appreciate. The fabric is a blend of nylon and Lycra, which I believe makes them so comfortable. They also have two convenient front pockets and two artificial pockets in the back, adding to the style without compromising the sleek fit. I absolutely love these pants and know they’ll get plenty of wear. I’m even planning to bring them on my upcoming European trip because they’re easy to pack without wrinkles and how versatile they are. They can easily be dressed up with heels for a polished look or dressed down with flats, and even dark sneakers for a more casual vibe. These pants are a true wardrobe staple, suitable for any occasion.

I am a huge fan of these and other G4Free Yoga Pants! This is my 3rd pair (all different styles), and they have never let me down. The fabric is soft and light, and there are no buttons or zippers or tags that would irritate someone with skin sensory issues, and they do well in the washer and dryer. I have some belly fat that other pants seem to emphasize, but not these G4Free Yoga Pants. I love them.

They don’t fall down or bunch up in the crotch, and the belly panel is plenty stretchy and not compressive. Inseam is perfect for my height. Fabric is soft. I’m 5’6. I wear 7.5 in all shoes. But 8 in boots with a heel. . I have a narrow foot, really skinny ankles and calves. Purchased 7.5 becausee most reviews said it ran a little big. With socks on it was a little snug but boots stretched a tad and they got more comfortable by the end my night. Wore for eight hours at a concert. Danced & walked so much. Zero blisters. My toes were a little sore the next morning but normal for first time wear. There was a deep crease down the center/front of the boot. I blow dried on a low setting to soften the material then put a 5 lb weight on it to flatten for a hour or so to get crease out. It worked a lil bit. They did scrunch up a tiny bit by the ankle (you can in in photos). Honestly the only negative about the boots. But overall they’re great quality boots. They are so so so affordable! I own $200+ boots and I swear these boots are neck n neck. Plan on getting different colors!
