These faux leather boots are great. I ordered the brown and it's a really nice chocolate brown color. I also ordered my normal size and often in boots I order a half size large as I have wide-ish feet and want to accommodate socks. I'm glad I stuck with my normal size in these as they fit great. I don't have any rubbing, socks fit great (thin and medium socks), and there's no pinching around the toe box. I'm pretty short at 5'3" and have an average to large-ish calf and these fit easy with no issues. The zipper in the back was easy to pull up and down. So, getting them on and off was simple. There was plenty of space in the calf and they go up to about my knees. Inside the boot shaft is a different material which is really nice against the skin. I'm guessing it's a polyester of some sort. I like the flat-ish heel so it's very comfortable to walk around. I live in a walking city, and these will work well. I'm still wearing sandals out as it's still warm in my area but look forward to the fall season. I will say, the shaft is very floppy and when it arrives it will be folded over in the duster so I will probably invest in some tall boot inserts to help keep its shape. Nevertheless, these are great boots. Comfortable, wide calf [somewhat] friendly and the sole is a rubber texture so you're not slipping around on different walking surfaces.

I'm pretty short at 5'3" and have an average to large-ish calf and these fit easy with no issues. The zipper in the back was easy to pull up and down. So, getting them on and off was simple. There was plenty of space in the calf and they go up to about my knees. Inside the boot shaft is a different material which is really nice against the skin. I'm guessing it's a polyester of some sort. I like the flat-ish heel so it's very comfortable to walk around. I live in a walking city, and these will work well. I'm still wearing sandals out as it's still warm in my area but look forward to the fall season. I will say, the shaft is very floppy and when it arrives it will be folded over in the duster so I will probably invest in some tall boot inserts to help keep its shape. Nevertheless, these are great boots. Comfortable, wide calf [somewhat] friendly and the sole is a rubber texture so you're not slipping around on different walking surfaces.

These are nice looking pants. The material feels nicer than what it is, not flimsy and not see through. I really like the color - a warm brown.They fit really well at the waist. I’m 5’7” 120lbs, with a 26”waist. The pants are a tad too short, they are not crop length either. They would work nicely with flats but that’s about it. When I put on sneakers that tend to have a thicker sole, I’d need the pants to be about an .5 -1 inch longer. Forget about wearing these with heels, way too short. There’s about 1/2 inch that can be unhemmed if needed, but is it worth the time/$$?However, they did cut some corners with the construction, for example the seams on the hem are typically invisible on dressy pants, however on these, they didn’t bother with that level of detail and just sewed a basic stitch on the hem all the through which makes it look cheap and low quality. All my other dress pants from Ann Taylor, Bebe, Cache, even old Express pants all have invisible hems which is a much cleaner look.Second issue is with the waistband tab - the tab once closed sticks out quite a bit which it is not supposed to do that. It seems that here again whoever made these pants was not a professional because on a waistband tab that’s as wide the one on these pants, they should have used two hook + bar closures instead of just one. Plus, the hook + bar closures should have been placed closer to the end of the tab as well to prevent it from sticking out. Again comparing to my existing pants that have the same waistband closure detail, these are clearly “cheaply” made and it shows.

The flower pattern featured on this set of shoes is classically adorable. I normally wear a size 8.5 in all shoe brands. Since there are no half sizes available, I decided to order a size up to be safe. I ordered the size 9 for my 8.5 feet and I will say that it seemed to be a perfect fit. The shoes run a little bit more narrow than most of my other shoes. They were very snug around the width of my feet. I ordered the short version of these boots which covers a small portion of my calf muscles. I have extremely thick legs, but the circumference of the boots is big enough to accommodate my thick calves. The material and build of the boots is waterproof. I tested this out by walking through puddles of water after it had rained outside. The boots did an excellent job at keeping my feet dry. For very heavy storms, I would recommend the tall version of the boots. If I were to splash around a lot, it’s possible that water would go up into the inside of the boots with the short version of this product. There are adjustable straps to tighten or loosen around the calf muscle. I find the shorter height to be extremely cute. The boots are fashionable, functional, and comfortable. I do wish the width was a bit bigger, but my feet still do fit in snuggly. The current price of $70 seems like a fair value for the money based on the quality and usefulness of the item.

I have been looking & looking for a pair of lounge pants to fit my athletic build in a comfortable way. I am 5’8, 150lbs with a decent sized booty & thicker thighs. I literally have been trying on pants for a year, & leave the stores so depressed feeling like something is wrong with my body shape b/c no pants ever fit me right! I am so happy with the fit of these around the booty, in the thighs, AND finally—there is enough length!! I purchased a medium. Bonus: they are 100% cotton, so goodbye static cling! (Full disclosure: I haven’t washed these yet, so I’m holding my breath they don’t shrink terribly, but so far, I’m VERY happy. Finally.) Update 6/1/2020: These did shrink in the dryer a bit but not terribly. They went from a perfect fit to just a decent fit upon washing. They probably shrunk 3/4 of an inch & got a little tighter in the booty which I didn't want.

**for reference i am 5’0, typically size XS/S, 24/25” waist, 34C, and 98 lbs*** okay, these were my first Sunzel purchases and they SCREAM high quality!!! i’ll admit- i’ve purchased a lot of amazon active wear that left me wanting more, but these checked ALL the boxes for me. i absolutely love that there is options for short girlies and the tall girlies!! as a shorter gal, it’s REALLY hard to find pants and leggings that i don’t have to take to get altered or they drag on the ground….these fit like they were made for me?! the material is SO buttery soft but thick in a good way and that waistband has just the right amount of compression to snatch you in!! Oh and for my gym girlies, they’re squat proof and not see through 🤭 I would recommend these a million times over, will DEFINITELY be purchasing more✨

I have two pairs and I am quite satisfied with them. I purchased one in gray a long time (years) ago, and the navy just 2 weeks ago. I'm not sure but the materials feel different to me. The gray is softer, body-hug, while the navy is cooler, straight-fit, still a little body-hug. Pros: 1. versatile for casual and formal enough for office wear. I always want multi-setting functional items like this. 2. Good for a petite body. I'm 5 ft, 102 lbs, with a short torso and prefer mid-high waisted (10 in from belly button to crotch). I got XXS, it fits well generally. Cons: 1. Weird shape at the crotch. It may or may not be a camel toe. To me, it's more like the extra fabric formed to one side and gives a weird look at the crotch. Darker color (navy) helps to hide this. 2. Wide ankles 3. Will pill over time on areas around the pockets and butts

I got XXS, it fits well generally. Cons: 1. Weird shape at the crotch. It may or may not be a camel toe. To me, it's more like the extra fabric formed to one side and gives a weird look at the crotch. Darker color (navy) helps to hide this. 2. Wide ankles 3. Will pill over time on areas around the pockets and butts

I bought the dark wash and I’m honestly really impressed. For reference, I’m 5’3”, weigh about 161 lbs, and I’m 50 years old. I’ve always struggled to find jeans that fit properly without making me look bigger than I already feel. I have a short torso and longer legs, so jeans are usually a challenge for me. I’m currently in a weight-loss phase, exercising and watching what I eat, so I didn’t want to invest in a whole new wardrobe. I just needed a few pieces that fit now and actually look appropriate and flattering. I’m also very picky and self-conscious about how I look, so it took a lot for me to even post vid/photos, but I’m hoping this helps someone else in the same boat. I ordered a Size 10, and these fit EXACTLY how I wanted. They’re amazingly comfortable, well-proportioned, and don’t dig in. I even had leggings on underneath (it was cold today), and they still fit comfortably no struggling, no holding my breath. That alone sold me. The color is great, and while the material isn’t “Levi’s-level premium”, these jeans are far from cheap or scratchy. They’re soft, comfy, and feel like good everyday jeans. For the price, the quality is more than fair. Also follow the care instructions. These should be washed in cold water and dried on a low setting. If you take care of them properly, they hold up really well. A lot of fit and fabric complaints come down to not reading the care label. Since these are discontinued colors, I’ll probably grab one or two more pairs, but that’s it. I’m not building a full wardrobe while I’m losing weight. For context, I was 167 lbs at the end of November and hadn’t bought anything for myself until now, so I’m really happy these fit and did more than just “the job.” If you’re shorter, curvy, or in between sizes and looking for something comfortable and flattering without breaking the bank, these are definitely worth trying.

5'7", 115 lobs, 28 w 30" inseam; bought small/long and they fit me perfectly. Love the color and slightly "worn" look - excellent fabric weight that is not at all transparent. Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

Very Comfy with Stretch but causes excessive wrinkling on my legs. I have somewhat thin legs but the pants still bunched up. Its worse when I've been sitting and stand up. If I pull the pants up so that they sit very high just above my belly button, the stretch isn't as bad but as you can see in the second photo, that will cause a camel toe, so I need to wear a long top over them. Even with all that, I have to say they are very comfortable and will wear them around the house and with tall boots. I'm 5'3" about 138lbs and purchased a regular medium and the length and waist are otherwise perfect.

y'all...I'm pretty picky with my jeans, but was pleasantly surprised by these. They aren't luxury jeans, they aren't thick, and aren't going to be your sturdiest jeans...but they are going to make you feel good and they will be comfortable. They're not too tight, not too loose, not too anything. The waist is my favorite because it's a high waist without being unflattering in the girly regions (im talking no knuckles of the moose). Only warning I can give is if you're a tugger, again these aren't the sturdiest so they'll rip where you tug if not careful(see picture); But they're sturdy enough to enjoy and pretty forgiving. I'm actually going to purchase more since they're the only jeans I've enjoyed since I gained weight (currently 5'4, 180, with thick thighs and thickish booty. these are size 12). I plan to make more purchases as I lose the weight as well, to have a set on hand. Again, pleasantly surprised...and recommend if you're good with a basic, comfortable skinny jean. The color was just right as well (these are the medium wash[i will be trying others...to be continued...but yay for now]

Unexpectedly rather like these pants. They fit really well, though I typically don’t like a pull on style, and are super stretchy. No pinching at the waist, and the inseam is long enough for me but doesn’t drag. Often dress pants can be too long on me (5’4”). The material isn’t a dressy dress pants / trousers style. It’s def more of a light legging feel including the stretch. When I opened these up I really thought that would be a turn off but tbh it’s not. They are not a business formal look but for everyday or slightly dressed up they are a solid choice and super comfortable. The pockets are functional but they are not placed in a way that you’d want to use them for most things. It’s like a tailored legging so you’re not sacrificing the movability of a legging while adding a bit more polish. Nice balance

These pants are comfortable and provide quite a bit of stretch for easy on/off. The faux front buttons and pockets elevate these above a simple straight leggings type look yet provides the comfort of a legging. The length is perfect for a 5'4" female with a 30 inseam. The material does not feel or look cheap and could easily be worn in a professional setting.

These are very stretchy, like leggings. They are decently thick, so they probably won't show small lumps and bumps like some leggings. I was wanting to wear these with a satin button down shirt to the office, but I think the stretchy snugness isn't going to let them be office attire. They are true to size. I am only 5'3, but wear a 32" inseam and I would have to wear these with flats due to the length. The fabric is soft and feels good on my skin. Overall, I'll keep these and definitely wear them to run errands on the weekends, but they aren't going to cut it for business casual attire, and I most definitely would not wear them to my executive team meetings.

I am so happy with these boot cut leggings! The fit is absolutely amazing — super comfortable, with just the right amount of flare at the bottom. It gives that stylish look without being overly dramatic or wide. I wear a lot of platform shoes (even my sneakers), and the 32.5" length is perfect for my 5'7" frame. I love leggings that give me a choice of lengths, so these did not disappoint! What really impressed me is that I'm actually a bit larger than the largest size I ordered, and they still fit me like a glove — no need to size up at all. When I first pulled them out of the package, they looked small, and I was nervous, but the stretch is fantastic. If you’re a bit over the size chart measurements for the largest size, these should still work for you. Bonus points for the hidden waistband pocket (perfect for a credit card or key), and the tummy control is excellent without being restrictive. Yes, they’re on the pricier side, but the quality absolutely justifies the cost. These feel high-end and look so flattering. Look, my queens, you work hard for your money and slay all day... so, you deserve to treat yourselves! Love, love, LOVE! I'm definitely getting these in black as well. Absolutely recommend! Note: The lighting I used for my review pics was crazy bright, so the color looks off. These are definitely a rich, espresso brown and match the listing's pics.

I am 5'8'', 145 pounds and I ordered a Large. As you can see, the pants are designed to be curve-hugging, with abdominal control and a flare bottom. The pants are as advertised: form-fitting. They are true-to-size if you accept the idea they are designed to be tight. The material will flow with you whether walking or just lounging around. I did a few knee squats and the pants stayed in place. The length works with shoes; in bare feet, the bottoms are long. The material is soft, strong and flexible. The workmanship is excellent with strong seams and accents to support your abdomen and lift your butt. The material feels "weighty", hopefully maintaining its shape for many outings. I would say that the pants are flattering but that's an opinion best left to the owner. I am a bit modest, so I will be wearing them with an oversize sweater or with a jacket. Even at the gym, I would most likely wear a large sweatshirt. At around $40, the pants are priced to sell -- and a good value.

They are true-to-size if you accept the idea they are designed to be tight. The material will flow with you whether walking or just lounging around. I did a few knee squats and the pants stayed in place. The length works with shoes; in bare feet, the bottoms are long. The material is soft, strong and flexible. The workmanship is excellent with strong seams and accents to support your abdomen and lift your butt. The material feels "weighty", hopefully maintaining its shape for many outings. I would say that the pants are flattering but that's an opinion best left to the owner. I am a bit modest, so I will be wearing them with an oversize sweater or with a jacket. Even at the gym, I would most likely wear a large sweatshirt. At around $40, the pants are priced to sell -- and a good value.

These pants are buttery soft and remind me of a more expensive popular brand! These are well made, comfortable and has a great price point. Im 5'4 with 1 24.5 inch waist and these fit perfectly! The are flattering and squat proof so they are not see through at all! I ordered a size small

These cotton pants are great! They stretch, hold their shape and are flattering. I wore them on a 17 day trip to Scotland and England. I have purchased 2 black, Gray, Maroon and Green. I’m waiting for the Navy to come back in stock. They wash well in cold water, and I really like that they come in shorter lengths. I’m 5’4” 165 lbs. I purchased the XL. These are by far my favorite pants! I wear them everywhere. They are thick enough to not show through when you move.

I am so glad I ordered these HOUMOUS pants! They are classic looking and the fabric is of substantial weight because it is 95% cotton, 5% spandex. I would definitely wear these to travel because they are not prone to wrinkling from sitting. The khaki is a warm camel colored shade, which will go with just about any color in your wardrobe. I like the wide waistband which is very flattering. I can’t help but love that these were available in a 28 inch seam length. Yay, no trip to the tailor! For reference I am 5 feet tall, 115 pounds and the small is perfect, no gapping pockets in front. The rear pockets are unusually low, but they lay flat, so no problem. I consider them more of a detail vs a functional pocket with this positioning. The straight leg on these is not overly wide so they will not add pounds to your frame or soon fall out of fashion soon.There is literally nowhere these yoga type pants would not be appropriate. Cotton fabric makes them year round perfect! Highly recommend these and I will definitely re-order in another color this Fall!

These have many color options, I have about 7 pairs. These are a nice thick material and have pockets that are big enough to hold my phone. They are thick enough to be comfortable in the winter time but not too much that I'm uncomfortably hot while running around the house cleaning. I got a bit concerned about wearing sweatpants all the time. I'm a stay at home mom and I want to be comfortable but I also don't want my husband looking over from his office thinking I've given up and am not trying anymore. These are easy to match with a t-shirt, sweater or something a little dresser and I feel put together. It's easy to run out of the house and do quick errands, go to an appointment or the grocery store or drop something off at the school for the kiddos. I wouldn't wear these to go out with friends or a date night but they are suitable for more relaxed office wear and I love them for traveling. These pants are supper stretchy but are tight enough so they do not slide down through out the day. I am 5'6" and depending on if I'm working out or not I am between 122-130 pounds. It's great that these fit me well no matter how I'm doing on my eating and exercise. I got a small tall because after these are washed and dried they do shrink up a bit so you can't really judge the fit until that first wash is done. Hope this helps!

Fit is too petite! Also material is not nearly as stretchy as expected like other scrubs or my other pair of Healing Hand joggers (different style). Rise from crotch to waistband is shorter than expected, like a low rise, and leg length is very short. Pulled taut to full length, the ankle band does not reach even the top of my ankles, fit like capri pants ending at the calves. Quality is good, good pockets, and these would be great for someone who is short, like 5’4” or shorter. When I squat, it’s bringing plumber vibes. Returning bc unable to wear. For reference, I’m 5’7”, size 10, athletic build, and purchased a size small bc my other HH joggers (diff style number) in size medium are too big.

My fiance has worn these so they are a tad dirty. These are very sharp looking and quite flatering. They seem well made, high quality, warm and durable, the pockets aren't very deep though. I love the different range of sizes, that being said I wish they didnt just offer tall or short but also long or short torso for a tall or short person. Im 5'6 and 125 with a sorta athletic build for reference, i put them on to show were they sit, In the 2nd photo im wearing them with the straps fully extended(they need to be to bend and sit comfortably) and the top inseam part sits a bit low for comfort on me, in the 3rd photo i have them held up(I didnt shorten the straps just held them up i probably could shorten the straps and be comfortable as my torso is shorter than my fiances is) to were pants would typically comfortably sit on you. The torso could probably be a few inches longer to accommodate for movement for those with either a larger chest size or longer torso. I believe I went small for my fiance who is 5'4, 130lbs, she has a longer torso but has got a narrower waist. A medium definitely would have been too big everywhere besides the torso length. The small fits good but for comfort in all activities, an option for a longer torso would definitely be ideal. Highly recommend but definitely recommend checking the reviews and size chart before deciding on what size and length.

I bought these in a panic the other day because I work at a barn out doors and I seen all the hype of the super winter storm and the negative some odd degrees. These came fast as all get out. They fit great which is awesome considering I’m a 5ft tall girl with lots of curves and can never find bottoms that fit right. So anywho. I wore these outside in the negative whatever’s while I worked for 5 hours without the parts of my body in the jumper getting cold. I proceeded to leave my job and drive into a small snow ditch (on accident) where I spent another 2 hours outside. Went out of the car to try and dig it out. Sat in the 7 or so inches of snow questioning all my life choices and had a good cry. My legs never got wet or cold. So here I am to say- hands down worth every penny and then some cause Mother Nature would have destroyed me today without them.
This is nice and warm- fits good happy I was able to exchange it for the medium long. I’m 5”7 and weight 150 it’s perfect. I wear a decent size hoodie under it and there’s still room.

QUALITY AND WARMTH: The days of freezing during winter farm chores are finally behind me. These are great quality bibs…like a winter coat for your legs. They’re comfortable and keep me warm in the coldest of Midwest temperatures. Today’s wind chill was -17 and they kept me warm while I worked in my chicken run. Keep in mind that the quilted insulated lining only goes up to the hips. This is ok with me considering I layer up with sweatshirts and coats on my top half. They are thick and durable, but are also easy to move around in. They stretch and move with me when I bend and crouch down, making them easy to work in. The straps and fasteners are easily adjustable and have stayed in place so far. I love that I can undo the hip buttons to fit a thick hoodie tucked in underneath. They don’t seem to be completely waterproof. Water resistant..yes, but I wouldn’t use them as snow pants. SIZING: I am 5’5” and 155-160lbs. Kind of a mom bod, small chest and wide hips. My bust is 35”, my waist is 33” and my hips are 44”. I ordered a size large and they fit perfectly. Due to other reviews, I almost bought a medium but there is no way they would have fit. Large is perfect. They felt a little tight in the hips at first but they quickly stretched on the first day as I broke them in…very similar to a pair of jeans. I wear fleece lined leggings underneath and have even worn a pair of pajama pants underneath and they still fit well. I wouldn’t consider these a loose fit in particular, at least not for my body type. But they are definitely still relaxed enough to be comfortable. The short length is perfect for my height. They look a tad long in my picture because I’m in my socks, but with my boots on they’re perfect. All around, I definitely am happy with my purchase. I don’t think I have anything negative to say about these bibs. They’ve been great to me so far. I recommend them to any woman in need of keeping warm while working outdoors.

QUALITY AND WARMTH: The days of freezing during winter farm chores are finally behind me. These are great quality bibs…like a winter coat for your legs. They’re comfortable and keep me warm in the coldest of Midwest temperatures. Today’s wind chill was -17 and they kept me warm while I worked in my chicken run. Keep in mind that the quilted insulated lining only goes up to the hips. This is ok with me considering I layer up with sweatshirts and coats on my top half. They are thick and durable, but are also easy to move around in. They stretch and move with me when I bend and crouch down, making them easy to work in. The straps and fasteners are easily adjustable and have stayed in place so far. I love that I can undo the hip buttons to fit a thick hoodie tucked in underneath. They don’t seem to be completely waterproof. Water resistant..yes, but I wouldn’t use them as snow pants. SIZING: I am 5’5” and 155-160lbs. Kind of a mom bod, small chest and wide hips. My bust is 35”, my waist is 33” and my hips are 44”. I ordered a size large and they fit perfectly. Due to other reviews, I almost bought a medium but there is no way they would have fit. Large is perfect. They felt a little tight in the hips at first but they quickly stretched on the first day as I broke them in…very similar to a pair of jeans. I wear fleece lined leggings underneath and have even worn a pair of pajama pants underneath and they still fit well. I wouldn’t consider these a loose fit in particular, at least not for my body type. But they are definitely still relaxed enough to be comfortable. The short length is perfect for my height. They look a tad long in my picture because I’m in my socks, but with my boots on they’re perfect. All around, I definitely am happy with my purchase. I don’t think I have anything negative to say about these bibs. They’ve been great to me so far. I recommend them to any woman in need of keeping warm while working outdoors.

Welp, here I am again. Wrote a similar review on a similar pair of wide leg yoga pants only a couple weeks ago. Sigh! Here’s the verdict- these are overall cute yoga pants with good options on color and inseam length. They could really use a size XS though, the size small was just a touch big for me (jeans size 24/25 waist). My real gripe here is the lack of description of the waistband and the actual waistband. I hate HATE having the little elastic band at the top of the waist in leggings/yoga pants. It is not comfy, not flattering. I want a nice wide seamless band that lays flat. I read every word of this item’s description and could not find a clear answer. I then read all the reviews, fruitless venture. I then posted my question to the Amazon community, fruitless venture. Answers were literally like 4 people said no, doesn’t have the elastic and 2 said yes. Baffled by this, I decided to just give them a shot on a lightning deal sale. They arrived today and are going back. I decided to post this review to help anyone who shares the strong hatred of skinny elastic and I decided to include a photo to help show the Amazon community what it is. (I should note that I bought the khaki color and because this is Amazon I cannot rule out the possibility that some colors may have it and others may not. I will not be purchasing 8 pairs in order to find out).

I like these pants. I generally wear size 6/S but I sized up to a M and they fit well. The waist is not super stretchy but does have enough give to feel comfortable. I’m 5’8 with long legs and the 33” length is right where I like it to hit. The only things I don’t love about the pants is that they do look very casual in person. There’s no dressing these up, they are purely lounge pants. They also aren’t great at obscuring bulges and things. Even the outline from the pockets is pretty visible.

I really like these pants! Ordered a Large to begin with, but DANG! BEWARE! They run SMALL! For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & apx. 140lbs with 30" waist & 40" hips & the large was SNUG in the waist! For better comfort I went with the XL & I'm happier! LOVE the length! That was one of the BIGGEST reasons I went with these! The reviews said they WERE longer, and they ARE! LOVE that I can wear heels! YEAH! Only issue I have is the material! It's more a thinner cheap polyester so I took off a star for that! Would have expected, for the price, for them to have been a higher grade material! The pics made me think they were too! But, all and all, I like them & will keep them! LOVE the pockets! Just be aware that if you want that look they show in the pictures, I'd go up TWO sizes! You can tell from the pics, even with XL they are a wee on the tighter side showing ALL! So, I think I'll be adding some shape wear!! But, for the price and the LENGTH I recommend them!! Hope all this help!! Thanks much! ENJOY!

This is my second purchase of these pants. I have them in yellow. I came back to write a review this time because they are worth the hype and priced reasonably. I’m 5’4 and 112lbs. Usually wear a 0/XS I got a small, since that was the smallest they had and I feel they are TTS. I'm 5'1" and wear a size 7. I purchased a 7 and they fit my feet just fine. Unfortunately, there is no stretch in the calf. My calves are rather....shapely. I wish that I could convey to you how soft these bad boys are. You know when you have a pair of jeans that are getting almost threadbare in some places because they’re so worn in they don't even feel like the thick jeans material anymore? That’s what these feel like - only they aren’t threadbare, just soft. These don’t even feel like jeans to me at all. They’re stretchy from the top to your bottom, then they get loose and comfy from the legs down. If they feel a bit tight at first, just give it a minute, because they will conform to you. The fit is a little higher on the waist than I typically like, and I kept trying to pull them down more on my hips, but they’d just creep back up 😆. They have real pockets on the back AND front, which is nice because we sometimes have fake pockets on the front. There IS a fake zipper since these are pull on pants. I am 5’1,” about 120 and I ordered the XS. It’s possible that I should’ve ordered the small, but these still fit really well. They are a bit tighter on my bottom and around the thighs in the back than I’d like, but my husband thought they looked really good. When I ordered these, the petite wasn’t an option, nor was the dark color - both of which I would normally get. I purchased the blue, which is a bit lighter than any other jeans that I own, and which also is a bit more unforgiving in my opinion. However, I’m really glad I got them. I think once I get to my usual weight of about 107-110, I will, personally, feel more comfortable in them. They will still be a tad long, though, which I’m totally used to. 😁. However, if you are the same size as me, they will absolutely fit you - it’s more a “me” thing than a “not fitting” thing.

I wish that I could convey to you how soft these bad boys are. You know when you have a pair of jeans that are getting almost threadbare in some places because they’re so worn in they don't even feel like the thick jeans material anymore? That’s what these feel like - only they aren’t threadbare, just soft. These don’t even feel like jeans to me at all. They’re stretchy from the top to your bottom, then they get loose and comfy from the legs down. If they feel a bit tight at first, just give it a minute, because they will conform to you. The fit is a little higher on the waist than I typically like, and I kept trying to pull them down more on my hips, but they’d just creep back up 😆. They have real pockets on the back AND front, which is nice because we sometimes have fake pockets on the front. There IS a fake zipper since these are pull on pants. I am 5’1,” about 120 and I ordered the XS. It’s possible that I should’ve ordered the small, but these still fit really well. They are a bit tighter on my bottom and around the thighs in the back than I’d like, but my husband thought they looked really good. When I ordered these, the petite wasn’t an option, nor was the dark color - both of which I would normally get. I purchased the blue, which is a bit lighter than any other jeans that I own, and which also is a bit more unforgiving in my opinion. However, I’m really glad I got them. I think once I get to my usual weight of about 107-110, I will, personally, feel more comfortable in them. They will still be a tad long, though, which I’m totally used to. 😁. However, if you are the same size as me, they will absolutely fit you - it’s more a “me” thing than a “not fitting” thing.

I ordered these jeans, not knowing what to expect and not really having high hopes for pull on jeans. I also tend to have issues with "high waisted" pants. Being 5'3", a lot of high rise jeans come up WAY too high and look terrible on me. When I first saw these, they kind of reminded me of maternity jeans, but I tried them on anyway. Wow! Was I pleasantly surprised! They are actually way cuter than I thought they would be. The XS fits perfectly. I got the standard size, and I probably should have gotten the petite/short size, but now I have jeans that look good with my platform sandals. I think I will buy another pair in a different color in the short size to wear with sneakers. I like that if you fold the waistband down, they are suddenly mid rise jeans, so there are two ways to wear them. These are going to be my new travel jeans. How perfect will they be for air travel with no zippers, buttons, or belts to worry about as you go through security? These also looked great after throwing in the washer and dryer, so no special care was needed. Overall, a great pair of jeans to add to your wardrobe!

I'm 5'4" and ordered a size Small. They fit really well—just a bit long, but once I put on shoes, the length is perfect. The jeans have a great balance of comfort and style; they’re flattering without being too tight or restrictive. The fabric feels soft but still structured enough to hold its shape. I also like how they stretch a little for an even better fit. Overall, a versatile and comfortable everyday pair—I’ve already reached for them multiple times so that's a good sign! I'd recommend these!

The reviews are all true… you NEED these jeans. They’re as comfy as sweats or leggings but actually look like jeans. So many of the sweats jeggings don’t have actual pockets or the ones they have are just printed on. But these actually have pockets! They’re incredible. I’m 5’7, 135lbs and the small fits great. They are the perfect amount of wide leg/baggy without looking sloppy which is a line I find most baggy jeans to cross. I wore them to work all day and they didn’t stretch out or start sagging either. Love them. Seriously, just buy them if you’re considering… you will not regret it!

These tall boots are perfect for upcoming cooler weather. They reach all the way to the knee. I am 5'10" tall and these are plenty long enough for my legs. The bottoms are patterned, which provides great grip and helps prevent slipping and sliding. The quality is great. The zippers move smoothly without catching. The material is a faux leather look and super easy to clean. The low heel provides stability. There is plenty of room in the toe box, thanks to the rounded style of the toe. The boots are very comfortable to wear, and there is plenty of cushioning on the inside. These will look great with leggings or jeans. These are a great value for the price!

Boots are exactly as pictured. Very comfortable and easy to walk in. If you’re not used to heels I wouldn’t recommend walking for hours in these until they are broken in a little. For sizing I normally wear an 8 in boots and sneakers. 7.5 for heels, flats and slides. I got these in a size 7.5 based off another review and they fit perfectly. They are a little tight/stiff on the top of my foot when I bend my foot to walk but I think that will loosen up after a few wears. I have a 14” calf and they fit well with a little room at the top but not baggy at all. I would say if your calf is wider than 15” they will be a tight fit. I also have short legs (5’3”) and have problems finding knee high boots that aren’t a weird height but these sit perfectly below my knee. Overall would definitely recommend.

I was VERY skeptical of these jeans due to the cost, let's face it, Levi's for under $20 is VERY rare! I ordered the "Totally Shaping Bootcut Jeans" in a size 6 short (I am 5'3, USA) and while they are a bit loose at the waist, they are super comfortable! PROS: VERY comfortable. My iphone 15 fits about 3/4 of the way in the back pocket and when I bend forward or sit down, my phone does ride up but it does not fall out of the pocket which is a BIG plus! I washed in cold water and color did not bleed. I also put in the dryer and while I am not sure if it shrunk or not, it definitely fit better, but again, still a bit loose at the waist. It is a "bootcut" and as you can see on the pictures, they have a fabulous flair which I believe works great with my figure. CONS: While I listed it as a "pros" above, I do believe it shrunk a little bit in the dryer which I loved as it fit better, but if it fits tight when you initially try on, make sure you air dry as it will shrink a bit. Overall, these jeans are a complete steal and highly recommend, they are true levi jeans so you get great quality material, great fit and super comfortable jeans!

I will say this has been a process. The first pair of jeans came with NO tags and had obviously been worn and washed. They had what appeared to be paint stains and were very worn looking. The material had obviously been washed in either very warm or hot water. They fit very well or I probably would have just returned them for credit and bought Levi’s 315. I was looking for an alternative to spending upwards of $65.00 on a pair of Levi’s. I ask for a replacement and I noted the pair I had received were used. While I support recycling, if you purchase a pair of NEW jeans, they should be that, new with tags. These jeans should be labeled as returned in good condition with stains with a price reflecting that. It took about 2 weeks for the replacement pair to arrive, but they are NEW, unwashed jeans with tags. They fit very well. In comparison to the Original Levi’s 315, the material is thinner. The fit is very similar, but not identical. The 315 size 2 short are snugger through the hips and thighs and the flare on the bootcut is not quite as much. The rise is about a 1/2 inch lower on the original 315. In comparison with price being the main selling point, the Signature series is a good buy. I don’t know if they will hold up to the Original Levi’s quality. I only wash my jeans every 3-5 wears. I turn them inside out and wash with other jeans on cold water, gentle cycle. The front pockets are adequate and the same as Levi’s original. The back pockets have the Signature stitching and are similar to the original in size and shape. These are a lightweight denim and I don’t see them as being warm, probably better for a milder climate, or Fall weather. I have attached pictures for reference. The tags are on the new pair. For reference, I am 5’4” have long legs and a short torso and weigh 112. I wear a size 2 in Original Levi’s so I say these run very true to size. The inseam was right at 29 1/2 inches. Due to these being bootcut jeans, if you shorten them you would loose the “bootcut”. If you need a true short length of 28”, I don’t see these working.

These are a lightweight denim and I don’t see them as being warm, probably better for a milder climate, or Fall weather. I have attached pictures for reference. The tags are on the new pair. For reference, I am 5’4” have long legs and a short torso and weigh 112. I wear a size 2 in Original Levi’s so I say these run very true to size. The inseam was right at 29 1/2 inches. Due to these being bootcut jeans, if you shorten them you would loose the “bootcut”. If you need a true short length of 28”, I don’t see these working.

I will say this has been a process. The first pair of jeans came with NO tags and had obviously been worn and washed. They had what appeared to be paint stains and were very worn looking. The material had obviously been washed in either very warm or hot water. They fit very well or I probably would have just returned them for credit and bought Levi’s 315. I was looking for an alternative to spending upwards of $65.00 on a pair of Levi’s. I ask for a replacement and I noted the pair I had received were used. While I support recycling, if you purchase a pair of NEW jeans, they should be that, new with tags. These jeans should be labeled as returned in good condition with stains with a price reflecting that. It took about 2 weeks for the replacement pair to arrive, but they are NEW, unwashed jeans with tags. They fit very well. In comparison to the Original Levi’s 315, the material is thinner. The fit is very similar, but not identical. The 315 size 2 short are snugger through the hips and thighs and the flare on the bootcut is not quite as much. The rise is about a 1/2 inch lower on the original 315. In comparison with price being the main selling point, the Signature series is a good buy. I ordered a size 14 long. I am in between sizes 12 and 13 right now. Most 13s are to big, but I read the reviews from others and went with the 14 long. I am 5'8" and 200-205lbs. Im hoping these jeans will fit me when I get back to 190. I put them on right away to walk around the house in them to see if they loosen up any! The front of the calf area seem a little big/loose for me. Not enough to return them. I dont like the "ruffle" look it's giving off, but after a wash, hopefully itll all pan out. The length seems to be just right with my shoes! If I were a heal person, the length would be just right too. They feel like jeans! Look like jeans!! Just not the pressure from a button or two and zipper digging into my belly anytime I sit down. (I hate jeans)

I actually originally found this tank top in a thrift store-- I recently realized I had lost it somehow, and immediately re-ordered. This is my favorite little tank top. The fit is really flattering and comfortable. It is a tad bit see through, but I think that comes with the territory of white shirts TBH. The sizing is also surprisingly generous. I'm usualy a 1X-2X and don't typically fit into straight size clothing that only goes up to an XXL (wouldn't have ever ordered this if I hadn't found it at a thrift store first tbh, straight size XXL's in mainline brands like GAP don't tend to be wayyyy too tight on me). But the XXL fits perfectly on me without being tight on my stomach (My waist is about 40" for reference, bust about 46"). I also really like the wide racerback fit in the back, I find it to be more flattering than thinner racerbacks. I promise I'm a real person who just likes this tank top, lol. (Enough to order it again when I lost it. If I had more spending money right now I'd probably order a few more.)

I am 5’6” not 6’3, but I can’t get the height to change. This wouldn’t fit someone who is 6’3”. 🤣 This shirt is very inexpensive and fits well. The cut is comfortable and the material is soft. However I ordered it specifically because it’s supposed to be 100% cotton. I thought maybe the 7% spandex wouldn’t make a huge difference and it was an exceedingly hot day today so I wore it - and was disappointed as it holds in the heat and doesn’t breath. Yuck. Even though it’s inexpensive, I won’t buy another.

I am so glad I took a chance ordering these jeans. I order a lot of clothes from Amazon but have never ordered jeans online before this. I’m 5’2”, 150-155lbs. My measurements are not ideal. My waist is 38.5” and my hips are 41.5” I refer to my shape as Sponge Bob-ish. There is almost no difference between my waist and my hips and I have hardly any butt to speak of. To top it off, I have slender legs! I got a 12 short, (31 waist, the tag says). I unsure about getting short because sometimes they end up too short but these work! I have pants in size 8 to 12 or large but most are medium or 10. I got 12 because HATE tight waisted pants. Also tight waists push the fat up.I’m 48 so trying out alternatives to skinny jeans because they don’t really flatter my box with legs shape. Hope this helps! I got one in dark blue and this light blue. Both fit the same, some say different colors fit differently, I did not find that trueHaving said that all that, these jeans fit and feel great! They aren’t too boot cut but just the right amount. The flat elastic wait band is perfect! I prefer mid waisted pants because I carry my extra weight in my mid section.

First I do like these jeans and I might order another pair because of the price. HOWEVER, they are very stretchy and don’t seem to be the best quality. They also aren’t as long as I thought they should be. (I ordered longs). I do like them I was just hoping the fabric quality would be less stretchy cheap and longer for my height of 5’9. I ordered 10 longs because I was worried about the length instead of 8 longs, I’m really glad I ordered the 10s because they are long enough to be socially acceptable.

Cute! I'm 5'1 130lbs I got the size 6 short first and the length was perfect and they fit everywhere else besides the waist where you button them was too tight. I needed to go up to a 10 short but they were out of the short so I went with the regular. They fit good around the waist I just wish they were a little tighter every where else. They are a little long but ok.. I wish they would of had the short that way they would stop around the ankles. I gave the 6 short to my daughter who is 5'4 115lbs and they fit her perfectly! If she was any taller they would of been to short. The material is thick and they are comfortable, overall the quality is really good.

ordered a size 0 (I’m 5'6", 100 lbs). The length is great, the waist fits, and they fit nicely in the back. Unfortunately, the front bunches quite a bit. The model shows a much smoother, second-skin fit than what I experienced.

Sizing was spot on for XS. Although, the size guide was inaccurate. For reference, I'm 5'4, 125lb, 32DDD. Length of the top is perfect. I ordered regular length pants. They're probably a little long on me for "joggers", but still fit okay. The material is nice. It's light, silky, and very stretchy. The waistband is great! It's wide and comfortable. Doesn't roll over. Would definitely order again. Has a feminine fit. Not boxy. Pockets are deep enough to fit all the things. I like that the pants have a zipper pocket. As far as wrinkle resistance, I haven't taken them out of the dryer yet so idk.

I’m 5’10” and 250 lbs with a longer torso, and I ordered the XXL Tall. The length is AMAZING. I can wear these high-waisted without them turning into high waters or creating awkward bunching in the front. As a tall girl, that’s rare. I purchased the full scrub set but have only tried on the pants so far. Honestly, these are already my favorite scrubs I’ve owned. The fit is comfortable, flattering, and structured without feeling tight. I saw some reviews mentioning stains or items arriving pre-used, but mine came vacuum-sealed in perfect condition — no issues at all. I’ll update once I’ve worn the full set, but based on the pants alone, I’ll definitely be ordering more.

super cute and comfortable for reference i’m 5’7 and usually im 126-130 Lb but currently i’ve lost a lot of weight so i’m 119 lb at the moment and it got a SMALL size it’s a lil loose from my waist but deff bc i have lost couple pounds
I’m 5’9 and 167 pounds and I got a Large tall. I love them extremely comfy and room to grow. I could have sized down and got a medium but these still fit great! I’ll definitely buy more.

They’re decent quality jeans with enough stretch for comfort. Their sizing was a bit off to me though. I ordered a 14 short, as I’m 5’3” and weigh 170. They suggested size 14; I should have gone with 16 as I had planned, to accommodate any shrinking of the 70% cotton. But the size they sent for “14 Short” was 34x32. I was expecting a 30” inseam, as stated in their description, knowing I’d still need to cut off some and re-hem at 28-29. Since they’re pulled with a traditional size, ie: waist and inseam, why can’t one just order that way? Then you’d know exactly what you were getting, like in the 70’s when I was a teenager… only now with stretch! LOL

I am 5'9, 135 lbs I like the length, fit, and comfort of these pants. These pant have no stretch and are true to size. If you are on the curvy side I would size up. The only thing is I did not like was I expecting beige colored pants and these are more of a yellowish, but it worked love then!! stars.

Not exactly the color l needed- l wanted yellow to match a sweater that has a yellow pocket. The pants are a butterscotch color, but l found a blouse in my closet (another Amazon purchase) that has gold accents, so I will keep the trousers. As for sizing, I am 5’10 and ~175lb. Amazon recommended an XL. I couldn’t fasten the pants below or right at my belly button bc I need to shave off 10lbs, but l pulled them up higher and I was able to hook the clasp together. Of course, I lost some of the length, but they are not high waters. They are thin, so great for summer. They look exactly like the picture as far as the style. If you need extra length, there’s only a 1” serged hem. The belt loops look like they were sewn on by an amateur, but my blouse will cover them. If you can wear a belt with the pants, no one will likely notice the loops. Lately, l have been receiving my clothing in see-through plastic ziplocked bags. I would prefer they be concealed better, but thankfully the delivery person hid them so well that I almost didn’t find them on my porch.

I absolutely love these pants in black! I was really excited to make the all white version work. I was excited to use this outfit for Bridal events. Unfortunately the pants are completely see-through. You can see the lining of the pocket and anything you’re wearing underneath. Wearing skin toned shape wear is an absolute must otherwise you are showing everybody everything. If that’s what you’re going for, you will love these pants. I considered sewing the pockets shut and cutting out the liner, but it’s just not worth the trouble. The quality is amazing but if they were double lined, it would be way better. I’m 5‘6“, 165 pounds to 172 pounds in an hourglass shape. The photos will show how you can see the body suit underneath and the pockets

So before deciding to purchase this scrub set, I was looking at various brands popular on social media. But the price point kind of set me back as a nursing student getting ready to graduate in a few months. I started doing some research and landed here. As an avid Amazon shopper I was glad to find sufficient positive reviews for this set and for less than $30! The negative reviews regarding the fit, stains, poor seams, odor, etc. made me a bit unsure but my budget says otherwise lol I said girl I got a sewing kit, a washer/dryer set, and a dream—I can fix her 😂😂 Upon receiving the first set I ordered, the drawstring on the pants were missing so I returned the whole set. I did try on said pants to see if I the size I ordered was the correct fit and it was!! For reference, I am 5’3 and 130 lbs on a good day (133 lbs when bloated). Bust is 36C and waist is 29.5 in. I went with the size Small. I almost picked Medium but reading the reviews here that one appears to fit those 145 lbs and up better. I’m very sensitive to my waist being squeezed too tight and waistband on the Small was perfect. It stayed in place without making me feel uncomfortable. The cuffs around the ankles are just right—not too loose, not too tight. I imagine someone around 5’5 and up might look better in it since the fabric above the stretchy cuffs kind of pour down on the cuffs due to my shorter height. It is, however, not a major issue with me! No wrinkles upon arrival either. I can confidently say I don’t have a lot of the problems others have shared with their sets. I haven’t washed my set yet, though. Some said the drawstring may detach from the pants in the wash so I might tie a bow before tossing them in. I currently own 2 of this set and plan on purchasing another for when I start residency this July. In addition, I also got a scrub jacket(?) from this brand in the same size and color. Same material. It’s like they took the same fabric and made it into a jacket(?). It’s quite thin but not see-through. I tend to wear thermal shirts underneath my scrub top so being cold in it is also not an issue. This is jot a review of the jacket(?) of course. My point is this brand effectively delivers every time 👏👏👏 Like I said, I haven’t washed mine so I can’t comment on shrinkage or sturdiness but I got a sewing kit and the 94% polyester part makes this more likely to maintain its shape. I highly recommend it if you are looking for something budget friendly. Those looking for thicker, more luxurious fabric might want to look elsewhere. As for me, this one will be my go-to.

These are my new favorite pants. I wear them for walking, for running errands or just lounging around. I’m sure they will be comfortable for travel. The fit is perfect for me. I am 5'3", 160 lbs. The Large Petite fits fine, comfortably snug without being tight. I believe these are what Skechers calls their “OG” pant on their website. I feel really no difference between this OG and the Joy version (of which I also purchased one). The XL petite also fits fine without being baggy. I wear both, but I prefer the fit of the L. The top hits just above my belly button. The length is good on me, it will cover the tongue of my sneakers, does not touch the ground. They feel a little stiff out of the bag but they soften up after the first wash, with no discernible shrinkage, and they hold their shape. Great quality! The side pockets are a huge plus, deep enough to fit my large phone with no fear of it popping out as I move. I have now bought several pairs of these OG pants. I can only speak for how the black color fits (88% nylon/12% spandex); I know some colors/blends sometimes fit differently. Get these, you will not be disappointed.

I am absolutely thrilled with these pants! Finding wide leg pants that truly work for petites can be such a challenge, but these are perfection. I’m 5’1 and typically wear a size 8 in pants. I ordered the Medium Petite, and the fit is spot on. The length is exactly right with no hemming needed, which is rare and so appreciated. The waffle knit fabric feels soft and comfortable without being too heavy. They have a smooth, polished look while still feeling like lounge pants. The elastic waistband with the drawstring makes them easy to adjust, and the pockets are functional without adding bulk. What I love most is how easily they can be dressed up or down. I can wear them with sneakers and a casual top for running errands, or pair them with a sweater or blazer for a more put together look. The wide leg silhouette is flattering and drapes beautifully. If you are petite and struggle to find the right length in wide leg styles, these are a fantastic option. Comfortable, flattering, and versatile. I would absolutely purchase again!

These G4Free waffle wide-leg sweatpants are easily my new favorite lounge pants. They are extremely comfortable, and the waffle fabric gives them a nice texture that makes them feel more intentional than standard “comfy pants.” Stitching and seams are all well done, and they feel solid without being heavy. I ordered the dark grey and it’s a really nice, neutral color. I washed and dried them using regular settings with no special care, and they didn’t shrink at all. The pockets are functional, the drawstring actually works. I’m 5’3” and absolutely love that these are offered in a petite length. I’m not stepping on the hems, and I don’t need to have them altered, which is rare for me. For fit reference, I ordered a medium. They fit slightly snug, but in a flattering way. My partner is a fan of what they do for my derrière! If you like your lounge pants to highlight your shape, your usual size should work. If you prefer a looser, more relaxed fit, I’d recommend sizing up one. That said, the price is the weak point. At the time of my review, these are listed at $39.99, which feels high for casual lounge pants. As much as I like them, they’d feel like a much better value at a lower price point. Comfort, quality, and petite sizing are all wins here…just be aware you’re definitely paying a premium for them.

I really want to love these jeans. They are comfortable. The size fits my waist just right. They are snug without being too tight. I ordered them in “short” length, but they are much too long. Even with higher soled/healed shoes I nearly trip on them. I am 5’1”. Short sizes are typically just right for me. Let’s talk about the width. These say “flare” or “boot cut” in the description. The picture shows them fitted to the knee with a moderate flare to the ankle. However, that is not actually the case. These take me back to the super wide style of the late ‘90s. They start to flare out at the hip. The material is comfortable with just enough stretch. And the color is really nice. The dye did not even bleed in the wash.

These are a great product for a great price. They remind me exactly of the NYDJ. They have the same quality feel and look for less. They fit exactly the same and I ordered the same size. I am 5’7”, weigh 150lbs, 37” hips and I ordered a 4tall. They do stretch and they fit perfectly. They don’t get baggy and are so comfortable

Reviewed size: 0 Regular Height: 5’2” | Weight: 114 lbs Measurements: 27” waist, 35.5” hips Typical inseam: 29” (28.3” minimum — I don’t like pants hitting at the ankle) I ordered these pants in white, size 0 regular. The pants definitely fit well — they’re comfortable and fit nicely through the waist, hips, and legs. The length is technically fine, but they hit about one inch above my ankle, which is a personal preference issue for me. I don’t like pants that sit above the ankle. I noticed this is exactly how they fit the model in the photos, so the product is accurate — it’s just not my preferred look. Even though I won’t be keeping them due to my length preference, these are still a great buy, especially for the price. At $18, the quality feels very good, the fabric is comfortable, and they’re not overly see-through for white pants. I’m still giving 5 stars, and I may reorder them in size 0 Long, which I think would better suit my length preference.

So I do kind of like these pants. I like the stretch fit. They are comfortable. However I had a hard time deciding what was going to be my size. I tend to be a size 6, sometimes size 7. And I'm really tall so I got them a size 6 and I got the size 8 and they were the both the same for length which was kind of a bummer. I got the eighth because I wanted length. And then the eight and six almost fit the same way around my tummy. But now after I had my surgery the eight is almost too big and I should have known better than to buy it before my surgery. Even so both of them were like there's extra material when I want a stretchy Gene. That's tight. I want him to actually be a stretchy Gene that's tight. This has got some loose material on my legs and it kind of looks like they're just oversized for me and it didn't matter if it was the size 6 or the eight. But with that being said, I do still like the pants. They're just not exactly what I had wanted. So I don't think that they should be less Stars, but I also don't think it should be full Stars because it's not what I had intended for. But it still works. Also, I'm 5'9 and I sit around 135 to 140 lb

Also, I'm 5'9 and I sit around 135 to 140 lb

I am appx 5’8”, 127lbs, 26 inch waist and 35.5 hips. I would have normally ordered 4Long, but 6 Regular was the only size “drastically” reduced at the time, so took a chance. They are ankle cut now, which is fine, and so is the fit. I’d say they’re pretty close to true size, or size up one, if wanting looser fit. They are stretch denim and on the thin side. Just fine in my warmer climate of avg temp of mid 60’s in winter, scorching in summer.

I’ve ordered three pairs of these Amazon Essentials Mid-Rise Stretchy Skinny Jeans (size 4 long) in black, light olive, and medium wash. For reference, I’m 5’5” and 120 lbs. Pros: I really like the mid-rise, it’s flattering in the front and comfortable to wear without constantly having to pull my pants up. The length is perfect for me, and the olive green and medium wash materials feel nice. They ride up a little on the calves, but that’s probably just me. The fit is snug but true to size, and they contour nicely without feeling restrictive. And for only $15 (at least when I bought them), they’re a great deal. Cons: The black pair is made of a completely different material that attracts lint and cat hair like crazy, and it bunches oddly in the crotch area (something the olive and medium wash don’t do). The medium wash also had a strong mothball smell that hasn’t gone away even after four washes, which is pretty frustrating. Overall, I like the fit and comfort of the olive green and medium wash pairs, but the black ones are disappointing in terms of fabric and wearability. Still, for $15 a pair, they’re hard to beat!

Why did you pick this product vs others?: I was going to leave three stars, but I decided to leave four because I went back and looked at the description. Amazon recommended a size 12 for these. I’m 5’1” around 160 with an athletic stocky build. I typically wear between a size 8 and a size 10 in pants so I ordered the 10 short. These are very snug in the waist. They fit perfectly in the thigh, bottom, and leg. I’ll probably still trim the bottom if I decide to keep them because I like my skinny jeans right at the ankle and these fall just below. These are also high waisted. Can’t remember if the description said mid rise but these come above my belly button. From other reviews, I’ve read they do stretch out quite a bit and I think if I sized up to the 12, they would be too big in the hip and legs. They look fine with a shirt on so I might just get a button extender and keep them. Pretty good quality for the price.

These jeans are SO good! They’re not like that garbage “jegging” material, but they do have enough stretch that you can sit down and not burst open. The waist sits right at the smallest part of my torso, which I think looks quite nice! Also LONG! I’m 5’10” and I always find jeans so short so I bought these in a 22 long and WOW - I have to roll them up, but I think it looks cute that way too. Next time I’ll go with the regular length though. Overall totally love them!

I've worn scrubs for the past 40 years of my life -Old school scrubs, stiff material, drawstring closure, unisex fit. The newer material is nice. It's softer. I like a little bit of stretch in it. I do not, however like a tight fit to scrubs, these are work clothes, for the operating room and not fashion per se. I found a pair of Dickie scrubs that I really liked and of course after 6 months they no longer exist. This is my attempt to find something similar. The waistband is similar, albeit without a drawstring, that is a downer for me because I tend to carry my phone and other items in pockets. Without a drawstring these do tend to be less secure at the waist and they slide a little bit with weightier things in the pocket. I am 5'7 and 190 lb. An extra large slid down without anything in the pockets, but I liked how the legs fit. I returned those for a large and the waistband sits just below my navel and the legs are okay, not tight but not loose fit scrubs. The stitching that comes right across the knee is just weird. When you walk you have that feeling of having long shorts on because the band is not stretchy. The length is good and I like the flare at the ankle. I guess we'll see how the color holds up. Not a fan of the waistband if you were going to tuck your scrubs in, but I've conceded to ordering more Missy fit scrub tops that will be worn outside of my scrub pants.

Great quality jeans! I checked the size guide but according to the reviews many people were saying to size down. I’m 5’4” with most of my height being my legs, 190lbs of mostly leg muscle and I hold most of my weight in my belly. The size chart recommends a size 16plus but I ended up getting a 14plus and they fit absolutely perfect! They feel very stretchy and comfortable and I ride horses so I believe these would be comfortable enough to use at barrel races and I wouldn’t feel like they’re too baggy or too tight. If I had one single thing to say constructively, I’d say maybe they need to be a smidge longer. If I got long sized jeans they’d be too long, and since I’ll wear them with cowgirl boots they may look a little short, but I’m willing to sacrifice that and have them fit everywhere else. Most jeans I purchase are too long so I really like these. Overall 10/10 and worth the money! Quick delivery within 48 hours.

I ordered 2 pairs of these about 10 days apart. The first pair in perfect condition (color Magic), fit well, the average length on me is very long ( I am 5’3” ) and I have to pull them up now and then as they get long in the crotch, not a deal breaker. I liked them enough to order a second pair, but this time I ordered the length in short, the length is perfect and they fit well. Disappointing that there is a defect in the stitching on the back pocket and material is a little thin below the pocket which could make them wear out quicker. I tend to wear longer tops so not sure if I should exchange. The colors in the pictures look the same but the Christy Pure is a little lighter. Both pairs are very soft. I do recommend these jeans, they are very comfortable. 4 stars for the stitching defect, 5 stars for the fit in the short length.

The color was just as described and consistent with the other reviews. Nice stretchy fabric. I would have given 5 stars if they fit better. As you see from the pic, they are a little fitted in the butt and hip area, which I like, but the waist is a little large. With the amount of stretch in these, I would have been fine with a 4. Love these leggings! Overall, these leggings are great! The material is buttery soft while still feeling supportive, and the stretch makes them incredibly comfortable for all-day wear. I especially love the high-waisted band—it offers just the right amount of tummy support without feeling too constricting. The hidden scrunch and V-back design are subtle but flattering, giving a natural, lifted look. They’re perfect for life on the go or a workout at the gym. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 116 lbs wearing an extra small. I chose the 28” inseam, and while they’re slightly long for my height, I personally like having that little bit of extra length.

Smooth, Sculpting & Very Flattering (Charcoal Gray, Small – 28”) I ordered the Sunzel Sunzfly Invisible Butt Scrunch leggings in Charcoal Gray, size Small, 28” inseam, and they’re incredibly flattering. The fabric (80% polyamide, 20% elastane) feels ultra-soft and stretchy with light-to-medium compression. It smooths everything out without feeling stiff or restrictive. The material isn’t thin, and I feel confident wearing them for workouts or running errands. The high-waisted waistband provides solid tummy control and stays in place — no rolling down. The no front seam makes a big difference in comfort and gives a clean, smooth look in the front. The hidden scrunch detail in the back enhances shape subtly. It lifts without looking exaggerated. Very sculpting but still tasteful. The 28” inseam hits right at my ankle for a full-length look without bunching. The Charcoal Gray color is a true neutral — easy to pair with black, white, or muted tones. Overall, they feel supportive, smoothing, and flattering from every angle. Great quality for the price. I would love to get different colors.

My favorite leggings I very much enjoy these. They are very stretchy and buttery soft, and flatter my body exceptionally well. For size reference, I bought a small and I have a 27-28” waist and 39” hips. I bought a medium about a year ago which held up nicely aside from 1-2 dangling seam threads here and there, but honestly they were always a bit too big on me, so I bought a small this time and definitely prefer it. My one complaint is that since I’m short (5’3”) the rise is very long on me, like the waist goes all the way up to my longline bra, but I’m ok with that and it’s not a design flaw whatsoever. Can’t have everything.

Not for taller people My first impression when putting these pants on were regarding the fabric. Buttery soft and very comfortable. I actually kept them on all day because they feel like yoga pants. When it comes to wearing them as slacks, a few things to know…they have a working button, zipper and pockets. So they feel like legit work pants. But much comfier since I could just slip them on without unzipping. They have a lot of stretch to them which is great. I think they are more meant to be worn with a shirt tucked in because the pockets are high up on the high waist rather than on the sides. So they’d be trickier to access with a shirt untucked. The inseam was perfect on me when I pulled them up to the waist. But then the hem was too high for me at 5’7”. And pulling them down to make them longer made the crotch too low. So maybe they’re better for someone a few inches shorter. But with the right shoe, it probably wouldn’t matter. Overall they’re a great pant. Just wish they were longer. I’ll be wearing them with an untucked shirt just because the fabric does hug the body enough to show your shape and I’m not crazy about mine. For reference, I am 165 pounds. Waist is 35” and hip is 42” and I got an XL.

But much comfier since I could just slip them on without unzipping. They have a lot of stretch to them which is great. I think they are more meant to be worn with a shirt tucked in because the pockets are high up on the high waist rather than on the sides. So they’d be trickier to access with a shirt untucked. The inseam was perfect on me when I pulled them up to the waist. But then the hem was too high for me at 5’7”. And pulling them down to make them longer made the crotch too low. So maybe they’re better for someone a few inches shorter. But with the right shoe, it probably wouldn’t matter. Overall they’re a great pant. Just wish they were longer. I’ll be wearing them with an untucked shirt just because the fabric does hug the body enough to show your shape and I’m not crazy about mine. For reference, I am 165 pounds. Waist is 35” and hip is 42” and I got an XL.
