Be aware that these jeans do vary slightly from one color to another. I noticed the darker colors seem to run slightly larger than the lighter ones. I am short (5’-2”) so I bought the short inseam (actually 30” not 29” as noted in description), as recommended by the size chart. Upon trying them on, the leg length fell perfectly at my heal. After a few washes the length is now above my shoes and looks awkward. I then purchased the medium inseam (actually 32” not 31” as noted in description) which was a few inches too long. I had to fold them so they wouldn’t drag on the floor or bunch up at my shoes. After a few washes, sure enough, they shrank and the length is now perfect. I would recommend purchasing the longer inseam if you are in between the heights noted on the size chart. Also, if you are like me and have bigger thighs, the material will pill quickly in the crotch area as well. I snapped a photo after a few months of wear, for reference. Overall, I am satisfied with the jeans and would purchase again.

I think they could fit absolutely anyone Edit on edit: Well I notice quite a few ppl at work wear these jeans and they mold (mould?) to every body type. One woman has that round booty tiny waist, another wide hips, skinny legs, another average. Looks great on all. The darker color is a tad more rough and stiff. And there is a slight chemical smell (the dye?) Edit: I have since bought several colors and sizes and even bought a size 2. Since there is stretch they get loose throughout the day. 2 is perfect. A little tight at first, but they loosen. So I’d consider going down your normal size idk. Since skinny jeans are out and I’m too old for the baggy grunge look wanted straight leg jeans. Happened to see on IG and tried them. Love em. The light color is perfect for spring and cold summer days as the fabric is on the lightweight side. Def not thick lucky jeans or gap, and not as closely knitted or buttery as Paige. Has a very slight course feel to the touch. Almost as comfy as pjs because of the stretch and thinness. The stretch could allow for many different body types to wear I think. I’m 5’6” 120lbs w heavier thighs flat a$$ and wide hips and size 4 M length is loose perfect, goes over hips easily, I may even consider these high rise and length is perfect w a slight crease at ankle if wearing flats

These boots FIT my larger calves … I’m 64 years old, and I’ve dreamed of owning a beautiful pair of beautiful suede boots, however I have larger calves. I’ve recently lost some weight and when I was offered an opportunity to buy these boots at a discounted price, I jumped at the chance. The boots arrived today, with a beautiful chunky heel, comfortable insole and for once in my life, they fit my calves! I am so excited.

Really excellent but size up and expect distressed These are GORGEOUS! I was blown away by how good these are. I purchased a similar pair from Frye and I honestly like these wayyyyy better! I love the shaft height, hits me right below the knee, I'm 5'7" for reference. I love the toe shape, the extra space at the calf, I do not consider myself to have wide calves for reference. I wear a women's 8-8.5 and since they are only offered in whole sizes, I went with an 8 and they are almost too small. I will definitely have to use some shoe stretch spray on them, so if you are between sizes, go up if you want to wear socks!! Now, my only major issue, not sure if I got a funky pair, but there is some weird distressing that I was not expecting and does not seem intentional, see pics. I do plan to take a suede brush to them to see if I can get those out before waterproof spraying them. I was a bit disappointed in that, especially for the price point. Otherwise, the shape, the shaft height and fit around the calf, color, its all so super good and I will wear these ALL SEASON! I love them!

A little cheap but still great! The material is a little odd, but that is both a pro and a con. Con: it's a little thin, a thicker material would make it seem fancier. Watch out for VPL. Pro: it shrunk length in the wash. This was a pro for me because i would have needed to alter it so it wouldn't drag on the ground. I'm 5'6.5, 175-180 lbs, just had a baby 4 months ago. Pre-pregancy i had 30-33 waist and 40-44 hips, now i have about 36 waist and 45 hips (self measured which is inaccurate sometimes) and had a hard time ordering things the right size so i aimed high and ordered a 2x. Fits great, like leggings, the top cinches the waist so if you wear a tight top, it will create an indent in your silhouette, and your tummy pouch will show on crop tops (i don't mind it). Leg is wide, doesn't feel as pajama-y as palazzo pants, but not as nice as dress pants. An easy way to upgrade your outfits and add pizazz. A little more of a blue green than a yellow green. I included a picture and a video to show movement!

Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

THE JEANS ARE FABULOUS! Omg.....I freaking love these jeans! I've lost over 75 lbs and wasn't sure what size i wear now. So I went off of the reviews. I'm 5'2", 152lbs, have always had curvy hips. I ordered the large based on the size chart and reviews and they fit perfect!

Okay so I was really hesitant to buy these. I'm 5'8" and was really worried about the length. But I took a risk and assumed I could wear them as capris, worst case. Well I'm glad I bought them. Hips are 41" around and waist is 32" so it can be hard to find pants that fit my ass that actually stay up around the waist. These pants fit my body great! They're a little snug to pull over the booty but once they're up- they're a dream. They're slightly tighter in the waist than most pants so if you are apple shaped or have a wider tummy than booty, these may not be a good fit for you. I do have muscular legs and calves and they are not uncomfortably tight in these pants. In fact, they're appropriately loose even when sitting. No tight pulling and has slight looseness and give when seated. I ordered a size medium based off reviews and they fit exactly how I expected. Large would have been too baggy and not flattering on my body for my personal taste. They do have quite a bit of stretch so I'm glad I sized-down. I'm sure they'll stretch as I wear them and I don't wash my pants after every wear so a large may have been problematic. Apologies for the crappy angle on pics. I don't have a full length mirror and had to make do with the mirror I have! I even reccomended these for my sister who is 5'10". 6ft sister MAY be okay in a large if the hem is a bit longer. They'd definitely be more classic ankle pants. At the ankle, no rolling. They're seemingly petty wrinkle resistant which is awesome. Tried them on right out of the bag and there are no creases from packaging. I will likely buy these in other colors. I purchased navy and black. Black is pictured. See no roll, one roll, two rolls. Also tried to get a pic of the waist band and rise of pants. Ordered a Medium. 5'8", 170lbs, hourglass. Great fit.

Okay so I was really hesitant to buy these. I'm 5'8" and was really worried about the length. But I took a risk and assumed I could wear them as capris, worst case. Well I'm glad I bought them. Hips are 41" around and waist is 32" so it can be hard to find pants that fit my ass that actually stay up around the waist. These pants fit my body great! They're a little snug to pull over the booty but once they're up- they're a dream. They're slightly tighter in the waist than most pants so if you are apple shaped or have a wider tummy than booty, these may not be a good fit for you. I do have muscular legs and calves and they are not uncomfortably tight in these pants. In fact, they're appropriately loose even when sitting. No tight pulling and has slight looseness and give when seated. I ordered a size medium based off reviews and they fit exactly how I expected. Large would have been too baggy and not flattering on my body for my personal taste. They do have quite a bit of stretch so I'm glad I sized-down. I'm sure they'll stretch as I wear them and I don't wash my pants after every wear so a large may have been problematic. Apologies for the crappy angle on pics. I don't have a full length mirror and had to make do with the mirror I have! I even reccomended these for my sister who is 5'10". 6ft sister MAY be okay in a large if the hem is a bit longer. They'd definitely be more classic ankle pants. At the ankle, no rolling. They're seemingly petty wrinkle resistant which is awesome. Tried them on right out of the bag and there are no creases from packaging. I will likely buy these in other colors. I purchased navy and black. Black is pictured. See no roll, one roll, two rolls. Also tried to get a pic of the waist band and rise of pants. Ordered a Medium. 5'8", 170lbs, hourglass. Great fit.

Comfortable, love the waist If you are looking for very vibrant pink pants that are stretchy and comfortable, these are for you! They have a wide, super comfortable waist that comes nearly to my belly button. Slide on pants with no zipper or buttons. Stretchy and comfortable, however I am taking off one star since they do that weird bunching thing that some stretchy pants do. They are almost over-stretchy like they won't really provide any support or hold you in at all. But I like the lines and the length. I like how they fit at the ankles. They are so vibrant, it is almost hard to find a shoe that goes with the shade, so I am still playing with that. Overall a good, fun pant for the price point. I am 5'3" and 135lbs and the small fits well

Order up if you want to wash! I gave these pants five stars because I like them a lot once I figured out what to buy. I usually wear a size 2/4 depending on the brand and am 5’6” tall, 120 pounds. The first pair I ordered were on this site called “ The millennium”. I ordered a small but received a medium and didn’t realize it. (they might’ve sent it to me because Company knew they were small ?) They were a little big when I tried them on and then I washed them and they fit perfect! So order up.I bought some more colors which I thought were the same, but weren’t. They were called “Point Pants”. Same brand and looked the same in the picture but weren’t the because they had more nylon in them than the millennium. I ordered a small and they fit perfect and didn’t really shrink. They were much thicker which would be good for winter, but it is spring and I wanted a thinner pant so I am keeping the medium size millennium pants so I can wash them. I hope this makes sense because these pants are great for winter and spring and summer. And they are also a great price!PS. I would’ve liked to have them a little shorter, but they were all out of the “short”pants.

Great pants for the price! These are great! I'm a college student with a 40-hour/week summer internship where I can't wear jeans, and I don't own a ton of business casual-type clothing. I only had two pairs of dress pants, so I wanted another pair to break up the outfit rotation (and to make laundry easier) but didn't want to break the bank on something I probably will only wear once during the rest of the year. These fit the bill! I am 5'7" and 140 pounds with a 28 inch waist and a 39 inch hip at the fullest point (aka I have a huge butt). I ordered the medium, and apart from being just slightly too long (by maybe half an inch, but someone with a smaller butt would probably need a hemming job), they fit really well! I like that they're a higher rise in the front (so my tummy is contained) as I just find that really comfortable. They're VERY comfy, flexible, and stretchy, so I could see someone who wears an 8 or maybe even a 10 at my height fitting in these well (I usually wear a 4 or 6 in pants depending on brand). They don't have pockets, so they're pretty much leggings made out of nicer material, but they do have inside and outside seams so they look like any other skinny fit dress pant. I ordered navy and it's very true to color. Thanks, Amazon!

Cute! Shrank but still fits. I am 5’9” and weigh 190 lb. I typically wear a US size 12-14 and ordered an XL. The photos were taken before they were washed, and one leg was slightly shorter than the other, maybe 1/2-1”. The first time I washed them I only let them dry about 15 minutes on low temp then hung them up to finish drying. They needed to be ironed afterward and did not shrink. The second time I washed them I forgot about them, and they went through a full dry cycle on low temp. They came out wrinkle free but shrunk 2-3” in length. They also shrank in width but still fit. They wear like hot pants from the 60s. The material is a stretchy twill and I’ve received numerous compliments on them. They also hold their shape well during wear. My only caveat is that it has no pockets. I’d love a hidden pocket in the back. Overall, I think these are a good value for the money and will order more.

Size inconsistent, dry clean only Here are several "mediums", and different angles of an average medium. My suggestion is to order several of your color(s) in the same size, and return all but your best fit due to inconsistent sizing. Lucky for me, the Royal Blue fits like more of a tight ankle legging, so I will keep and have the others altered. Must wear a thong, and make sure it is flat or the texture will show bunching under the pants (intentionally shown in side angle picture). Does not show cellulite for me, which is a plus. All but the Royal Blue had crotch horizontal lines (shown in front closeup), but could be due to how wide that area is in me. These can't be altered away, due to needing a less shallow crotch seam and you can't add the extra fabric to fix it. Pretty stretchy, but you couldn't do lunges in them. Last picture is just to show general bunching, but it actually doesn't look that bad, it's just a weird angle. I'm 5'3", ~124 pounds, wide hips. I haven't washed/worn these yet, but based on other pants with these fabric %age and types, I know from experience to dry clean only; it helps prevent little elastic fibers from breaking down in the crotch/rear.

Very good product, good quality, elegant, and greate fit. I love them! (I would love for the leggings to have a little zipper in the waist so they could be a little more tight in the abdomen, but they are wonderful nonetheless.) The fabric of these leggings is thick and helps contour the figure. They are not sports leggings, they look a lot more elegant, more like pants, so they are perfect to wear in the office. They are made in the USA! with good finishes, and the high waist is perfect to wear with blouses (which you can put inside them) But ( there is always a but) the red color at least has to be used with light-colored underwear, You can see the dark underwear a little bit through them (as you can see in the pictures). To take care of the leggings I recommend washing them in a Delicates Laundry Bags , with COLD water and avoiding sitting on surfaces such as gravel, this way they will last longer. In the long term ( 1 year if you use them ones a week) and since they wear out on the butt with rubbing (and white elastics appear). I had some that were a very small size for me and for obvious reasons the butt was worn out before. I'm 5' ,121 lb and the small size fits me.

I had some that were a very small size for me and for obvious reasons the butt was worn out before. I'm 5' ,121 lb and the small size fits me.

Very good product, good quality, elegant, and greate fit. I love them! (I would love for the leggings to have a little zipper in the waist so they could be a little more tight in the abdomen, but they are wonderful nonetheless.) The fabric of these leggings is thick and helps contour the figure. They are not sports leggings, they look a lot more elegant, more like pants, so they are perfect to wear in the office. They are made in the USA! with good finishes, and the high waist is perfect to wear with blouses (which you can put inside them) But ( there is always a but) the red color at least has to be used with light-colored underwear, You can see the dark underwear a little bit through them (as you can see in the pictures). Great quality and very comfortable! I am 5-5 and 125lbs. Ordered size small in Orange. This is not the first time I have bought this brand and must say their clothes run very true to size. 90's Style Sweatpants I bought these 90-style sweatpants for a costume: Rachel from Friends "the one with the football". It worked out perfectly. As far as the quality goes, these are good quality in my opinion. They are thick material, the black hasn't faded after multiple washes. My only complaint(s), and they are minor, is that they seem to attract every hair and every lint imaginable, way more than any other piece of clothing I own. That and the elastic at the legs are very tight and it can get uncomfortable after a while. For reference, I am 5'3", 112 lbs, and ordered small.

Had low expectations based on ratings, but Quality is outstanding! It’s hard to determine whether sweatpants are going to be the “next favorite pair” when shopping online and having only the description and ratings to judge from. Each pair of pants made usually end up being completely different from the next, so it’s really chance taking when spending this much money on sweats. The chance was worth it for me! - The fabric isn’t too bad, there isn’t a lining inside but they still have some weight feeling to them and are comfortable! - I am 5’7 and the Med length is perfect- as you can see the fabric bunched at the ankle, they could go down over my heels if I wanted - I will also add that the waist elastic is tighter than most mediums fit, (rn Im at 123lbs saying that it fits slightly tighter) so sizing up could be better if you don’t want waist band lines. - the pockets are great with depth but not too much. - the inseam is perfect, there isn’t too much fabric between that feels awkward Will be buying more colors!

Great fit and wear. This is my favorite athletic-wear brand right now. I love a good matching set and have six or seven from Pavoi. They’re cute, soft yet have a bit of compression, don’t fall off my waist during a heavy workout and I get compliments daily. After a pilates class last week, I had four women, who were not together, surround me after class asking where I got them. I wore my new light blue set today and had random people stop me to tell me how great it looked. Makes a 40 something mother of three feel like she is on to something… The gym is my second home and I work out almost every day. They hold up great in the wash, wear after wear. The longer leggings are the perfect length for my 34” inseam. No front seam and the back makes my flat butt look like it’s trying to do something. True to size fit for me. Pavoi needs to sponsor me because I need more. Please and thank you.

A great, practical boot for daily us I'm writing this based on my wife's experience with these boots. She has found them to be a practical option for daily wear. The main positive she reports is comfort. She has worn them for full days at a time without any issues or breaking-in period. The flat heel and round toe seem to work well for her. They have also held up in wet conditions. She wore them in the rain on one occasion and mentioned that her feet stayed dry and the boots showed no immediate damage. For sizing reference, she is 5'2". The shaft of the boot hits her right below the knee, which was the fit she was looking for. One thing to note is the tread. It's fine for paved surfaces like sidewalks and floors, but she felt it wouldn't provide enough grip for uneven or rugged terrain. Overall, based on her use, these are a solid and functional boot for general daily and urban use.

Stretchy, comfy, tummy control I love these pants! They’re very comfortable but they also look stylish too. I love how they have semi deep pockets on either side. They’re beyond stretchy and feel thin enough to wear on summer nights. They definitely have a tummy control that makes me appear more flattering after having my son. I would recommend! I went with a medium (my usual size) and tbh I think I could have gone too small. I also like how they don’t go under my feet as I’m only 5”4,— but they don’t drag! Which is hard to find with wide leg pants like this, for me. You can’t go wrong with black pants anyways, especially when they include pockets. I would recommend.

Cute for a costume, not very warm Ordered for my clown costume. Looked so adorable!! I am 5’5 140-145 and have bigger calves. The material was stretchy and were not tight on my legs. The material was a little thin so I had to double up on the socks.

Elastic band not great Pretty comfy overall except the top piece of elastic in the waistband is thin and it irritated me because it felt like it was cutting in to me. The pants are very flowy, which I liked. They were long enough on me, which I was pleasantly surprised because I’m 5’8” and it’s hard to find pants long enough. I have a bit of a belly so I ordered size XL and they fit great, I’m a size 18 in jeans. Ignore the two spots on the pants in the pic- that was just from accidentally splashing water on them while washing my hands.
The length is perfect for 5’2” So cute. I’m 5’2” and the length is perfect for me. I’ll update review after I wash them. These are large. I’m just under 180lbs and carry my weight in my thighs.

Easy, Breezy, Comfy & Cool As a plus-size girly it is HARD to find palazzo pants that fit and also flow. But these are the ones! I love these pants so much that I bought two more pairs (chocolate & black) in the same size. I’ve worn them to church, date night, around the house, and running errands. They are as soft as your favorite sleep shirt but not see through. Flowy enough that you won’t get hot in the summer but don’t show panty lines. The ruching across the midsection is strong enough to pull high to natural waist or down around your hips and it’ll stay there. Some slight pilling after several washes but nothing big enough to be bothersome. (And my only other complaint is that, while your cell WILL fit in the pockets, excess weight WILL pull the waistband down a bit. Not all the way, just feels weird). If you are looking for breezy summer trousers, buy these. Edit: I am 5’8” and weigh 275lbs

Almost Perfect I honestly don’t know what magic they used to make these sweatpants, but the material is amazing. They are soft and stretchy with a velvety feel that’s comfortable to wear. I’m usually a size 8 and about 5'6", and I went with the medium in the 29" inseam, which ended up fitting really well. They look more polished than other sweatpants and I also appreciate how they don’t wrinkle easily, which makes them great for travel. The only thing I’m not a fan of is that the outline of the pockets shows through, and that does take away from the elevated look, especially because the pants feel high quality otherwise. Still, aside from that detail, I am really happy with these and love wearing them.

Super comfy and warm! These sweats are exactly as described. The material is a slick feel on the outside that I am not familiar with but they are definitely fleeced lined on he inside giving that warm feeling. I wore them with an oversized sweatshirt and sneakers and even though I was in a baggy look; I felt super cute and comfortable. My inseam is a 33. For reference. I am in the process of losing prednisone weight but still deal with an arthritic inflammatory disease. I’m 5’8 and 188#;. Longer legs with a shorter torso, a perfect fit is large or size US 12. Weight is mostly in tummy area now; 13# left to go. I still go one size up for extra looseness in clothing but it’s almost too big now to do so. Shoe size is about a 7 wide when inflamed. 7 medium when normal. US shoe size 7-8 is about normal depending on shoe. FYI: online purchases I try to keep rating simple. If I was in a local store and could hold and view it; 5 star would buy and take it home, one star would put it back on shelf and pick something else.#OnlyJesusSaves.

Runs a Little Big I ordered the small in petite and thought it would fit perfectly as I am 5', between 120-125 lbs. My waist measurement is more want is stated in their chart, but I feel the waist ran a bit big or sat too high that I had to fold fold down the waist. The pant itself however, is comfortable and soft with fleece lining the inside. The pants is well made, the waist band wide, thick and sewn on the edges preventing it from folding/rolling while wearing

These are good for a costume. They are very stretchy, I only tried them on with the costume, but for the 10 minutes I had them on I could tell they are going to be a single use item. Which is fine with me, just dont expect them to hold their shape to wear again. And I will probably need to pull them up throughout the night. With that being said I am short only 4'9. So there is alot of extra bunched up at hip.

Cute Lace Pantyhose Looks just like the seller's photos. I like the floral pattern of the lace. The price was good and they have stretch but they are lace so they are basically like a netting. I've seen some styles that have the lace pattern on top of a traditional pantyhose, this is not that. It is all lace and no other feature. There isn't any compression either, it's lace pantyhose and then a half inch waistband at the top. The waist came up to my natural waist but I am short. This may not be long enough for anyone over 5' 5". I've only worn these twice and have been gentle with them. I don't expect them to last longer than a season b/c they are all lace with no reinforcement anywhere. Next time I might look for a similar pair but that have a traditional nylon with the lace for durability and a reinforced toe (this pair has no reinforced toe).

Love them!! These are actually better than I was expecting. The fabric is pretty decent quality, and really smooth and soft to the touch. I am 5’3 and 175 lbs, and I got the XL with the 28” inseam. They fit really well, not too tight at all. There’s a slight tummy tuck quality, but it still doesn’t make as much of a difference for a tummy like mine. The length is perfect. I would say if you’re any taller than 5’4, then definitely get a longer inseam or you’ll be dealing with high-waters. The pockets are decently sized, they do bunch up a bit and look a little strange since the pants are skin tight, but they’re not too bad. I think these are great for the price, I’m definitely considering getting more colors…

Luv! Ok didn't have any full pics of these but i love them!! They fit very nicely and accentuate your waist beautifully! Note though, i am 5'4" and they are a perfect length on me, so if you are much taller than that: beware. The pockets on the front are not super usable either tbh, but they'll hold your phone (not if you are running or anything though, it'll pop right out).

STYLISH and COMFY! Highly recommended! I bought these pants in size medium in the color black. Im 5’5”, 137 lbs and they fit me true to size. I do have hips and a bum haha! They are a nice length on me and I could definitely wear a bit of a heal or sneakers or flip flops like I am wearing in my pics. They are very stretchy and comfortable and also cute! The functional pockets on each side are great for my phone. The non functional pockets on the rear are just so adorable and add style! The material is very soft and not shiny like some less expensive similar styles. I love these pants! I do recommend them!

👠 Easy slip on Design, Flexible Soft Fabric and Petite Inclusive Makes These A WINNER ! For Reference I am : 168lbs , 5’4” Size Large I'll be the first to admit, my initial reaction to these pants was skepticism. As I unboxed them, I thought to myself, "These are going to be a disappointment." The fabric, while incredibly soft to the touch, has a clingy quality to it that I wasn't sure I'd be comfortable with. But, as I decided to give them a fair shot, I slipped them on and immediately Changed me mind about them. Moving around in the clingy fabric, which I had initially seen as a drawback, actually is quite flexible and comfy. I received these in my normal size Large in the color Dark Gray. The fantastic part of all this is that they actually come in a petite length which is so wonderful for a person like me who is on the shorter side. The fact that I don’t have to worry about my pants dragging on the ground or having to pay to have them hemmed is FANTASTIC ! The petite length is perfectly proportioned and hits me at my ankle. As per my measurements the inseam is XXX” and not to mention that when I am wearing pants that are the right length, I instantly feel I look more put together and polished. I don't have to worry about tripping over my own hem, or not being able to show my shoes. Another feature that really stands out to me is the waistband. It's like a little piece of heaven no annoying, tight elastic that digs into my skin, just a smooth, flat design that stays put all day. I've had issues with waistbands rolling down in the past, but that's not a problem here. The only downside I've noticed is that the pockets could be a bit roomier. They're not tiny or anything, but if you've got big hands or like to carry a lot of stuff with you, you might find them a bit cramped. Also, because the fabric is on the thinner side, when I put my hands in my pockets, it can look a bit awkward - my hands kind of bulge out and it's not the most flattering look (check out the picture I've included to see what I mean). Despite those minor quibbles, I'm really enjoying these pants. The comfort level is top-notch. In closing, I have to say that these pants might not be everyone's cup of tea, and I'm still on the fence about whether they're really my style. However, The softness of the fabric is unparalleled, Whether I'm running errands, lounging around the house, or just need something to throw on to walk my pooch these ft the bill. I think they look best with sneakers or sandals, and can easily be dressed up or down depending on the occasion. They are being offered in 10 colors , in tall, petite and regular lengths and 6 sizes. They are machine washable And dryer friendly.

No complaints, dare I say perfection! I absolutely adore these! Honestly they are so comfortable it almost feels like nothing at all. They drape lovely on the body with the front pockets large enough but yet without adding additional bulk. Nice a smooth to touch and look. There’s a fair amount of stretch for enough wiggle room if you’re carrying a little extra water weight. The waistband is smooth without rolling down. And love that we have the choice to choose our inseam. I am almost always a size small, wearing a size 4us at 27”W/36”H at 124lbs. I chose size small petite. For these, if I could have tried on first I would have chose XS Petite. So something to think about ladies if you’re close to my sizing. The Small Petite is fine, and I’ll make them work. It’s my own fault for not looking closely at the size chart, it actually suggests I order XS. Please look closely prior to ordering. The coffee brown color is a great choice going into fall. I will be wearing these on repeat and think I’ll need another color! I can easily style these for a smart casual look with a sharp white button down, or a simple tee with a coordinating sweater tied around my neck. The length is nice too. I’m barely 5’ but with sneakers or a little kitten heels or ankle boots they hang perfect. Seriously worth the try with versatile styling options, comfy fit and front pockets make these a great value in my opinion.

Breathable yet relax fit that ultimately offers a very comfortable fit I ordered and received the wide leg yoga pants in dusky purple, tall with an 31 inch inseam. The yoga pants are comprised of a soft and breathable Lycra, has two side pockets and two faux pockets on the backside. This pants are versatile for leisure or any outdoor or casual wear whether yoga or other sporting activities. The Lycra offers a very breathable, relax and comfortable fit and value.

Great color and very stretchy I weigh 204 lbs and am 5’5. I’m more of an apple shape. They are stretchy and fit me really well. These were so cute and went really nicely with my holiday outfit. The color was perfect. They are silky texture so they didn’t snag along the legs but they snagged on my toenail and tore at the foot. Still worth it.

Perfect, flattering fit in a size petite This polyester/elastane blend is super comfortable and is a perfect size medium fit on me at 5'2" and 125 lbs. Smooth and soft with very little wrinkles right out of the box. Hit just above the ankle and have 2 large deep pockets on either side. This is the 24" inseam option, so I could have gone a few inches longer perhaps. Great with sandals or boots for travel or exercise. Easy to move around with limited stretch.

SUPER soft and comfortable! Super comfortable, well-fitting leggings! These are very, very soft. Sometimes the leggings that are described as “buttery” are kind of slick, but these a just super soft. I love the bright red color. The pockets on the side are great for holding my cell phone. The length is perfect and they fit all around (I’m 5’5” and about 125 lbs). It’s high-waisted and feels very comfortable around my waist, not too tight but enough stretchiness to fit snugly. I am wearing black underwear in my pictures so you can see they aren’t see through. If I had to make one complaint, I don’t really like the seams that run up the front of the legs, but definitely not a deal-breaker. I know I’ll wear these often!

SUPER soft and comfortable! Super comfortable, well-fitting leggings! These are very, very soft. Sometimes the leggings that are described as “buttery” are kind of slick, but these a just super soft. I love the bright red color. The pockets on the side are great for holding my cell phone. The length is perfect and they fit all around (I’m 5’5” and about 125 lbs). It’s high-waisted and feels very comfortable around my waist, not too tight but enough stretchiness to fit snugly. I am wearing black underwear in my pictures so you can see they aren’t see through. If I had to make one complaint, I don’t really like the seams that run up the front of the legs, but definitely not a deal-breaker. I know I’ll wear these often!

Buttery soft & sooo comfy, but boho Sooo I am extremely torn over these leggings. The color is absolutely perfect, matches the photo exactly and is just what i wanted! Not to mention, the material of these leggings are next level. Soooo buttery soft, stretchy and comfortable. I would say they are on the thinner side but not see through from what I could tell. I bought the beige/khaki color in size S/M. So the only actual issue I have with these is that, in my opinion, they do run bigger in size. Mind you, I am pretty petite (5’3, 100lbs; basically no butt sadly) .. and i consistently have trouble finding pants that fit me perfectly. I would honestly love if they had an XS/S sizing option. These were just slightly baggy in the waistband area and upper thigh/hip. I was able to roll down the waistband which did help make them appear less loose and more form fitting, so with that being a possible way to wear these (and the possibility/hope they may shrink after washing & drying in high temps) I am conflicted on returning these or not. I mean these truly are the softest leggings I’ve ever felt and the pricing was great. Curious if anyone else felt these ran bigger than expected?

Soft, comfortable, lightweight, and machine washable! These leggings are super buttery soft, they stretch well in all directions, they’re lightweight, and very comfortable! I’m 5’4” and am a slender person. The small leggings fit well, not too tight, nor to loose. The material is great! It washed good on a normal cycle, did good drying on medium heat, and didn’t shrink! Definitely would buy more!

Love, but watch the length and size So, ladies, here's the skinny... very much pun intended 😆. But seriously, I am 5'5, 150lbs, long legs shorter torso, 36C.. i got the l/xl in red. Whilst I am of average height, albeit longer legs, I still found these fall after movement, about mid to lower calf. I might imagine a bit tighter after even a cold wash. AND, if you get a color like I did, WASH IN COLD AND HANG DRY OUTSIDE OVER GRASS.. SEPARATELY from anything!!!!! You've been warned lol!!! The color is beautiful, vibrant red.. i can imagine the other colors, which i will be buying more of, in a larger size 😂. Oh, and it. Is. SOFT! O. M. G. Yummy. Like the velvety side of a 2 layer blanket, fabric. The pic is it next to my burnt orange blanket.. the underside 😁. I took 1 star for the length. It's borderline, and for the size, it's 1 size too small. I like them comfy, not restricting.

Pants in a pinch I purchased the lite khaki and grey. They are okay in terms of fit. I’m 5’2” at 130lbs and bought size small. You’ll need blouses that cover the camel-toe and undies lines. They feel super smooth and the thick band holds the tummy a bit. Black undies will show through the lite khaki but the grey seem to mask it pretty well. If you need pants you can just throw on, then these are good for the price. The camel-toe, somewhat sheer, and show of lines is what bugs me about them.

My HOT new pants! The name “pull on pants” make me think of leggings, but these are completely lined dress pants - and they’re adorable! Great stretch and color (I bought them in red). These will be perfect for dinner on our upcoming cruise, date nights with hubs, or even to a school function. I’m 5’2” and although these could be about 3” shorter and hit more like peddle pushers, I’ll be leaving them at this length shown in my picture.

Don't recommend for C-section Mommas, but super comfy and stretchy These are super soft and stretchy, and great for maternity or postpartum to wear. They have a comfortable but form-fitting style, and open up wide along the bottom, almost to the point of bell bottoms, not yoga pants, which I will occasionally trip over so I am deducting a star. However, I think it's fair to point out that I am a short 5'2" and 225lbs postpartum 16 months, and ordered the petite XL and they're still pretty long on me. The extra fabric for the belly is about 7" wide, but stretches well. I don't recommend these for C-section mommas, as the crotch is sewn low and sits right near the uterus area postpartum. I love the color and while it does have pockets, they're not very deep (my smart phone sticks out a bit from them), and the back pockets are sewn shut and purely aesthetic, which is an annoying trend in women's clothes - especially those made for moms. The fabric is made from quality materials and they shipped quickly in a vacuum-sealed bag.

So comfortable I’m 5’3 and these pants fit perfectly. They are extremely comfortable and soft. I absolutely love them and will order more colors.

These cargo pants fit like a glove, with a good amount of stretch. The material feels like butter when you put them on. The length is just right for my 5’ 4”. The navy blue color is perfect for fall. These pants will work good for my school job.

I love the look and feel of the fabric, and the style of the cargo pockets. I ordered these in dark gray size large. I usually wear bottoms in numerical size US12. The inseam measured 31 inches. For me (5' 4" height) these are perfect if I am wearing shoes that aren't completely flat. They work with my athletic shoes which have some height to them (see photo).I love the look and feel of the fabric.Thes do not have an elastic-banded waist, the crossover style waist stretches due to the stretchy fabric they are mode from. In my photo you can see that I measured the unstretched waist of the pants (flat on the floor) at about 14 inches side to side. My waist is in the mid-thirty inches and these were comfortable. But I would not want anyone to be able to see every bump and bulge of my waist area while wearing these, so for sure I am wearing a longer length top that covers my stomach.These pants are pretty form fitting through the stomach and thighs until they flare out and are wide around the calves.I really like the style and interest that the cargo pockets add.

Long pants I'm 5'6" and usually don't have an issue with length but these were a good 6 inches long than my foot. It's is very stretchy and comfortable material everywhere but the band around the stomach. It's fairly thick material as well which will be good in the winter. There are 2 hand pockets, 2 back pockets, and 2 side pockets. Seems to be made well and has held up well so far.

Sooo Comfortable!! These boots are so comfortable. I was in the market for some new ones when my old ones just gave out on me. I usually order from a department store but decided to go this route. So glad I did. I read the reviews and I was sold. I literally wore these all day with no issues. I like the fact that they don't make a lot of noise when walking on tile (an issue I had with my last boot). Everyone heard me coming...lol! I will say the reviews are pretty accurate on the wide leg gap. Even the models on the site show large gaps. However, for me it wasn't that big of an issue. I'm 5'7" and weigh about 167 pounds and the leg gap was not an issue with a standard legging as you can see in the picture. Jeans would probably fill in the gap more. All in all, a great boot that is comfortable, stylish and durable.

I will say the reviews are pretty accurate on the wide leg gap. Even the models on the site show large gaps. However, for me it wasn't that big of an issue. I'm 5'7" and weigh about 167 pounds and the leg gap was not an issue with a standard legging as you can see in the picture. Jeans would probably fill in the gap more. All in all, a great boot that is comfortable, stylish and durable.

Couldn't ask for better I have other Yogipace pants and love them all. However, I wanted to review these 25-inch Small Yogipace joggers. I am 5' tall and 120 pounds. If you are short, you can relate to how hard it is to find pants that fit. I always bought cotton pants in the past but I feel very differently now. These joggers are very lightweight and feel very good on my skin. I wash them in a mesh bag and hang dry them. They dry fast. The joggers are beyond comfortable. A very flexible waist and a flattering fit. I posted a picture of the navy and black ones I bought and a picture of the navy's waistband. I've happily worn them in temperatures between 40 and 70 degrees. I have fleece-lined Yogipace pants for winter. While they are a bit pricey, all of my Yogipace pants are wearing beautifully. To me, they are very worth the investment.

Comfortable I'm so happy! I've always had issues finding long enough anything. I'm 5'8 140 lbs. I have a shorter torso and longer legs (31.5 from inner top thigh to ankle bone). I usually buy 34 in inseam because I don't like pants to ride up too far above my ankle when sitting. These are perfect. They're light weight, not winter worthy, and breathable. I have thinner legs, thigh is 21.5 in. around, so they aren't tight but fitting and not overly baggy. My waist/ hips area widest point is 39 in. and medium fits well. I do have animals and while hair attaches, it does not stick. If I see any I can easily shake it off. I always wash in cold and line dry so I cannot attest to the dryer and any shrinkage issues. This is my first full day of wearing and if anything changes I'll update.

NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY!!!!!!!!! NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY. I was to start this review with saying do not order if you’re a short girl. I, thankfully, know how to sew and was able to hem the pants myself. For the average shirt girl, I would strongly avoid, unless you can sew or want to pay a tailor to alter them. I am 5’2 and had to cut off five inches from the bottom. You can see in my photo that I have it pinned in one, I was measuring for the hem. The length look ridiculous, honestly it looked like I was wearing scuba fins with how much fabric there was at the bottom. To GTP: either shorten the length to adjust to the average woman’s height (5’3-5’5) or provide a petite option because it truly was ridiculous. The pros are that they are stretchy and indeed comfortable. The quality is as expected from TGP, as always it’s not great but it’s not bad. They look cute, but they don’t look like real jeans either.

Comfortable and Reliable Bibs for Women I bought these 2 sizes up, because the XL was on closeout (for reference, I'm 5'4 and about 180 lbs. I am wider at the hips and smaller at the waist, more pear shaped). I knew I'd have some alterations to do, but it ended up being as simple as sewing on four buttons 1.5 inches in on each side. For the price and minimal effort of taking it a few sizes back down, I'm very happy with these. The value for money can't be matched. Mobility is good, the zipper on the big pocket works nice and the smaller pockets at the front make for convenient and secure storage. These bibs are unlined, which will work great for offroading or any general outdoor activity on cold mornings or evenings. The thickness of the material is comfortable enough that wearing a pair of leggings and a hoodie underneath will allow for different comfort levels. On the photos, you can see where the first 3 are the fitted version, after the buttons have been sewn on. The other 3 are of the original product, wearing it with the buttons that come on the bibs.

Perfectly loose, comfy, and colorful I have these pants in 4 different designs. The pair I’m wearing in these pictures are my favorites, but they all are very cute and colorful. I had ankle reconstruction surgery and wanted pants that were comfortable, soft, cute, easy on and easy off, and would allow easy access to my ankle and foot during PT. They also had to be long enough and loose enough to cover my shoes…I have to wear 2 different shoes because my surgical foot and ankle is so swollen I had to buy a tennis shoe 2 sizes larger and wider than I normally wear. These check all the boxes. They are perfect. I will wear them even after I heal. I’ve worn them to PT, dinner, church, and during a day out with my husband. I’m getting my moneys worth from these pants. Wash on cold, gentle and hang to dry. No ironing needed. They will pack and travel well. I did return 1 pair because the material seemed different, almost plastic like. But the other 4 I have are great. 5’4” tall. 132 lbs. Size M, which fit me a bit loose. My others are a size S and the fit is a little snugger, but still very comfy. There is some stretch and no shrinkage. The tank top is ANA from JC Penney.

Need to decide between sizes - do you want a compression feel or not? Great pair of leggings... I actually ordered 3 sizes (M, SM, XSM). I thought I was going to settle on the Small but after trying on the XS, I decided that I wanted a pair of these as well. I have read many reviews (I have the capri version as well) and one thing that stuck out was the number of people who said size up and the number of people who said to size down. I thought that was weird, but I understand why. It boils down to body type and level of compression you are looking for; all 3 sizes DO fit me as they aren't that different (material does offer a good amount of stretch). For reference, I am 5' 7" and wiegh about 120# with a straight body type (think boy - no real curves). I can imagine that if you carry more weight in your waist vs. hips/legs that could make a difference on which size you order. And I will add this: I had purchased other leggings/capris for about $20 - $25. For a little more, I got some that I love vs the others that I liked. I think these are like 100% polyester and the others were not. So they are on the shiny side (that I like). I haven't worn them nor washed them enough to see the long term wear. They are thin. I don't know how warm they will be in the winter. You'd have to read other reviews for those aspects. And if you go with a size that fits you nicely but isn't super snug, chances are on the capris, they will bag and ride up. That is what bugged me, so going with a smaller size was right for me. P.S. I ADDED A PIC OF THE COLOR. IT IS MORE PURPLE THEN A TEMPERED STEEL (which to me means they are more grey/charcoal colored which these are not).

Petite Small vs. Petite Medium I found the sizing chart to be somewhat confusing, so I have notes on both Petitie Small and Petite Medium! I posted a picture of me wearing the Petite Small and the other picture is me in the Petite Medium. It's difficult to tell by the photo but the Medium is looser. Petite Small (actual pants measurements): Waist 30" circumference Hips 38.5" Calf 13.5 Hip to crotch: 11.5" approx Petite Medium (actual pants measurement): Waist 31" Hips: 40" Calf: 16" Me: waist 28" Hips 35" Calf 13" The small fits on me, but snug in the calf. The medium also fits. The waist band is thick 2.5" but does have stretch. The actual material of the pants does not have stretch. Overall, the medium is looser like sweat pants but looks fine with a fitted top! I wear both the small and medium! A lot of my coworkers wear these in a tighter fit especially if they are pear-shaped and these pants look like leggings on them. :)

great flattering scrub pants I am obsessed with these scrubs. I am 5'4" with a 30" waist and 39" hips. I am usually a medium in pants and generally prefer a looser fit, but with the waist not being adjustible I decided to order both a Small regular and Medium petite. The mediums were just too loose in the waist and the moment I bent down the waistline fell with it. The length on the petites look better to me with my height, but I have a very athletic build and the cuff was too tight over my muscular calves. On the other hand, the size smalls fit PERFECTLY in the waist. I was worried they'd be too tight in the hips/rear but the fabric has a surprising amount of stretch and I've been very comfortable with them at work (I work in a veterinary clinic so I spend a lot of time squatting and wrangling animals). They did shrink ever so slightly with my covid-compliant hot wash/hot dry cycles (the photos of the small size pants were taken post-wash) but again, still an amazing fit, highly recommend

Comparing NCL32 Cotton Leggings + HDL Active Flex What I ordered: NCL32 Cotton: - sizes: x-small & small -1in waistband HDL Active Flex: - sizes: small and medium -1in waistband My Measurements: - waist: 26in - hips: 37.5 - height: 5’3” - weight: 123lbs Here is a breakdown of the specs: HDL Active Flex: - 84% polyester, 16% spandex - made in Vietnam -buttery soft -sizes offered: small-3x -doesn’t shrink -unavailable in 1in waistband capri style with various sizing NCL32 Cotton: - 95% cotton, 5% spandex - made in the US -soft, but not nearly as soft as the Active Flex series -sizes offered: x-small–5x -available in 1in waistband capri style w/ sizing between x-small–3x Conclusion: I tried on both leggings and prefer the softer texture and fit of the Active Flex series. Since polyester in the Active Flex is more durable than cotton in NCL32, I wanted to actually size down to an x-small to achieve that smoothed out, perfectly slim look while still maintaining comfort and stretch. I’m disappointed in the company for not offering that size as an option in full-length or capri in the Active Flex 1in waistband. It seems the closest fabric blend being offered in a capri style is their Buttery Soft series at 92% polyester, 8% spandex, but they only offer One Size, One Size Plus, 1x–3x, and 3x–5x. I will gladly buy from this seller again if they make the Active Flex with a 1in waistband in x-small and begin to offer it in the capri style. For now, I’ll be sticking with Active Flex in size small to see how long it lasts.

I will gladly buy from this seller again if they make the Active Flex with a 1in waistband in x-small and begin to offer it in the capri style. For now, I’ll be sticking with Active Flex in size small to see how long it lasts.

Good Leggings - Will buy again I'm sick of those soft or so called velvety stretchy spandex nylon-like leggings! I love the cotton ones. I feared buying from online. You got all these size differences with this one being a size down (or 2 or 3 or 4) and that one being 1 to 2 to 4 sizes up. I ordered ONE pair as experiment. Perfect. I compared them to my No Boundaries cotton leggings and there was barely a difference. I buy the 15-16 large in theirs and bought extra large in these. I normally wear size 9 or 10 jeans. My size leggings in No Boundaries brand should be 11-13. But I like mine loose - less clingy.l I'm 5 ft 4 and 133 pounds if that helps. Don't hesitate. They are good leggings.

Comfortable and cute leggings They are nice soft cotton. I got small and they fit perfectly. I’m 5 feet tall and 115 pounds. My only complaint is they were obviously used because the tag inside showed signs of frequent machine washing! I also have the white leggings from the same company and that tag looks brand new. I love them so much that I just washed them really well and I’m ok with that this time!

Not a 26.5 inseam Love the pants, but only giving four stars because they're not 26.5 inseam they are more of a 27.5 to 28 inseam. I just rolled at they waist band and they fit purest. I have very heavy legs and they hide that perfectly.

Classy casual pant Bought these pants because the reviews were so good. So adding about great review of my own! Love these pants!!! They are so comfy and perfect for my job. I feel a little dressed up while being casual at the same time. The waistband is a little snug fitting but I didn’t mind the fit while wearing. If you’re someone who likes a looser fit in the waist maybe size up. I am 5’4” and usually wear a 2/4. These fit great. The length of the pant is exactly what I like and the fun barrel fit looks great if that’s the look you’re going for! Plus the material of the pant is not too thin so I feel covered.

Great pant! These are such a fun pant. My first time purchasing barrel jeans and they are pretty damn cute. I’m 5’6 and about 170lbs newly post partum and the larges fit great. Was surprised by the quality of the jeans pockets are deep. Maybe a little long for my liking but I’m picky about length. Definitely worth a try if you’re on the fence!
These are perfect length for shorties like me: I’m 5’2 barely. I got a size Medium. And wear a size 8. It has an elastic waist. And they aren’t over powering baggy. If you know what I mean: 10/10!

Must buy! 5’6, 140 lbs, and I ordered a size small. I LOVE them. I can wear them all day. I washed and dried them like normal as well. Will def be ordering in another color/wash.

*NOT* for the fabric sensitive 5’10” 173lbs (size 8 US) So I was looking for a tall girl friendly, Alo sweat pant dupe (65% cotton and polyester). The description says that this is what it is fabric wise. WELL good news? It’s almost tall girl friendly- I can wear flat shoes and be okay- if you’re over 5’10” I’d look elsewhere. I’d also recommend sizing up. I’m usually a medium, I sized up for a baggier look, and well it fits like a traditional medium on me. Where my issue really is? I can’t tell where the 65% cotton is. I’m super sensitive to fabrics and don’t love polyester but appreciate the flexibility it provides. The lining of these pants is 95% polyester and 5% spandex. It’s giving sateen sheets for pants.

First of all, the side panel color is not a white or light cream as is pictured but beige/tan. They are otherwise cute, made well and mostly fit to size. I unfortunately have to pass on them because at 5’8” tall they are simply too short for me!

A good inseam fit for up to 6’1” women!! I gave these pants a shot and at medium they were too loose annnd way too long, so here is what my sister said: THE Perfect inseam length for women over 5’9”. I would suggest ordering your true size because they’re stretchy but I would not go up a size. Even if you gain 30-40 lbs they will still fit, I promise. The side pockets are nice and lay flat. The seams are well finished & they are incredibly comfortable. I am 20 weeks and all belly & thighs so these are appropriate for work, church, running errands, or going out to lunch.

Wonderful dressy maternity pants I hesitated on these at first, because I’d gotten a pair of “wide-legged work pants” for another brand and they were awful! These are pretty much the same material as those, but they used a different pattern to make these, and they’re actually very flattering on me! Not to mention that this material is extremely comfortable. I was also a little concerned about them being too short. I have another pair of maternity pants from this company, G4Free, and the description of those said the regular was a 31” inseam (which is what I normally need,) and those were the perfect length. These state that the regular is a 29” inseam, but when I tried them on, they were plenty long! So I’m glad I didn’t order the Tall this time. Just for reference, I’m 5’6” and was 165 lbs in a size 10 before pregnancy. Now I’m six months along and a few lbs heavier, but my pre-pregnancy size fit perfectly. (With this company, a women’s 10 is a Large.) You probably can’t tell in my photo, but this material is nice and smooth once you put it on. There’s a little split on the sides at the bottom of the pant legs that adds a nice touch to the overall look. I really like these, and can pair a dressier top with them and look really nice when I go out. I highly recommend these.
Great buy!! They fit great a little tight. I wish it was stretchy. It does fit me I am plus sized I weigh 230. My calves are pretty big.The shoe size is 9 I normally wear between 8-9 size.

Nice looking boot! Recommend! They are a nice looking boot I ordered my normal size which is a size 7. They fit, I feel the shoe area is narrow and the shaft is a little long for me. I am 5’2” & my feet are on the wider side. I will have to wear thin socks. The top of the boot comes to my knee so it’s uncomfortable when walking upstairs in them. I did however receive a compliments on how they look. I would caution you of you are shorter than 5’2 as these are truly knee high…. Regarding the look and overall feel, the boot is cute. I told the heel and sole. I like the material and it looks like leather. Recommend.

Get Ready Boots… Groovy. Retro. Spunky…and most of all, Comfortable. Sleek boots at a great price. Appearance is the primary feature of these boots. They elongate my leg which is definitely a plus, considering that I am 5’3. I feel comfortable walking and standing with these boots on. They also give me a confident stride. I ordered the lighter color, which has a hint of pink, depending on the light. They do attract scuff marks and dirt easily. That means I need to watch my step (lol) to keep the boots spotless. However, they do clean up easily with a white non-abrasive sponge. I’m not sure what type of material was used for these boots, but it’s amazingly buttery. Supple and flexible. The heel appears steep, however, if you measure from the top of the platform to the top of the heel, that’s the actual height of the heel. The inside of the boot is cushioned. The zipper is durable. The boots run true to size. The boots are sewn and not glued completely. Let me see. What else would you like to know ? Water resistant?- Yes. Slip resistant?- Very. The soles are made of textured rubber material. Easy to put on ?-Definitely. And that is due to the length of the zipper which provides a wide opening when unzipped. At the end of the day…”These boots are made for walkin’ .” (N.S.)

true chocolate brown, roomy, stays up, and comfortable! For reference, these are my true measurements: bust - 38” waist - 31.5” hips 46”, and I’m 5’6 around 185. I hold a good amount of weight in my hips/glutes, and my stomach is on the natueal flatter end, with a natural lower belly protruding. I purchased a Large! PROS: I really like the pants and truly underestimated them! For someone with a hourglass shape/bigger waist to hip ratio, I was looking for wide trousers that had room around my leg but cinched at the waist, and these pants do! It has elastic in the back and a button/zipper in the front, with belt loops all around. With buttoning the pants, the elastic in the back allowed for a secure & tight, yet still comfortable fit around my waist—with and w/o shirt tucking. I am closer to 5’6.5 and a half than 5’6, with my torso & leg being of equal length. I ordered the 31” inseam pants, and wore them with a pair of 1/1.5 inch platform loafers; they are the perfect long length without dragging on the floor. CON: the only issue I had is the loose pouch that I got around my crotch area (see pic). Pretty typical for my body type with any loose-fitting pants, just because of its tailoring for straighter figures. Unless I pose a certain way, it is slightly apparent when walking. But doesnt bother me too much—as long as you wear an appropriate shirt that compliments the bagginess, it won’t pull in as much attention. I will be ordering in different colors!

Superior slacks! These are flattering, most excellent! Perfect quality and fit; 30” inseam length actually accommodates flats, tennis, or kitten heals. Stunning boots and a perfect fit (true knee-high! These boots are absolutely stunning in person. The color is gorgeous, the embroidery detail is beautiful, and they look way more expensive than they are. They fit perfectly in the foot and are comfortable to walk in, with a nice chunky heel that feels stable. Just note they are true knee-high boots. I’m 5’4” and they come all the way up to my knees, which is a little taller than I expected. They’re also slightly wider in the calf than I’m used to, but honestly it worked out fine and made them easy to pull on. Overall, I’m obsessed with these and would definitely recommend them!

Some loose threads on pocket | Great Fit | Very Well Dyed These fit really well! There was little to no gapping in the back of the waist. They were also a perfect length. I am 5'7" tall, but struggle with finding pants that are long enough, and these did splendidly. They are very comfortable too with good streatch and the zipper and button were easy - no sticking or difficulty. They are overdyed and very black - they look fantastic, but take care when washing these! I always wash my blacks and grays together and didn't have an issue, but some of my black jeans look a little more black hahaha. I washed on cold with detergent and white vinegar and did not experience any shrinking or issues with seams. Overall, I am very impressed with these. Despite the small quality issue with the pockets, which was easily mended, these are an excellent pair of pants. Also, one last thing to note, these are straight leg - NOT skinny jeans, so they are open at the bottom just like the picture shows. Just a heads up if you are thinking these are skinny jeans.

Comfortable Stretch Jeans – More Bootcut Than Skinny I’m not tall, but I have long legs and a short torso, so I usually buy tall sizes in pants to get the right length (32" inseam.) These jeans were perfect in the inseam – finally a pair that actually reaches my ankles without looking cropped. The fabric feels more like jeggings material than traditional denim, with lots of extra stretch and softness. They’re very comfortable for all-day wear and move easily without feeling stiff. The description says skinny leg, but on me the fit is closer to a slim bootcut, which I actually ended up liking for a more relaxed, everyday look. For sizing, I’m a 32 waist and followed the size chart, ordering the XXL because I expected a tighter fit. I definitely could have sized down – these run true to size, not small like many stretch jeans. If you’re between sizes, I’d recommend choosing the smaller one for a more fitted feel. Overall, these are comfy, flattering, and great for anyone who needs extra length with plenty of stretch. These also have a good quality and seems it would be durable enough to last for years to come. I’d order again, just one size smaller next time.

Soft, stretchy, flattering tall jeans—perfect everyday fit 🖤👖✨ These jeans deserve five stars. They are soft, stretchy, stylish, and genuinely comfortable right out of the package. The denim has a nice amount of give without feeling thin, and the fit is classic and flattering. 💖 I ordered the Medium Tall, and they fit me exactly how I like my jeans to fit—not too tight, not baggy, and no digging in anywhere. For reference, I’m 5'8", 127 lbs, with a 29" waist, and these are a great everyday fit. The length is perfect, and I love the dark blue wash with the subtle faded look—very versatile and easy to dress up or down. Construction is also excellent: smooth zipper, nicely stitched seams, and well-made pockets. The front pockets are big enough for your whole hand, and the back pockets are well placed and roomy enough for a large phone. For around $50, these are a great deal, and I know I’ll get a lot of use out of them. They are now the best jeans I own! 👏 Highly recommended! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Might need a size down- I find myself pulling them up all day These jeans are nice quality. The material is soft and stretchy. A good deal of stretch. They’re definitely comfortable to wear. They look like good quality jeans and are a flattering cut. They run true to size and are just the right length. I am 5’10, 36” waist, 41” hips, and 31” inseam. I typically wear a 14 tall/long. If I had gotten the flares I would get 34 inseam but for skinny I got 32 and they’re perfect. Though it’s more of a fitted straight cut than skinny. Which I prefer anyway. They’re high waisted which I also like. My only issue is I find myself pulling them up all day long. I ordered xl tall which is the size I would normally get, but maybe I should have gone with the large, maybe then they wouldn’t do that. It’s slightly annoying but other than that I really like them.

shorter than expected These black denim jeans are well made and comfortable but they are not 31" long as stated. As you can see in the photo, the inseam measures 30" in the size Medium Tall. I have lots of denim this length, pretty standard, so I was hoping for some longer ones to wear with heals. I haven't washed them yet but if they shrink they will actually be too short. For reference, I am 5'10" tall, 150 pounds and wear a size 29 jeans or a size 10/medium in most clothes. I am rather straight up and down with not a lot of curves. These are comfy in the waist and a little big in the hips and upper legs. Straight jeans are more in than skinny jeans now so the cut of these are better than true skin tight skinny jeans. At 5'10" I am at the very bottom of the height range but I wish I had ordered the extra tall.

Really warm, unique In the item description it mentions a 2-in platform, there is no platform hidden in these boots, they actually feel like slippers and there's just a small bit of cushioning between you and the ground.(The sole measures 1 in) I'm actually very happy for this because I did not want a platform but I took a chance on them anyway because they were so unique looking. When I open the package they did have a unpleasant odor, it wasn't overpowering but I'm also not sensitive to these things. The odor dissipated after I left them out of the packaging for a couple of days. I have thicker calves (16.5 in) And I am normally a US size 8, I ordered these in an 8.5 and I'm glad I did because they are perfectly snug on my foot with a pair of thin socks and I'm afraid the eight might have been too tight. I did have to struggle to get them up my thick calf but once they got there they were actually comfortable and didn't restrict, but I would not suggest this for anyone with calves thicker than mine. Also they are very warm, but I imagine they would be terrible in rain or snow since they are just a faux fur fabric. The soul is a brown rubber with treads. To give you an idea of height I am 5'3 And these came up to right under my knees.

Gonna order more! All I can say is....where have these pants been all of my life?! They are so lightweight and comfortable that I feel like I'm not even wearing pants. These will be perfect for Florida weather, possibly even in the summer. I'm 5'9, pants size 14, and ordered the one size extra long, and they are plenty long enough. I'm definitely going to get more colors, but I will try the regular length inseam option because there are more colors/patterns to choose from. Update: I ordered a one size regular length. They are long enough, but just barely. The elastic just comes to my ankles and I have to wear them at my belly button, the the long version I could wear the rise up higher, which I really wanted to fold the waist over a bit with the long size anyway. With the long version I end up with a more puffy look at the ankles and she shorter version is not puffy. I think the long version could accommodate someone up to 6' tall but they may get the less puffy look at the ankles. Overall, with my height, I like both. I also like the more fitted look I get with this brand compared to other brands I have tried that are very big and oversized in the width of the legs. I do wish there were more color/pattern options (especially in long size!) because that's one thing that other brands have available that this brand does not. I did find that with other brands that the inseam listed in the product description was longer than the actual pants, sometimes the pants were shorter by 3". Both types I've tried of this brand have accurate overall length and inseam. I do recommend this brand!

Best Cherokee pant I am 5’7, 150 lbs with an athletic build. I wear a small very well in a regular seam. In clogs these pants do not become high waters either. I enjoy these more than other cheaper Cherokee scrubs because of the soft waist band. It provides tummy control support and also prevents the pant from slipping down which I can really appreciate. The waistband also has a drawstring to tighten. These scrubs are the same great material which is soft and gets softer with each wash. The waistband is very stretchy and it looks great. Love the pockets, I own 3 pairs of these and they are my go to pant! You will not regret spending the extra coin on these pants at all.

Thick thighs and a booty beware. Ordered a size large as suggested on the chart. I’m 5”10 and 200 lbs. Was hard to pull over my thighs and butt but once I got past that they fit great. Plenty of room in the waist. And plenty long. A little see through if going with the white color.

Okay so I was really hesitant to buy these. I'm 5'8" and was really worried about the length. But I took a risk and assumed I could wear them as capris, worst case. Well I'm glad I bought them. Hips are 41" around and waist is 32" so it can be hard to find pants that fit my ass that actually stay up around the waist. These pants fit my body great! They're a little snug to pull over the booty but once they're up- they're a dream. They're slightly tighter in the waist than most pants so if you are apple shaped or have a wider tummy than booty, these may not be a good fit for you. I do have muscular legs and calves and they are not uncomfortably tight in these pants. In fact, they're appropriately loose even when sitting. No tight pulling and has slight looseness and give when seated. I ordered a size medium based off reviews and they fit exactly how I expected. Large would have been too baggy and not flattering on my body for my personal taste. They do have quite a bit of stretch so I'm glad I sized-down. I'm sure they'll stretch as I wear them and I don't wash my pants after every wear so a large may have been problematic. Apologies for the crappy angle on pics. I don't have a full length mirror and had to make do with the mirror I have! I even reccomended these for my sister who is 5'10". 6ft sister MAY be okay in a large if the hem is a bit longer. They'd definitely be more classic ankle pants. At the ankle, no rolling. They're seemingly petty wrinkle resistant which is awesome. Tried them on right out of the bag and there are no creases from packaging. I will likely buy these in other colors. I purchased navy and black. Black is pictured. See no roll, one roll, two rolls. Also tried to get a pic of the waist band and rise of pants. Ordered a Medium. 5'8", 170lbs, hourglass. Great fit.

Mine came w/HOLES + they fit LOOSE on me They are very stretchy with lots of room for progressing pregnancy, I guess. I am 5'3" / 120 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually wear size small. They hit at the ankle on me and are a too little loose to be a true "skinny" jean. I like my skinny jeans to look more like leggings. You can see how loose they look in my webcam pics. For temporary pants to get you through the 6 months you need something to wear, they seem okaaay. On the pair I received, I noticed at least 2 holes (1 large, 1 small) in the back, on the stretchy part near the hip & above where it meets the jeans. This was straight out of the package; I have not washed them yet. So I guess I lost the quality lottery with my pair. I was desperate for pants though so I am planning on repairing them myself with a needle/thread. If the rips were in the front, I might be less lazy about returning them. It's been difficult for me to find maternity pants that are small enough, but somehow these aren't as loose as others. It's still pretty early in my pregnancy (not quite halfway yet) so I don't expect the belly to fit just yet, but they are fairly loose around my thighs/hips so I feel I need to constantly pull them up. Not the most comfortable, but better than no pants. I'm hoping I like them better later in my pregnancy when my belly can help to hold them up. If after washing they are still quite loose, I'll just take them in a bit along the leg seams so I don't feel so frumpy.

Great find. Highly recommend These jeans feel wonderful and fit exactly as I had hoped. I debated on what size to buy and finally decided on 28S. Some reviews suggest that the jeans are heavy weight, but I find them to be just right. Not too heavy and not too light. Highly recommend purchasing.

Great pants for the price! These are great! I'm a college student with a 40-hour/week summer internship where I can't wear jeans, and I don't own a ton of business casual-type clothing. I only had two pairs of dress pants, so I wanted another pair to break up the outfit rotation (and to make laundry easier) but didn't want to break the bank on something I probably will only wear once during the rest of the year. These fit the bill! I am 5'7" and 140 pounds with a 28 inch waist and a 39 inch hip at the fullest point (aka I have a huge butt). I ordered the medium, and apart from being just slightly too long (by maybe half an inch, but someone with a smaller butt would probably need a hemming job), they fit really well! I like that they're a higher rise in the front (so my tummy is contained) as I just find that really comfortable. They're VERY comfy, flexible, and stretchy, so I could see someone who wears an 8 or maybe even a 10 at my height fitting in these well (I usually wear a 4 or 6 in pants depending on brand). They don't have pockets, so they're pretty much leggings made out of nicer material, but they do have inside and outside seams so they look like any other skinny fit dress pant. I ordered navy and it's very true to color. Thanks, Amazon!

I only had two pairs of dress pants, so I wanted another pair to break up the outfit rotation (and to make laundry easier) but didn't want to break the bank on something I probably will only wear once during the rest of the year. These fit the bill! I am 5'7" and 140 pounds with a 28 inch waist and a 39 inch hip at the fullest point (aka I have a huge butt). I ordered the medium, and apart from being just slightly too long (by maybe half an inch, but someone with a smaller butt would probably need a hemming job), they fit really well! I like that they're a higher rise in the front (so my tummy is contained) as I just find that really comfortable. They're VERY comfy, flexible, and stretchy, so I could see someone who wears an 8 or maybe even a 10 at my height fitting in these well (I usually wear a 4 or 6 in pants depending on brand). They don't have pockets, so they're pretty much leggings made out of nicer material, but they do have inside and outside seams so they look like any other skinny fit dress pant. I ordered navy and it's very true to color. Thanks, Amazon!

Great fit and SO comfy!! However... had to size down. I’ve always heard great things about Grey’s scrubs, so I figured I’d give them a shot. With that being said, I am beyond glad I did! These scrubs are SO SOFT. I love the side pocket with the zipper and the slits at the bottom of the pants. When I ordered these the first time, I ordered a Small Tall. The length fit perfect but they were baggier than my liking, so I did have to send them back for an XS Tall. For reference, I’m 5’6 and around 135 lbs. They fit my waist and legs great. The length could be just slightly longer for my liking (I have long legs and a short torso, so my pants sit up higher on my waist— and I also have slim legs so finding pants that fit my body type can be hard) but overall I’m very happy with my purchase and would recommend these pants! I’ll probably end up buying another pair.
