My FAVORITE scrubs! The scrubs are super flattering and comfortable... when you finally get the size right. I had to go through the hassle of ordering a couple pairs and returning the sizes that didn’t work for me. However, I found that and XS top and S Petite bottoms fit best. For reference I am 5’3, 112ish pounds. I also bought the burgundy pair and the fit is much looser and bigger, even though I bought the same size. Because of that, I give the scrubs 4 stars instead of 5. The cuffs at the bottom of the joggers aren’t as snug as I wish they could be though (like other reviews have mentioned) These scrubs are super functional and the material is comfortable during long shifts. Overall I definitely recommend them!

Bad fit for my body type. Good quality though It can be hard to find pants for myself, especially skinny jeans, because I have large hips but skinny calves and ankles. My height is 5’ 4” and weight is 175, I have a pear shaped body and usually a size 14 in jeans so I ordered the 14. ▪️These leather pants on me started bunching up halfway down the thigh and got worse lower down my leg. Snug fit in the upper thighs without being tight though. ▪️They have a pretty wide leg opening and I like my skinny pants to be very snug at the ankle. ▪️ The rise of the pants come at least 2 inches below my belly button and I prefer pants to hit at my belly button. I also tend to carry my weight in my midsection so the pants are a little bit snug at the waist band. ▪️ I will be returning these pants, but again my body is very hard to fit so if you tend to be less curvy and are more thin than I am these pants will probably still look good on you. They are great quality though.

Runs way too large and no communication from company As embarrassing the photos are of these scrubs on me I am posting to show how large they run as I wish someone had shown or told me :) I made an order of several pair of scrub bottoms (including these red ones) in different colours and styles trying to find the right fit. The colour is a vibrant red and after washing in hot water and drying the colour remains but the size did not change. I ordered a XXS Petite and the pictures are taken 1, as they arrived in a plastic bag 2, the side view with nothing in my pockets but the back looks like it is full :( 3, the extra length and 4 I tied them as tight as possible and am showing that they still have several inches where they slide up and down. I had no communication with the company despite several attempts on this pair, a green pair that arrived without any tags and no bag and note the white pair is EXTREMELY see through! Since I needed something to wear immediately I'm keeping these and taking the loss. I give an average 3 star review because the colour is good and they will fit many people...plus I hate leaving negative reviews even when the company ignores my questions about refunds, returns or exchanges. For reference I am about 5'1", 102lbs and waist is abt 24"and again I ordered the XXS Petite, washed in hot water and machine dried and they are still embarrassingly large.

The rise is higher in the back. Love that it has real pockets in the back. They look like jeans I already own and love, I don’t think anyone will notice they’re maternity jeans.

Perfect!! Will buy more! These are amazing!!!! I will probably buy more colors!! I’m 5’7, 126-130lb normally and now 14 weeks along and I bought XS. I Just hit the stage where all my jeans are uncomfortable to wear around the waist, especially in the evening. I honestly didn’t have high expectations for these but they’re almost perfect. They fit snug around my legs but the material is stretchy and lightweight so comfortable. I’m skinny with thin legs so I don’t think I’ll gain a lot of weight in my legs. The waist band does start very low in the front so I will definitely have to wear my longer shirts with it but since the color of jean is dark I won’t really worry about the band showing. The rise is higher in the back. Love that it has real pockets in the back. They look like jeans I already own and love, I don’t think anyone will notice they’re maternity jeans.

Longer than expected, feels cheap. Going by the size chart, I ordered the 4X since I wear a US size 16. The waist fit fine; however, I'm 5'9 with long legs and so I ordered the "tall" ones based on it saying that 5'8 and up should get the tall and it's waaaay too long. The bottom is all scrunchy. They definitely don't fit like I thought they would. These don't feel sexy, very frumpy. They're comfortable but but I imagine they would be hot in any other weather but winter weather. They are definitely too expensive for what they are, the material is very cheap feeling, especially the buttons. I'm sure that this would rip easily. Maybe my legs are just too thin? Either way this is a pass. They fit but they just don't look right, not like everybody else is pictures do anyway. Will be returning.

Long, long, long,but ok I'm 5'5", 140ish lbs, and the small petite fit as expected: fitted, but not skintight... but they were SOOOOO LONG. They still are just barely off the ground with a petite. The material on these is a thick-threaded, stiff fabric, it almost reminds me of the fabric tents are made of, or work coveralls (but thinner), or low thread count sheets... For the fabric being so stiff, i was super surprised with how comfortable these scrubs actually were. Not like your fave pair of pjs or anything, but not bad at all. I ordered both the pants and top on Amazon, and they match perfectly in style, material, and color. (I've included both tags in picture so you can compare item #s). For a $30 set of scrubs, these aren't bad... but they're not as amazing as some of the reviews state. I guess i was expecting more.

Size inconsistent, dry clean only Here are several "mediums", and different angles of an average medium. My suggestion is to order several of your color(s) in the same size, and return all but your best fit due to inconsistent sizing. Lucky for me, the Royal Blue fits like more of a tight ankle legging, so I will keep and have the others altered. Must wear a thong, and make sure it is flat or the texture will show bunching under the pants (intentionally shown in side angle picture). Does not show cellulite for me, which is a plus. All but the Royal Blue had crotch horizontal lines (shown in front closeup), but could be due to how wide that area is in me. These can't be altered away, due to needing a less shallow crotch seam and you can't add the extra fabric to fix it. Pretty stretchy, but you couldn't do lunges in them. Last picture is just to show general bunching, but it actually doesn't look that bad, it's just a weird angle. I'm 5'3", ~124 pounds, wide hips. I haven't washed/worn these yet, but based on other pants with these fabric %age and types, I know from experience to dry clean only; it helps prevent little elastic fibers from breaking down in the crotch/rear.

Extremely comfortable and flattering. I originally ordered the 12 tall and they were a tad tight so I exchanged for 12-14 tall, I am 6 foot and wear a 12 or 14 in jeans depending on who makes the jeans. My waist is 29 but my hips are 45 (large bum and thighs) I'm sure if I had a smaller bum I would have fit the 10 or 12. I spent 6 hours in them on my birthday and was not only comfortable but I felt amazing. I didnt even want to take them off they were so nice, almost yoga pant level of stretch I will buy the other colors when they are available for my size. Please make the red and teal available, ASAP.

Warm, Boyfriend Style, comfortable pants, slower shipping When I ordered these sweatpants the word "jogger" was used in the title and description so I'd like to be clear the pants I received are not true jogger style bottoms if that is what you need. [If you have worn Victoria's Secret or PINK sweats these remind me of the original Boyfriend fit that had the elastic ankle bands vs being straight open] However these are lovely, stark white, warm sweatpants that will hopefully last at least a season! I put them in the dryer on high heat for 40 minutes before trying them on (as a policy) and didn't notice any shrinking or "pilling" inside. I will update after a few washes. These do have an elastic waist band that stretches several inches as do the ankle bands.. i am kind of short and on the petite size so they were almost too large for me but I like the baggy look when wearing light coloured sweats. Please excuse the photos but I needed to get the review in and was solo trying to take a full length picture. I wanted to take a star away for shipping time since I ordered with Prime and it took nearly two weeks but I'm trying to leave my petty side out of it ;) For reference I am almost 5'3" /105 lbs/ 24.5"waist and bought the Small (there was no extra small)

great flattering scrub pants I am obsessed with these scrubs. I am 5'4" with a 30" waist and 39" hips. I am usually a medium in pants and generally prefer a looser fit, but with the waist not being adjustible I decided to order both a Small regular and Medium petite. The mediums were just too loose in the waist and the moment I bent down the waistline fell with it. The length on the petites look better to me with my height, but I have a very athletic build and the cuff was too tight over my muscular calves. On the other hand, the size smalls fit PERFECTLY in the waist. I was worried they'd be too tight in the hips/rear but the fabric has a surprising amount of stretch and I've been very comfortable with them at work (I work in a veterinary clinic so I spend a lot of time squatting and wrangling animals). They did shrink ever so slightly with my covid-compliant hot wash/hot dry cycles (the photos of the small size pants were taken post-wash) but again, still an amazing fit, highly recommend

Advice for Ladies that are ~5’4” and weigh around 65 kg The product is exactly what you expect. It’s slightly tighter around the waist, it goes above the navel, slightly long and baggy at the bottom. I am 5’4” and weigh 65kg. If you have a small butt then your butt will look pretty flat, it’s not ugly flat tho. If you are a female around my height I would recommend a small short. A medium for a hip size above 35inches would be too large. As for material, the best I can compare it to would be a shower curtain. It’s a pretty thin material.So if you wear a women’s size 7 or 8 in regular jeans I highly recommend these because they are pretty cheap compared to the others.

Pant cuff wasn’t a jogger fit 👎🏼 I’m 5’2” and weigh 149 lbs, ordered a Medium Petite. The pant fit wasn’t too tight or too lose, length was fine. It’s as comfortable as any regular scrub pant. The material is much thicker kinda like workout pants. I agree with other reviews that said that the zipper digs in you’re skin if you zip all the way up. Makes sense for fashion but not convenient or comfortable in that aspect. I am returning them because unlike in the display pictures, the pant cuff wasn’t snug around my ankle. Seemed like they were stretched out or defective from the company. If you look at the picture I uploaded, the cuff if almost the same width as the rest of the pant leg so what’s the point of ordering a jogger pant if it doesn’t look like one. Cherokee is a good brand but not sure if I’d order this type of pant from them again.

Really cute VERY comfortable and soft, must buy! I am 7 months pregnant and am recently feeling huge and uncomfortable in everything I own. I ordered these pants fully intending not to wear them til after I have this baby and to my surprise they actually fit really nicely and I could see them still fitting great different but still great after I am no longer pregnant. They are SUPER soft and feel exactly like LLR leggings but have the nice flare at the bottom and feel like better quality than some thin LLR leggings I have had. They have a nice wide waist band which for us with mom bods is important and I suspect they will be flattering on my less round body. They do look pretty good even on my bloated pregnant body tho. The pattern I got definitely draws the eyes vertically making me look taller rather than wider. Only thing I could see being an issue for some people is I am a tall lady 5’8”-5’9” and they are long which I personally love but it is something to note if you’re significantly shorter than my height you may need to cut or hem them. Otherwise a great purchase. Full disclosure I just received them and only tried them on so if they don’t hold up after a few wears I will update. Update: I wore these all the way through the end of my pregnancy and my baby is now three months old I still wear them all the time, I bought 2 other colors as well. I basically live in these pants. They are as I thought they’d be, flattering while still being comfortable. They have held up super well, still soft and not faded or ripping, after heavy use and washes on many sanitize cycle washes.

Falls down in back Lined pants so they don’t stick too badly. I’ve ordered and tried on all three inseams at size 14. I’m regularly a 16, 5’7”. The short pant was about upper ankle height. Long was past my toes and the middle size, 29” inseam, was still a little long...almost to the floor. Problems come at the top. Waaaay high waist (above belly button) in front (cool triple buttons get covered up unless you wear a half shirt) and dips down in back. Enough to have me needing to hike them up relentlessly and that is just intolerable for me. Bunches up all along legs too so it doesn’t end up looking as smooth as it does in the pictures. I badly want to keep them because they look bad ass but they’ll be going back.

Could be worse, but could be A LOT better! Just starting nursing I'm finding it so hard to find nice but affordable cute scrubs. Not only that but looking for ones that fit me right is even more of a struggle. These pants however are not the greatest I do like the fit on me though. The fabric is not stretchy it's stiff and not soft and the elastic waist comes up past my belly button. There is also a lot of extra fabric in the hip area that's not needed for me. With that said a small fits me relatively nicely I like my scrubs to fit me more on the snugger side with a little bit of looseness but definitely not baggy everywhere. I don't have to hem the bottom the length hits a little below my ankle hovering above the ground. My measurements are hip: 40, waist: 30, wt: 152 ht: 5'4. I hope this helps some else see if these work for them. UPDATE: I wore them to work once and they were the most uncomfortable scrubs pants I own. I tried to make it work but they were not staying up, kept falling down at the waist. They were super tight in my thighs it felt like it was almost gonna rip when I sat down. I ended up returning them 😑 disappointed....

Petite fits like it's made for a giant I'm 5 ft 3 in tall, and ordered the petite size. Even that is way too tall. The pant legs drag on the floor. The large also sits very low on me, and pulls down anytime I keep something in the pockets. I hate dealing with returns so I'm keeping them anyways lol. They are comfortable, just super long, and I will hem them.

Great price and quality... but petite is still too long Pants fit great... except petite is more like a regular. Still had to hem the pants because they where too long. But for the price, the fit and the quality you cant go wrong with these pants. Will definitely be buying more since buying it directly from school cost about $10 more. In the picture included you can see my pants where hem and also folded at the waist. (Im 5 feet tall)

Good Leggings - Will buy again I'm sick of those soft or so called velvety stretchy spandex nylon-like leggings! I love the cotton ones. I feared buying from online. You got all these size differences with this one being a size down (or 2 or 3 or 4) and that one being 1 to 2 to 4 sizes up. I ordered ONE pair as experiment. Perfect. I compared them to my No Boundaries cotton leggings and there was barely a difference. I buy the 15-16 large in theirs and bought extra large in these. I normally wear size 9 or 10 jeans. My size leggings in No Boundaries brand should be 11-13. But I like mine loose - less clingy.l I'm 5 ft 4 and 133 pounds if that helps. Don't hesitate. They are good leggings.

Love these scrubs Unfortunately, the color of the pictures I uploaded are not accurate. The black is a more true black and the green more of a hunter green (less blue). The colors pictured in the ad are accurate. I bought the green pants to match a shift based on the color in the picture and they match perfectly. People cannot believe I bought them separately. I love the fit and material of these scrub pants. I am 5'4" and weigh 130lbs. The size small fits great and is the perfect length. They are soft and flexible to wear for a long shift and have a good number of pockets for holding all those nursing supplies. I have tried a lot of different scrub brands and Grays Anatomy are some of my favorite for comfort compared to price.

Warm and Comfortable is an understatement! BUY THEM! Holy Moly, I feel like I hit the jackpot with these boots. We are heading to Heinz Field in Pittsburgh in the middle of December. I'm so happy with this purchase, buy them...you will not regret it!!!

Perfect for skinny/ curve-less girls! FINALLY!! Scrub pants that fit me so PERFECTLY! For reference, I’m 100 lbs, 5’2”, short torso/ high waist - I wear XXS regular length. I have a very thin frame with NO curves/ thighs (sigh), so it’s tough finding scrubs that don’t look insanely baggy on me. I’ve even tried pants that have a little stretch in them and are even categorized as “slim fit,” but those only enhanced my LACK of curves (doh!). I know I might be speaking to a small percentage of the population, but I hope this review helps my fellow skinny-minnie, not-so-blessed-in-that-department gals. These pants are form-fitting and truly slim fit. My colleague pointed out to me that my pants look very nicely fitted on me. Yet it still has enough room to move around in, and it doesn’t hug my behind so tight to where it accentuates my flat behind. It’s so very comfortable around the waist, and the pockets were nicely designed as they are subtle, and they don’t make your pants look bulky or like there’s just too much going on (I prefer simple designs, but with functionality). The slits sit perfectly on top of my shoes. I don’t have a side view, but I do have this view from the top. These are truly the only scrub pants I will wear!

Great scrubs, not much variety. I’m a new CNA in a LTC facility. I needed scrubs that would hold up to everything I was going to be putting them through. These fit the bill. I am 240lbs and 5’7”, I ordered a Large tall cause I wanted extra length for when they shrink in the dryer. My only set back is I wish there was more options and colors in tall. I tired to order the standard large and they hit me at my ankles. I gave them away.

Small reg vs small petite (both fit great) Very good product. I like the material and the waist band elastic is comfortable, and doesn't extend fully in the front so it doesn't get bunched up when pulling the ties. Based on reviews, I ended up getting the small and small petite to try. I am 5'3.75" and 130lbs, usually wear a size 5 juniors or 4/6 depending on the pants as I have a booty. I was concerned as based on the measurements I didn't think my booty would fit in the small (39" hips) but they did, easily! Plenty of room in both sizes. I had room behind the thighs as well, and only had to pull the drawstrings slightly, which is surprising for me with pants in general. I laid out the small reg and small petite and tried on both. The only discernable difference I found is in the length of the pant leg. Small reg measured at 31" inseam, and it was 2" longer than the petite. For me both fit well, and just depended on what "look" I would want- fully over the top of the tennis shoe with wrinkles (reg) or just laying on top (petite). For reference the one pictured is the petite and the shoes are standard asics sneakers (about 1" heel).

High Quality, ALL USE Pants This yoga/scrub pants by KECOLS are high quality, fit very well and are true to size. The fabric is soft to the touch, and thicker than normal yoga pants and is a 4-way-stretch which gives a lot of freedom of movements. They have plenty of pockets and even with a dedicated phone pocket there is no pulling down typical with thinner fabrics in cargo pants, where with the weight of the pocket is very uncomfortable to walk because of bouncing and weight. Not in this case. However for me the bad part was that the pants are 1 inch too long in the inseam as seen in the photos. I am 5'3" and 130 lb for reference.I eventually had to alter the leg by eliminating the slit on the sides which I really liked, hem and run an elastic in the ankles as seen in photo. The result was a customized look which I love anyway but definitely changed the style of the pants. Washability of this garment was easy and practical . I washed them with same color in hot water and dry them at high heat. No damage, no wrinkles, no color fading all around a very well made pant that I hope will last me for a while.

Really excellent but size up and expect distressed These are GORGEOUS! I was blown away by how good these are. I purchased a similar pair from Frye and I honestly like these wayyyyy better! I love the shaft height, hits me right below the knee, I'm 5'7" for reference. I love the toe shape, the extra space at the calf, I do not consider myself to have wide calves for reference. I wear a women's 8-8.5 and since they are only offered in whole sizes, I went with an 8 and they are almost too small. I will definitely have to use some shoe stretch spray on them, so if you are between sizes, go up if you want to wear socks!! Now, my only major issue, not sure if I got a funky pair, but there is some weird distressing that I was not expecting and does not seem intentional, see pics. I do plan to take a suede brush to them to see if I can get those out before waterproof spraying them. I was a bit disappointed in that, especially for the price point. Otherwise, the shape, the shaft height and fit around the calf, color, its all so super good and I will wear these ALL SEASON! I love them!

oversized and soft This review is for the GRAPENT Wide leg palazzo pants, xx-large, dark blue. I usually wear an XXL in pull on pants, 17/18 in jeans, and XL (XXL is usually a little too big) in leggings. Approximate measurements are 41” waist and 51” hips, 5’6” and a 30 inch inseam reaches just to the floor on me. These pants are a delight to wear. They are unbelievably soft, thin, stretchy, and flowy. The style is meant to be oversized so definitely don’t size up even if you want a loose fit. The size chart was based on body measurement and not the dimensions of the pants. It says that XX-Large, US size 20 - US 22, waist 36.5 - 38”, hips 47 - 48.5”. My waist and hips are bigger than that and the pants still are a flowy fit on me. The waist has a few inches of smocking and is very stretchy. The fabric has a good horizontal stretch to it. These pants drag on the ground for me. I wouldn’t expect this with a 29” inseam but I measured and they are indeed 29 (maybe 29.25) inches. However even with the waist an inch or two above my belly button (which is perfect for me) the crotch sits a little low. It looks and feels good but that’s why the leg length is too long for me. They look like the could be a straight wide leg but are actually about 16” at the thigh and 20” at the hem. The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.

Wear These Anywhere It's nice to have something to wear out that isn't work clothes. These G4Free Wide Leg Pants are just the ticket for me. Just under 5'5" and thick waisted, I was concerned whether or not I could find the right fit. I got the Lg and the measurements are spot on. The fit feels great and the length works perfectly. The stitching is pretty good, but these will probably be hand washed or in a wash bag to keep them nice. I don't really like the zippers. They zip up/down just fine, but they don't hinge well. In other words, you need to push them down so they don't stick up. Kind of weird, but they work. The pockets are roomy and lay nicely. You can't really see them unless you have your hand in one. Quite comfy. They have a sewn pleat down the front that doesn't stand out, just makes the pants look nicer. That also gives you a slimmer look, imo. Nice smooth feel. The waist size was smaller than I wanted, but actually fits great and they feel like they were made for me. I like them alot.

Wear These Anywhere It's nice to have something to wear out that isn't work clothes. These G4Free Wide Leg Pants are just the ticket for me. Just under 5'5" and thick waisted, I was concerned whether or not I could find the right fit. I got the Lg and the measurements are spot on. The fit feels great and the length works perfectly. The stitching is pretty good, but these will probably be hand washed or in a wash bag to keep them nice. I don't really like the zippers. They zip up/down just fine, but they don't hinge well. In other words, you need to push them down so they don't stick up. Kind of weird, but they work. The pockets are roomy and lay nicely. You can't really see them unless you have your hand in one. The waist size was smaller than I wanted, but actually fits great and they feel like they were made for me. I like them alot.

Love luv love these! True to size. These boots are great! I am a true 7.5 but got an 8. The 7.5 probably would’ve fit perfectly. I wore regular socks and wore them for 4 hours and danced on concrete for 2.5 hours. When I wear them again I will get an insert with an arch. That’s the only part of my foot that was strained. They have a zipper which was really nice and easy to get my foot in. I’m 5’3” 117lbs. My calves are average for my size. The tops were a little loose but still looked great. No slouching. Nice chunky heel with good height that did not hurt my feet while dancing. I walked on tile, concrete, rocks, grass and felt they were sturdy for all of them. I did not notice an overly strong smell but I really would not have cared. Shiny white and the scuffs I got from the black rubber bottoms came off fairly easily with a Mr. Eraser sponge. I highly recommend these. I will definitely wear these for more than just costumes. I took off 1 star for the lack of any instep and the material around the heel might come off at some point.

Very good product, good quality, elegant, and greate fit. I love them! (I would love for the leggings to have a little zipper in the waist so they could be a little more tight in the abdomen, but they are wonderful nonetheless.) The fabric of these leggings is thick and helps contour the figure. They are not sports leggings, they look a lot more elegant, more like pants, so they are perfect to wear in the office. They are made in the USA! with good finishes, and the high waist is perfect to wear with blouses (which you can put inside them) But ( there is always a but) the red color at least has to be used with light-colored underwear, You can see the dark underwear a little bit through them (as you can see in the pictures). To take care of the leggings I recommend washing them in a Delicates Laundry Bags , with COLD water and avoiding sitting on surfaces such as gravel, this way they will last longer. In the long term ( 1 year if you use them ones a week) and since they wear out on the butt with rubbing (and white elastics appear). I had some that were a very small size for me and for obvious reasons the butt was worn out before. I'm 5' ,121 lb and the small size fits me.

Really nice comfypants, but no front pockets. Why, oh why wouldn’t you put in some front/pockets instead of that fake/pocket seem to give the effect of having a front pocket? I like these pants so much. They have a very deep pair of back pockets. But no front pockets. No one wants to put their phone in their back pocket and then sit down and have it fall out. I get that you might not want to have a pocket line in front, but if you use a thin enough material and stitched it down along the side seam it would be so much better.I really do like them. I love that they come in length I like the back pockets really stretchy really comfortable finally long enough. I have not worn them or washed them. I just tried them on so I can update this after time because I know some people said that after washing and drying they lost some length. Maybe I will just wash them and line drive them.Again, put in front pockets!I am 5’11” 225 lbs and I got XXL Long. Fit is perfect.

Nice quality - Worth a try! I bought a 2-Short anticipating a fit, but they were too big. They're marked 2S, Waist 26 and Length 30. The rise is ~9.75" and fit just under my waist - which is a little higher than my idea of a mid-rise. On the plus side, the fabric seems to be of good quality - strong denim and good weight. Wish they came in a size 0, I'd give them another try. I ended up buying Levi's 315 Shaping Bootcut style in a 25 Short (30 inseam), and they fit perfect - they have a slightly lower rise too at 9.25". If you're looking for a nice fitting pair of jeans that aren't skinny or baggy, then either of these jeans are worth a try.

GREAT pair of pants! GREAT pants & WOW! Super comfy too! & LOTS of stretch! So much so, that I could have gone with the 6 & probably been fine, but this way, I'm good if I put on some extra lbs over the holidays! LOL You can see just HOW much stretch there is in the last pic! For ref. I'm about 5'7.5" tall & apx. 135lbs with a 29-30" waist & 39-40" hips & this is the 8long! LOVE the length so I can wear heels! (note that in the pics, the 1st is me flat footed & barefoot, & the 2nd is my standing on my tippy toes barefoot to see how they'd be with heels) I have trouble with pants not being long enough on Amazon, so this was a pleasant surprise! The material is a super soft feeling jean material & they are VERY comfy on! LOVE them! These pics are them right out of the package, so I can't say if they fade with washing yet, but will come back & let you know if that is an issue! So far though, they seem very well made & reasonably priced, so I'd recommend for sure! I think I will have to get some in blue as well!! I think these are going to be my new FAV jeans!! Hope this helps! Thanks much!

Misleading size chart - way too short I am leaving this review for the taller girls among us. It is already a challenge to find long enough pants on Amazon for those of us who are above 5'7". It is that much more frustrating, when the size chart is misleading. While the quality of these sweatpants is probably good (I haven'twashed them because they are going back, so hard to say for sure), the sizing chart is very misleading on the length. It says these sweatpants are 42" long, but they are only 38.5" long, so be aware.

Don't recommend for C-section Mommas, but super comfy and stretchy These are super soft and stretchy, and great for maternity or postpartum to wear. They have a comfortable but form-fitting style, and open up wide along the bottom, almost to the point of bell bottoms, not yoga pants, which I will occasionally trip over so I am deducting a star. However, I think it's fair to point out that I am a short 5'2" and 225lbs postpartum 16 months, and ordered the petite XL and they're still pretty long on me. The extra fabric for the belly is about 7" wide, but stretches well. I don't recommend these for C-section mommas, as the crotch is sewn low and sits right near the uterus area postpartum. I love the color and while it does have pockets, they're not very deep (my smart phone sticks out a bit from them), and the back pockets are sewn shut and purely aesthetic, which is an annoying trend in women's clothes - especially those made for moms. The fabric is made from quality materials and they shipped quickly in a vacuum-sealed bag.

I bought a medium for my daughter who is about 5'5 and 117 lbs. They fit her great, and she says that they're relaxed and comfortable. The material is soft and they're flattering. They'll be great for her to wear around the house or to school. Great sweatpants!

I’m about 5’1”, and the length is perfect for me. The fabric feels nice and comfortable, and they fit well even with my belly being much larger than usual. The zipper feels well constructed and opens and closes smoothly. I like the overall look and fit, and they’re easy to wear. One thing to note is that the back pockets are decorative only, so you can’t actually put anything in them. Overall, these are good-quality pants and very comfortable, but they are on the higher end price-wise at around $40 per pair.

New Boot Goofin 🤠👢 Hey Yall! 🤠 First let me say these boots are beautiful. However they are not wide calf friendly . I purchased a size 7. I don’t consider myself having wide calf’s, I actually feel like I have chicken legs from the knees down lol. So to struggle to get my legs in these boots was a shocker. But they fit! I would recommend wearing long socks as your legs will stick to the boots if you sweat. But they are overall comfortable and I love the length of these boots . I am 5’2 for reference. I went the extra mile and measured my calf . It’s 15 inches in diameter . According to google that’s considered medium- wide calf lol 😂 these boots are labeled as brown but can be considered as Camel 🐪 brown.

Smooth, Sculpting & Very Flattering (Charcoal Gray, Small – 28”) I ordered the Sunzel Sunzfly Invisible Butt Scrunch leggings in Charcoal Gray, size Small, 28” inseam, and they’re incredibly flattering. The fabric (80% polyamide, 20% elastane) feels ultra-soft and stretchy with light-to-medium compression. It smooths everything out without feeling stiff or restrictive. The material isn’t thin, and I feel confident wearing them for workouts or running errands. The high-waisted waistband provides solid tummy control and stays in place — no rolling down. The no front seam makes a big difference in comfort and gives a clean, smooth look in the front. The hidden scrunch detail in the back enhances shape subtly. It lifts without looking exaggerated. Very sculpting but still tasteful. The 28” inseam hits right at my ankle for a full-length look without bunching. The Charcoal Gray color is a true neutral — easy to pair with black, white, or muted tones. Overall, they feel supportive, smoothing, and flattering from every angle. Great quality for the price. I would love to get different colors.

I am so happy that I am on the verge of crying!!! In my teens to early 30s, I wore nothing but denim. I wore them hard too, b/c of my figure (way smaller than now), I always had issues fitting my hips and waist. I had to give them up after giving birth to my son due to medical issues. That was 16 years ago. I have been watching tons of reels of ladies working out and their new wardrobes, and it inspired me to try a pair of jeans again. I am shocked I found the perfect pair on my first purchase! Holy Cow! I am in love. Of course, they aren't as comfortable as leggings, however, this pair has stretch in all the right places. I am still working on losing weight and went with flare b/c my thighs are WAY bigger than my calves. I am just stunned that I have no back gap. I always had an issue there b/c I am swayed back. Seriously, check out this brand on Amazon. Read the reviews and measurements. FYI, Elastic, high-waisted pants are best for those with a sway & not a gap. I hate the idea of high-waisted pants, but these are so comfy. I wear large in leggings, which is what was suggested for this purchase. My waist is larger than the sizing chart, 40, and my hips are 43. I fit perfectly in the large for 30.5 - 32 waist and 40.5 - 42 hips. Keep this in mind. Color is on point. Value for money is great considering I have issues fitting in most years. The Fabric is of great quality and itchy. The seam isn't tight in the crotch/butt region.

Bootyliciously awesome!! Must have! Love love love these pants!!! I bought 2 pair, blue and red. I’m 5’3”, 154lbs and Large fit very comfortably! I wore the blue ones out dancing and they are soft and made my butt look soooo good! I received so many compliments!!! I was drenched in sweat from dancing all night but the pants didn’t make me overly hot at all. I was worried about under buttcheek sweat leaving a line lol but it was all good. No embarrassing sweat marks through the pants. I got the 31.5” inseam which seemed to be a little long on me and I was wearing 2 1/2” heels. I did wash them before wearing. Cold water wash and tumble dry 30min and low heat 30min with dry clothes already in the dryer to help speed it up. No shrinkage or issues after washing and wearing. Beautiful bright blue and didn’t fade or bleed at all. I haven’t worn the red ones yet so I’m going to be returning them to exchange them for a 30” inseam pair. I think they’ll be the perfect length. These are an absolute MUST HAVE. I’ve sent the link to all my friends. They are reasonably priced and I personally think they’ll are worth it!

Perfect Y2K Style Almost Goth Boots : ) These are exactly what I wanted them to be! The boots have a thick bottom, side zipper, and are tall for a combat style boot. They are very true to size - I usually wear an 8.5 but I get a 9 when I want to wear 2 layers of socks underneath.They fit really well and are very comfortable. For this particular style, they are pretty lightweight as well. The boots look very Y2K but are simple enough that they could be worn a number of ways. The half-side zipper is convenient for getting the boots on quickly. However, the ankles and calves almost fit me and I typically wear child-size clothing. That tells me the calf part of the boots may run small and if one has a bigger frame - these boots may not fit. They're not ultra-skinny but they're quite skinny. I'm 5'7" and 80-85lbs for a size comparison.Overall, I think the boots are made well, look great, fit well, and are comfortable. I have no criticisms personally and would definitely recommend these boots to others. : )

Had low expectations based on ratings, but Quality is outstanding! It’s hard to determine whether sweatpants are going to be the “next favorite pair” when shopping online and having only the description and ratings to judge from. Each pair of pants made usually end up being completely different from the next, so it’s really chance taking when spending this much money on sweats. The chance was worth it for me! - The fabric isn’t too bad, there isn’t a lining inside but they still have some weight feeling to them and are comfortable! - I am 5’7 and the Med length is perfect- as you can see the fabric bunched at the ankle, they could go down over my heels if I wanted - I will also add that the waist elastic is tighter than most mediums fit, (rn Im at 123lbs saying that it fits slightly tighter) so sizing up could be better if you don’t want waist band lines. - the pockets are great with depth but not too much. - the inseam is perfect, there isn’t too much fabric between that feels awkward Will be buying more colors!

*NOT* for the fabric sensitive 5’10” 173lbs (size 8 US) So I was looking for a tall girl friendly, Alo sweat pant dupe (65% cotton and polyester). The description says that this is what it is fabric wise. WELL good news? It’s almost tall girl friendly- I can wear flat shoes and be okay- if you’re over 5’10” I’d look elsewhere. I’d also recommend sizing up. I’m usually a medium, I sized up for a baggier look, and well it fits like a traditional medium on me. Where my issue really is? I can’t tell where the 65% cotton is. I’m super sensitive to fabrics and don’t love polyester but appreciate the flexibility it provides. The lining of these pants is 95% polyester and 5% spandex. It’s giving sateen sheets for pants.

Loose Fit HUGE compared with Original Fit The product description for Carhartt Women's Rugged Flex Loose Fit Canvas Work Pant says, "Loose Fit, previously known as Original Fit, is a label update in name only. Loose and Original size and fit remains the same." Not so! I love my size 6 Original fit (tan) so I bought a size 6 Loose (black; I also measured myself first). See the tan photo: fitted but not tight or loose. Compare the black photo: yes, that is my entire arm stuffed down the leg hole (along with my leg) up to my elbow. :/ Love Carhartt quality, so I ordered the size 4 (I am 5'7", fluctuate 130-135#, usually wear an 8 in pants). Edit: Just got the size 4 (4! I usually wear an 8), and it fits me! Upgraded my rating. So, despite the manufacturer claim that the new "Loose Fit" fit "remains the same" as the "Original Fit", know that (for me), a size 4 "Loose" fits like the size 6 (Fall, prior to COVID) "Original."

Really Nice, Versatile Tall Boots I ordered these tall boots and the color brown. The brown is like a chocolate brown with a slight reddish undertone. I’ve attached a photo for reference, but the boots do look much better on then they do in the photo I ordered these boots in a size 9. For reference, I’m between a size 8.5 and a 9, but I often size up to a 9 in boots because I like to wear socks. So the size 9 fit me perfectly with socks. If you don’t plan on wearing socks, order your true size. The shaft of the boot is fairly wide, so if you have a wider calf, they should fit pretty well. Note, however, that the shaft of the boot is rather tall and it hits the very bottom of my kneecap. I'm 5'4" tall, so if you are much shorter, it may go over your knee. The heel is substantial and easy to walk on. It’s a little higher than I expected, but it will look nice with jeans and dresses. Overall, I’m really pleased with the quality of these boots. The fold leather is soft and realistic looking.

Exceeded expectations - but size up Based on other reviews, I sized up and they fit perfectly. For reference, I’m 5’4” 132 lbs. Normally I wear a size 6; in the dark wash I ordered 8 regular and in medium wash 8 short since regular length was unavailable. I love them both, just enough stretch to make them comfortable without being tight. No noticeable shrinkage after washing. The dark pair keeps their shape while wearing, the lighter pair stretched out a little by the end of the day.

Great fit / stretch I am 4’11, approx 190 pounds. I am 7 weeks postpartum and about to go back to being an ER RN. I bought a MP. I bought a few different pairs of joggers because I’m picky and wanted to find some that fit well on my ankles. These fall just below, which I don’t love, but they’re comfortable still. They have great stretch, don’t come down with squats, lifting legs, or sitting. The pockets are fantastic. I just wish there was one more on the left leg. I ordered navy blue. I don’t see any issue with the thickness/not see through or have any problems with underwear lines. They seem durable enough like they’ll hold up well.

Love this!!! COMFORTABLE I’m so happy with purchase. Needed something blue and quick. Got a size large. 5’8 1/2 and about 185lbs. Pants came all the way down as it should. The material is so nice and not cheap. I felt great and COMFY. Easy to put on. Not wrinkly. Great stretch.

Omg this deserves 27 stars. Get it. It's the softest, most luxurious feeling fabric. I'm 5'5, 155ish lbs, and usually wear a large. The large was a bit oversized. So I tried a medium and it was perfect. I could have made due with the large but will gift that to my mom. It's comfortable and not too thick or thin. Perfect for running around in while still looking put together. I like the jogger legs because I don't have to worry about it being too long. The red is bright and just as pictured. Already have another color in my cart.

Super awesome pants Totally in love with these pants! They are so comfortable, a little stretchy and light weight without being see-through. I am 5'3" about 145 lbs and a 38" hip. I normally wear a medium and got the medium (based on the size chart) in Khaki. Obviously a little wrinkly from opening but it washes and dries well (doesn't shrink). I love the color and the waistband is comfortable too. It is a little long since I'm a shorty, but I can still wear them with flat and not drag on the ground too much. There are quite a few colors and the price is a little high, but I am definitely getting more of these pants in different colors. Highly recommend!

Soft, and comfy, and very loose in the legs Hello! I'm 5'5, 177lbs today (changes daily bc of perimenopause, lol) and apple shaped. I usually wear 22/24 (2x to 3x. The suggested size was 3x. I bought a 4x so I would have more room in the legs to breathe. As you can tell by my pics, the pants are very loose and don't look like they do in the picture advertised. Had they been more honest, I would have sized down 1 or even 2 sizes. They are also longer than I was hoping. They were out of white petite, so I had to order a regular, so in all fairness, this was my doing for wanting a decent pair of white pants now. Let me say that the material is super soft and light weight & super comfy. I am going to order another pair, and this time in the correct size. I definitely recommend!

Comfy Im 120 and 5'5, got the XS pants and they fit perfect, i love them! just beware: if you enjoy your joggers to be tight and really stick to your ankles, these arent like that, they are a bit wide, i find it perfect since tight joggers tend to make my ankles itch but i know some people do like them more tight. Peso 53kg y mido 166cm, compre el pantalon XS y me quedo perfecto, es muy lindo y comodo, no mas tengan en cuenta que si les gustan los joggers que son bien apretados en el tobillo, estos en realidad quedan algo holgados, a mi me parece perfecto pero se que hay gente que les puede llegar a molestar eso

5'8, 375 lbs, Apple Shaped- Happy with my purchase, will be buying again! ☺️ My issue with jeans is that they will usually fit my legs but then can't button them. I don't have that problem with these jeans. These jeans have a good ratio of denim to stretch where it stretches enough in the waist if you need that extra inch but also still feels like denim and not jeggings. They are not the thickest material but also not thin. I like the look, I don't think they look cheap for the price. I took these pictures right after taking them out of the package. I am planning on wearing them to an event next week. I will update how they held up through sitting and standing and through a wash. So far I can say I really like them and am happy with my purchase. I hope these pictures can help give you a reference if you are someone shaped like me. UPDATE: The jeans held up great! Did not stretch out, was comfortable to sit, stand, move around for multiple hours. Updated to add pictures wearing the jeans at the event.
